title: fln-env: canonicalize full-hash collision buckets for schedule-independent iteration
description:
Problem: PMap iteration is insertion-order independent only for non-colliding hashes. Distinct keys with the same complete 64-bit key_hash are appended to a leaf bucket, so bucket iteration, Debug output, and any future environment enumeration depend on insertion/scheduling order. This violates FL-INV-01 under adversarial Name collisions even though LogicalRootBuilder currently masks the defect by sorting again. Scope: give PKey a deterministic total tie-break consistent with Eq and maintain every full-hash collision bucket in canonical key order during insert/overwrite/remove; do not sort the entire map at iteration time or rely on randomized hashing. For Name, use the pinned Name.cmp order after its stack-safety prerequisite is complete. Document the CGSE policy and complexity for large collision buckets. Verification requirements: permutation/property tests use many distinct constant-hash keys and prove identical iteration/Debug/equality after insert, overwrite, remove, fork, and reinsert; mixed near-collision tries exercise every hash chunk and the final partial chunk; a schedule matrix constructs the same environment in several thread partitions and compares enumerations and roots; deep valid names remain bounded once the Name-order prerequisite lands. Extend the shared environment E2E lane with versioned detailed logs containing collision cardinality, insertion schedule, canonical key order, expected/actual enumeration and roots, operation counts, resources, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and parent gates. No iteration-time global sort, address-based tie-break, randomized seed, or collision rejection.

## Steps to Reproduce

Insert the same set of distinct constant-hash keys in forward and reverse order, then compare `PMap::iter` and Debug output. The current collision leaf retains insertion order, so the two enumerations differ.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. PKey exposes a deterministic total tie-break consistent with Eq, and every complete-primary-hash collision bucket is maintained in canonical key order across distinct insert, overwrite, remove, fork, reinsert, collapse, equality, iteration, and Debug behavior. No global iteration-time sort, randomized seed, address tie-break, or valid collision rejection is permitted. 2. Named unit/property/model tests compare many distinct constant-hash keys against an independent ordered model across forward, reverse, randomized, and all removal/reinsertion permutations; mixed near-collision tries exercise every hash chunk including the final partial chunk; exact-key overwrites preserve cardinality and canonical order. Named append-order, wrong-tie-break, missed-overwrite, and removal-order mutants are each killed. 3. Environment construction at thread partitions 1, 8, and 32 and repeated identical runs produces byte-identical canonical enumeration, Debug/equality observables, logical roots, operation counters, and semantic records. Deep valid Name keys use the pinned Name order without host-recursive comparison. Sublinear large-bucket storage and resource accounting remain additively owned by fln-amv.13 and are not weakened by this closure. 4. The shared real-path no-mock environment E2E includes positive collision construction, a planted append-order mutant with the exact expected semantic failure, byte-exact restoration, and clean recovery. It emits strict schema-versioned line-oriented records containing bead/invariant/claim IDs, collision cardinality, schedule and thread, canonical expected/actual order, expected/actual roots, operation/resource facts, input and final hashes, exit/signal, separate stdout/stderr artifacts, cleanup, and final state. Its validator rejects missing/extra fields, stale inputs, wrong or unrelated failures, merged streams, surviving mutants, and incomplete bundles. 5. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, warnings-denied Clippy, UBS on changed files, evidence-validator self-tests, the focused positive-mutation-recovery E2E, scripts/check.sh, and currently runnable parent gates are green with independently valid retained bundles. Final outer environment-schema migration remains explicitly owned by fln-amv.14.
close_reason: Canonical full-hash collision buckets and strict bounded-model E2E/validator/CI proof are complete with green exact parent gates; legacy outer-parent evidence debt remains tracked in fln-amv.14.

title: fln-env: integrate atomic three-way environment merge across all semantic state
description:
Final child integration for fln-env: component APIs can merge individual structures, but speculative branches need one atomic three-way merge across the combined committed state. After every dependency lands, implement a typed merge of base, ours, and theirs containing Environment declarations/extensions, attribute state, module graph, canonical provenance manifest, actual payload-bearing contribution state, and both provenance index directions. A plain Environment-only merge is insufficient.

The lifecycle child of fln-amv.9.3 produces the exact ProvenanceMergePlan. Global merge must consume and immediately revalidate that plan against base/ours/theirs logical roots, provenance roots, schema/epoch, actual declaration and extension values, ranges, contributor identities, and resource envelope before any publication. It must not independently reconstruct a second provenance truth. A stale, mismatched, incomplete, or value-collision plan is a typed atomic refusal. The final commit publishes Environment plus graph/provenance/index state together, or publishes nothing.

Constants added on disjoint names combine; identical same-name additions are idempotent only after actual ConstantInfo equality, not digest equality; different same-name declarations and equal-digest unequal-value cases conflict with both origins and canonical identities. Extension registration/journals follow declared merge and checkpoint laws, including new-on-one-side, identical-new-on-both, AppendOrdered precedence, SetUnion semantic-view/raw-journal law, descriptor/ancestry/range mismatch, opaque provenance, and review-required conflicts. Attribute state follows its pinned contract. Import topology, actual module contributions, completeness grades, contributor indexes, and invalidation roots follow the revalidated provenance plan. Missing or partial input remains explicitly graded and never becomes verified completeness or rejection.

Aggregate every independent conflict in frozen category then canonical Name/module/range order rather than stopping at map traversal order. Define deterministic ours/theirs precedence only where AppendOrdered makes it observable. Before allocating conflict/output collections, enforce independent limits for declarations, extension rows/bytes, attribute rows, modules/import rows, provenance/index rows, names/depth, conflicts/witness bytes, total output bytes, and merge work. Cancellation is observed at fixed deterministic boundaries. Exhaustion or cancellation returns Inconclusive with usage facts and no authoritative partial environment; InternalFault covers inconsistent validated-state/index/plan invariants. Neither outcome is cached as accept/reject.

Root behavior is explicit and checked twice, before and after publication construction. Metadata-only module/provenance changes do not affect logical_root. Merged declarations, extension deltas, attributes when contractually logical, and options affect logical_root exactly through their canonical builders. Provenance root covers topology/ownership/evidence/completeness and remains separate. Operational metadata affects neither. The result reports base/ours/theirs/final logical and provenance roots plus transition reasons, and preserves structural sharing for every unchanged trie, journal chunk, module record, contribution array, index branch, and payload Arc.

Verification uses independent Vec/BTreeMap models for every component and the combined commit. Random fork/edit/merge tests and exhaustive tables cover no-op, one-sided, disjoint, identical, conflicting, opaque/partial/missing, all extension policies, attributes, topology, actual contributions, equal-digest collisions, stale plans/roots/bases, multi-conflict aggregation, every exact resource boundary, cancellation point, Inconclusive recovery, and InternalFault nonpublication. Branch permutations, insertion orders, and thread partitions yield byte-identical authoritative products, diagnostics, roots, and provenance plans. Failure-atomicity and allocation/pointer instrumentation prove unchanged state remains shared. Named mutants drop a declaration, extension, attribute, module, forward/reverse provenance row, plan revalidation, conflict category, completeness grade, root transition, or sharing edge.

Extend the shared no-mock environment E2E lane with a real multi-module branch/merge/failure/recovery scenario and detailed schema-versioned line logs containing snapshot/plan ids, base/ours/theirs roots, actual branch edits and origins, merge policy, ordered conflicts, contribution/index/range facts, completeness and authority grade, logical/provenance transition reasons, sharing/allocation/resource facts, publication/cacheability flags, expected/actual result, recovery action, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env and fln-hash/domain tests, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, every component scenario, and parent gates.

No last-writer-wins declaration overwrite, digest-only equality, dropped extension/attribute/module/provenance state, stale merge plan, independently rebuilt provenance truth, partial success, authoritative inconclusive result, hidden logical-root change, or traversal-order diagnostic drift.

## Acceptance Criteria

The global three-way merge must consume and revalidate the provenance lifecycle plan and atomically publish the entire combined committed state across declarations, extensions, attributes, module graph, actual payload contributions, completeness, and both index directions. Every success/conflict/stale/collision/resource/cancellation/internal-fault/root-transition/sharing case, independent combined model, permutation/thread matrix, named mutant, detailed real E2E positive-negative-recovery log, fln-env/fln-hash/domain test, fmt, clippy, UBS, component gate, and parent gate above is mandatory. Environment-only merge, partial publication, or deferred semantic state fails closure.
design: Dependency topology: global merge consumes the concrete module integration output of fln-amv.9.4 plus attribute state and every other component contract. It does not consume an implementation artifact from the fln-amv.9 aggregation epic. The fln-amv parent separately joins both fln-amv.9 epic closure and this global merge, so the complete module acceptance matrix remains mandatory while module-epic bookkeeping and merge implementation can close in parallel. No component proof is weakened or bypassed by this topology.
acceptance_criteria: The global merge consumes and revalidates the exact provenance plan, then atomically publishes the complete Environment plus attribute/module/provenance/payload/index state with correct separate roots and structural sharing. Named unit suites and independent Vec/BTreeMap model/property tests cover no-op, one-sided, disjoint, identical, every conflict/collision/extension/attribute/topology/completeness case, each exact resource boundary, cancellation/InternalFault nonpublication, root transitions, and allocation/pointer sharing; exhaustive permutation/thread matrices and every named mutant in the description are mandatory. The shared real multi-module no-mock E2E covers positive merge, aggregated conflict/stale-plan/value-collision refusal, resource/cancellation Inconclusive, byte-exact restoration, and later clean publication. Schema-versioned NDJSON carries run/scenario/step/snapshot/plan ids, all roots and origins, policies/conflicts, contribution/index/range/completeness/authority, sharing/resources/cacheability, expected/actual, cleanup, and final state. Separate bounded stdout/stderr artifacts, exact input/output hashes, commands, process results, mutation identity, and first divergence are inventoried in a committed manifest; strict validation rejects missing/extra/stale/swapped/merged-stream/wrong-mutant/surviving-mutant evidence and the retained bundle validates independently. All fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, component gates, and parent gates must be green. No Environment-only merge, digest-only equality, partial publication, traversal-order drift, or deferred semantic state satisfies closure.

title: fln-env: replace implicit Rust discriminants in declaration identity
description:
Problem: Environment::decl_content_digest encodes DefinitionSafety and QuotKind with `as u8`, binding durable declaration/CAS identity to implicit Rust source-order discriminants. Reordering an enum, inserting a variant, or changing representation can silently rewrite logical roots without an epoch/schema decision, and new variants do not force the identity code to choose a stable tag deliberately. Scope: replace both casts with exhaustive explicit canonical tag matches, document the stable mapping as part of Domain::DeclContent, and keep every other field and byte order unchanged. Any future variant must fail compilation here until assigned an intentional schema value. Verification requirements: table-driven unit tests cover all DefinitionSafety and QuotKind variants, prove pairwise distinct content digests and stable repeated roots, and include fixed canonical tag expectations independent of enum declaration order; a deliberate cast-based encoder must be detected by the test or policy check. Existing declaration-field, logical-root, insertion-order, and thread determinism tests remain green. Extend the shared environment E2E lane with detailed versioned records containing variant, schema tag, expected/actual digest and root, epoch, and final result. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and parent gates. No repr-based workaround, debug-string encoding, or silent renumbering.

## Steps to Reproduce

Observe that declaration identity writes `DefinitionSafety` and `QuotKind` with `as u8`. Reordering either Rust enum changes durable digests despite no explicit identity-schema edit; the fixed-tag regression demonstrates the intended stable mapping.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: DefinitionSafety identity tags are exhaustively and explicitly frozen as Unsafe=0, Safe=1, Partial=2; QuotKind tags are Type=0, Ctor=1, Lift=2, Ind=3. Domain::DeclContent writes those canonical tags without enum casts, repr dependence, debug text, or any change to adjacent field order or bytes; adding a new variant fails compilation until assigned a reviewed schema tag. Table-driven unit tests assert the exact seven-row tag table, independently model the complete Definition and Quotient declaration streams, prove pairwise-distinct declaration digests across every variant, stable repeated digests and logical roots, exact LogicalRootBuilder propagation, and unchanged declaration-field and insertion-order laws. A compiler-enforced cast policy plus named source-order, omitted-tag, debug-text, adjacent-field, wrong-domain, and failed-root-propagation models kill the relevant single-defect mutations without pretending that current equal Rust ordinals can behaviorally distinguish a cast. Each of 1, 8, and 32 productive workers independently builds and hashes the complete seven-declaration environment from a distinct full permutation; unit evidence asserts full Environment equality, per-name digest equality, explicit aggregate-root equality, and detailed versioned rows while classifying this honestly as bounded component evidence rather than full PG-5 closure. The tag mapping is a const exhaustive finite match contributing exactly one canonical byte, with no input-sized iteration, helper-owned allocation, or independent cancellation point, so it introduces no new resource dimension or separate limit. The independent stream model proves the surrounding DeclContent layout and byte count are unchanged. Typed input-sized declaration hashing/admission budgets do not exist in the current API and are therefore not falsely claimed here; that preserved functionality is owned explicitly by franken_lean-j8h and remains a W2 parent blocker, while fln-amv.14 owns runner-level resource and artifact facts. The shared real Environment E2E emits schema-versioned records for every variant with epoch/profile, tag, canonical input hash, expected and actual digest/root relation, process result, resource facts, cleanup, and final state; strict validation rejects unknown, duplicate, stale, missing, or reordered cases and proves negative detection plus clean recovery. Separate bounded stream artifacts, exact input/output hashes, and the artifact manifest are retained and validate independently. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, UBS, scripts/check.sh, and every currently runnable environment integration lane pass. No unclassified skip, mock-only proof, silent renumbering, partial matrix, enum reorder/repr workaround, nonexistent resource claim, or deferred listed identity behavior satisfies closure.

title: fln-env: make adversarial full-hash collision handling resource-bounded
description:
Problem: a PMap full-hash collision leaf is a Vec. Looking up/removing a key is linear in collision cardinality, and persistent insertion clones the entire bucket, so constructing N distinct same-hash names is quadratic in comparisons and copied entries. Canonicalizing order in fln-amv.10 fixes determinism but not this denial-of-service/resource-accounting surface. Scope: after the canonical tie-break contract lands, replace large collision buckets with an immutable deterministic secondary structure or tiered representation that gives bounded/sublinear lookup and avoids whole-bucket copying per update while preserving O(1) snapshots, exact Eq semantics, canonical iteration, and small-bucket efficiency. Define promotion/demotion thresholds, secondary key identity, collision-of-secondary-key behavior, allocation/expanded-weight budgets, typed resource exhaustion, and structural-sharing rules; never reject a valid declaration merely because hashes collide. Verification requirements: model/property tests cover promotion, overwrite, remove, collapse, fork, equality, and iteration against BTreeMap for random and all-colliding keys; operation/allocation counters demonstrate non-quadratic 1k/10k collision-family construction and bounded snapshot cost; budget boundaries yield typed inconclusive/resource outcomes where the owning API provides budgets; deterministic permutations and thread partitions stay identical. Extend the shared environment E2E lane with detailed schema-versioned logs containing collision cardinality, representation tier, comparisons, allocations/shared nodes, budget, expected/actual order and roots, resources, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and every currently runnable environment integration check that does not require unfinished sibling slices; final fln-amv.11 and W2 aggregation remains downstream. No randomized secondary hash, collision rejection, global mutable table, or timing-only performance claim.

## Steps to Reproduce

Insert increasing families of distinct keys whose `key_hash` is identical while counting comparisons and copied bucket entries. The Vec collision leaf performs linear lookup and whole-bucket cloning per persistent update, yielding quadratic construction.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Collision buckets use the deterministic tiered persistent representation without changing key, value, equality, or canonical iteration semantics: inline sorted storage contains at most 8 entries, the ninth distinct exact secondary key promotes, removal back to 8 demotes, and secondary identity is exact PKey ordering with no randomized or probabilistic hash. Insert, overwrite, lookup, remove, collapse, clone, fork, equality, and canonical iteration match independent BTreeMap and simple-vector models for ordinary, random, all-primary-colliding, and exact-key cases. Arc path-copying preserves O(1) snapshots and shares every unchanged map and collision node. Allocation, comparison, collision-cardinality, expanded-weight, and fresh-node budgets are preflighted deterministically; zero, one-under, exact, one-over, refusal, and recovery budget cases return typed nonpublication outcomes, never rejection or partial mutation, and exact expanded-weight totals cannot saturate or wrap into acceptance. Where the owning Environment operation exposes cancellation or InternalFault, its integration tests must prove the same nonpublication and recovery law without forcing cancellation state into the pure PMap API. Exact 1k and 10k all-collision construction and lookup counters prove the registered logarithmic or subquadratic bounds against the legacy N times N-minus-1 over 2 copy baseline, while timing is recorded only as diagnostic evidence. Deterministic insertion permutations, removals, thread partitions 1, 8, and 32, repeated runs, and platform lanes produce byte-identical canonical order, environment roots, recovery roots, diagnostics, and counters. Unit and property tests cover both promotion boundaries, overwrite without cardinality growth, every AVL rotation shape, demotion, root collapse, snapshot isolation, equality across tiers, clone and drop behavior, budget refusal atomicity, exact-bound recovery, and adversarial comparison or allocation accounting; named mutants that disable promotion or sharing, alter ordering, skip budget charging, saturate weight, lose refusal atomicity, or accept a wrong key are killed. The shared environment E2E emits exactly the disjoint fln.e2e.environment-resource-collision version 1 records for 1, 8, and 32 threads, with positive, planted inline-threshold mutant, exact byte-restoration, and clean recovery phases. Strict validation checks the complete field set and exact identities, orders, roots, cardinalities, tier and threshold facts, per-worker schedules, comparison and allocation bounds, shared and fresh nodes, budgets and resources, monotonic timings excluded from the gate, separate stdout and stderr artifacts, process result, cleanup, and final state; it rejects extra or missing fields, stale roots, over-bound counters, false atomicity, duplicate or malformed schedules, compile failures, unrelated or wrong assertions, merged streams, missing mutant marker, and surviving mutants. Retained manifest-backed bundles validate independently. scripts/check.sh, fmt, cargo check and test, all fln-env and relevant fln-hash or domain suites, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, the focused E2E positive-mutation-recovery lane and all currently runnable environment integration checks that do not require unfinished sibling slices are green with artifact paths recorded. Final fln-amv.11 and W2 aggregation remains downstream and is not a circular prerequisite for this child. No vector-sized update cloning, randomized secondary identity, timing-only claim, valid-key rejection, global mutable table, hidden Inconclusive promotion, mock-only evidence, unclassified skip, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.

title: fln-env E2E: migrate legacy env_snapshots parent to authoritative evidence schema
description:
The shared scripts/e2e/env_snapshots.sh parent still publishes legacy fln-e2e/1 records and treats any nonzero extension-mutant run as a kill. Migrate the outer environment scenario to the authoritative fln.e2e/2 lifecycle instead of relying on a nested child for only fln-amv.10. Add a durable terminal finalizer for setup failures, timeout, cancellation, inconclusive execution, and internal faults; supervise every command with exact wrapper and child classifications; bind the live input and vendor tree; emit separate stdout and stderr artifacts; validate semantic extension-mutant failure signatures so compile errors and unrelated test failures cannot pass; restore from byte-identical pristine input; and publish a manifest plus complete-bundle commitment only after strict validation. Preserve the nested fln-amv.10 child as a separately scoped bounded-model claim or fold it into a composition that retains exact child identity without duplicating detail rows. Preserve every subsequently added child claim, including fln-amv.13 resource-collision evidence, with the same identity and non-duplication law. No deletion of retained historical bundles.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `scripts/e2e/env_snapshots.sh` and inspect the outer `run.ndjson`; the parent rows use `fln-e2e/1` rather than the authoritative `fln.e2e/2` lifecycle and do not independently commit a manifest-backed bundle.
2. In an isolated retained overlay, replace the intended extension-state mutant with a compile error or an unrelated failing test. The legacy parent accepts any nonzero exit as a mutant kill instead of requiring the named semantic failure signature.
3. Inject setup failure, timeout, cancellation, resource exhaustion, or interruption before parent completion. The legacy path lacks the same typed, durable terminal/finalizer guarantees used by authoritative child bundles.
4. Compare outer artifacts with the nested collision child: only the child has separately captured streams, exact supervisor classification, governed roots, strict validation, and a complete-bundle commitment.

## Acceptance Criteria

The outer environment scenario uses `fln.e2e/2` from run start through exactly one durable terminal event; every stage is supervised with exact wrapper/child exit, signal, classification, timing, budget, and cleanup facts. Fresh artifact-directory acceptance, governed live-input hashing, pinned vendor binding, source snapshots, host/toolchain facts, and separate bounded stdout/stderr capture all occur inside the evidence lifecycle. Positive, named extension mutants, SetUnion mutant, every preserved nested child, exact byte-restoration recovery, setup fault, timeout, cancellation, resource exhaustion, inconclusive, and internal-fault paths have strict validators and diagnosable artifacts. A compile error, unrelated failure, wrong assertion, merged stream, missing stderr, malformed/extra field, stale root, source drift, or surviving mutant cannot satisfy a kill or pass claim. Nested fln-amv.10 and fln-amv.13 claims remain separately identifiable and are referenced once by the parent without duplicating their detail rows. Manifest, digest, and write-once complete-bundle publication occur only after run validation and governed-root revalidation; independent read-only validation succeeds, while failed or cancelled runs cannot be promoted to pass. Historical retained bundles are never deleted. Shell syntax, ShellCheck, evidence validator self-tests, fln-env tests, positive/mutant/recovery E2E runs, fault drills, `validate-run`, `validate-bundle`, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and all currently runnable environment integration checks that do not require unfinished sibling slices are green with artifact paths recorded at closure. Final fln-amv and W2 aggregation remains downstream and is not a circular prerequisite for this child.

Identity-claim composition is mandatory: this migration is also the authoritative shared E2E owner for fln-amv.1, fln-amv.2, and fln-amv.12. Preserve separately identifiable child records for (a) all five mutual-block-bearing declaration kinds across empty, singleton, repeated, reordered, renamed, and declared-large membership, with exact content-digest and logical-root propagation; (b) all twelve extension descriptor combinations with frozen merge/checkpoint/provenance tags and descriptor-before-journal order; and (c) every DefinitionSafety and QuotKind value with frozen explicit schema tags. Each child keeps its own positive, named single-defect, typed nonpublication, exact-restoration, and clean-recovery matrix. The parent references each child exactly once and cannot substitute aggregate parent success for child-complete proof.
acceptance_criteria:
Migrate the complete outer env_snapshots lifecycle to authoritative fln.e2e/2 supervision and write-once bundle publication without narrowing any existing parent or child proof. Named shell/Python/Rust unit and validator contract suites must cover lifecycle transitions, exact supervisor/child classification, field/bound/schema rejection, semantic mutant signatures, terminal finalization, child-pointer uniqueness, manifest commitments, and read-only revalidation; planted any-nonzero, missing-stream, stale-root, duplicate-child, incomplete-terminal, source-drift, and promotion-before-validation mutants must be killed. The shared no-mock E2E exercises positive, named extension and SetUnion mutants, every preserved fln-amv.10/fln-amv.13 child, setup fault, timeout, cancellation, resource exhaustion, Inconclusive/InternalFault, exact byte-restoration, and later recovery. It emits concise human logs plus strict schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step/supervisor/child ids, exact redacted argv/cwd, governed inputs/vendor/toolchain/host facts, separate bounded stdout/stderr artifacts, expected/actual semantic signatures, timings/budgets/status, roots/manifest/digest, cleanup, and final state. Shell syntax, ShellCheck, validator self-tests, fln-env tests, positive/mutant/recovery/fault runs, validate-run, validate-bundle, fmt, clippy, UBS, and runnable slice gates are green with retained artifact paths. No any-nonzero mutant acceptance, partial terminal record, duplicated/lost child evidence, source drift, merged stream, historical deletion, or circular final-parent prerequisite satisfies closure.

The authoritative bundle must include field-complete, separately scoped fln-amv.1, fln-amv.2, and fln-amv.12 child matrices. For fln-amv.1, validate every current all-bearing declaration kind and membership boundary plus dropped-list, omitted-count, reordered-membership, wrong-domain, and failed-root-propagation defects. For fln-amv.2, validate all twelve descriptor combinations, exact stable tags, descriptor-before-journal ordering, and omitted-dimension, swapped-tag, debug-text, and after-journal defects. For fln-amv.12, validate every DefinitionSafety and QuotKind tag, repeated digest/root stability, the required thread matrix, and cast/source-order dependence. Each matrix must bind canonical inputs, expected and actual digest/root relations, resources, mutation identity, artifacts, cleanup, and final state; strict child validation rejects missing, extra, duplicate, stale, reordered, contradictory, or cross-child rows. Exact restoration and later clean recovery are mandatory, and parent publication is forbidden until all three child validations and independent bundle validation pass.

title: fln-env: bind extension contracts into logical environment roots
description:
Problem: Environment::logical_root hashes each extension name and ordered payload journal, but omits ExtensionDescriptor.merge, checkpoint, and provenance. Environments with identical declarations and entries but different merge behavior, snapshot contract, or invalidation transparency therefore share a logical root even though their semantics differ. That can alias Ledger cache keys, receipts, branch behavior, and invalidation policy. Scope: define a stable explicit canonical encoding for every current MergeSemantics, CheckpointSemantics, and PayloadProvenance variant and include it in the extension delta digest before the ordered entry journal. Do not rely on Rust enum layout or debug text. Verification requirements: (1) table-driven unit coverage proves every descriptor dimension changes the logical root while an identical descriptor/journal remains stable; (2) all variants are exhaustively matched so additions fail compilation until assigned a schema value; (3) existing entry-order and insertion-order laws remain green; (4) the shared environment E2E lane must eventually emit versioned line-oriented records with descriptor case, expected root relation, actual relation, root values, and final result; (5) cargo fmt, all fln-env tests, fln-env clippy with warnings denied, and UBS pass. Preserve all existing extension payloads and registry behavior; this changes identity only where behavior already differs.

## Steps to Reproduce

Register the same extension name and payload journal in separate environments while changing only merge, checkpoint, or provenance semantics. Before the fix, every pair receives the same logical root because descriptor contracts are omitted.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: The logical extension delta binds one explicit exhaustive canonical descriptor table before the ordered journal: MergeSemantics AppendOrdered=0, SetUnion=1, ConflictsRequireReview=2; CheckpointSemantics JournalSuffix=0, FullJournal=1; PayloadProvenance Understood=0, Opaque=1. No Rust discriminant, repr, debug text, implicit default, or reordered adjacent bytes may determine durable identity, and a new variant must fail compilation until assigned a reviewed tag. Table-driven unit tests hold name and journal constant while varying each dimension, prove every semantic change changes the logical root, identical contracts remain byte/root stable, all current combinations are pairwise classified, and existing journal-order, registry-order, snapshot, and 1/8/32-thread determinism laws remain green. Named mutants that omit each dimension, swap tags, encode debug text, or hash the descriptor after entries are killed. The shared real Environment E2E emits schema-versioned records for every descriptor family plus planted omission and clean recovery, including exact tags, expected/actual root relation and values, canonical input hash, process/resource facts, cleanup, and final state; strict validation rejects incomplete, duplicate, stale, contradictory, or extra rows. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, UBS, scripts/check.sh, and every currently runnable environment integration lane pass with retained independently validated artifacts. Existing extension payloads and registry behavior are preserved; only already-distinct semantics acquire distinct identity. No mock-only proof, unclassified skip, silent tag renumbering, or deferred listed behavior satisfies closure.

title: fln-env: refuse extension merges with mismatched contracts
description:
Problem: ExtensionState::merge checks base/ours/theirs descriptor equality only with debug_assert_eq!. Contract mismatches therefore panic in debug builds and are unchecked in optimized builds, where data can be merged under base semantics and returned with another branch descriptor. This violates the typed-conflict promise, failure atomicity, and deterministic snapshot identity. Scope: replace assertion-only preconditions with an explicit typed conflict that reports the base, ours, and theirs descriptor identities sufficiently to diagnose which name/merge/checkpoint/provenance field diverged. Validate contracts before inspecting journals or selecting merge behavior. Preserve successful behavior for AppendOrdered, SetUnion, and ConflictsRequireReview. Verification requirements: deterministic table-driven unit tests cover mismatches in extension name, merge semantics, checkpoint semantics, and payload provenance on each branch; prove no panic and no input mutation; retain success and bilateral-change conflict coverage for all three merge modes; add or extend a shared environment E2E scenario with detailed versioned line logs for typed mismatch and clean recovery; cargo fmt, all fln-env tests, fln-env clippy with warnings denied, and UBS must pass. No release-only behavior split and no compatibility shim.

## Steps to Reproduce

Call `ExtensionState::merge` with matching journals but a differing descriptor on either branch. Before the fix, debug builds panic at `debug_assert_eq!`; optimized builds continue under the base policy and may return another descriptor.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut in the description is mandatory. Closure requires deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path no-mock E2E success plus typed negative/recovery scenarios with detailed schema-versioned logs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. Descriptor validation must finish before journal traversal, payload cloning, merge-policy dispatch, or result publication; a mismatch therefore has work independent of journal entry and payload volume except for comparing the three bounded descriptors. Diagnostics use stable compact descriptor identities and field-level differences rather than embedding journals or payloads. Tests exercise every descriptor field mismatch with empty and declared-large journals, prove the journals remain byte-identical, prove no partial product/root is observable, and show a subsequent compatible merge succeeds. Named mutants that skip each descriptor field, defer validation until after traversal, select a merge policy before validation, clone payloads on refusal, or publish a partial product/root must each be killed by a discriminating test. The E2E covers positive merge, each refusal class, a planted validation-order/publication defect, exact restoration, and clean recovery; records include descriptor ids, differing fields, journal entry/byte counts, selected_policy set to none on refusal, expected and actual outcome, work/resource facts, mutation identity, artifact paths, cleanup, and final state. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, debug/release behavior split, partial implementation, compatibility shim, deferred feature, or surviving mutant satisfies this bead.

title: fln-env: validate extension branch ancestry before merge
description:
Problem: ExtensionState::merge documents that ours and theirs derive from base, then unconditionally skips base.entries.len() without verifying that either branch contains the exact base journal prefix. A shorter, unrelated, or same-length-different branch can silently lose entries, produce a false no-change decision, or merge histories that never shared the claimed base. Scope: after descriptor validation and before merge-policy selection, verify the full ordered base journal is a prefix of each branch. Refuse invalid ancestry with a typed deterministic conflict that identifies the extension, base/branch lengths, and common-prefix lengths without copying unbounded payloads. Preserve successful AppendOrdered, SetUnion, and review-required behavior for valid descendants. Verification requirements: deterministic unit tables cover shorter branch, first-entry divergence, later-entry divergence, same-length divergence, each branch independently, and both branches invalid; valid empty/base-only/one-sided/bilateral descendants remain green; errors do not panic or mutate inputs; existing order and root laws remain green; add or extend the shared environment E2E lane with detailed versioned logs for invalid ancestry and clean recovery; cargo fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy with warnings denied, and UBS pass. No silent rebasing or best-effort merge.

## Steps to Reproduce

Pass a base journal and an ours or theirs journal that is shorter or differs inside the claimed base prefix. Before the fix, merge skips `base.entries.len()` without validating ancestry and can discard or misclassify entries.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: After descriptor validation and before any merge-policy decision, ExtensionState::merge proves that the full ordered base journal is an exact prefix of ours and theirs independently. Short branches, first-entry divergence, later divergence, equal-length divergence, longer divergent branches, either invalid side, and both invalid sides return a typed deterministic HistoryMismatch naming the extension and exact base/branch/common-prefix lengths without copying unbounded payloads; neither inputs nor any Environment publication state changes. Valid empty, base-only, one-sided, bilateral, nested, AppendOrdered, SetUnion, and ConflictsRequireReview descendants retain their existing semantics, entry order, roots, and review outcomes. Unit tables and generated model/property tests cover every invalid/valid class, failure atomicity, bounded diagnostics, repeated refusal, and clean recovery; named mutants that skip prefix validation, check only length or one branch, compare unordered entries, or merge after refusal are killed. The shared real Environment E2E emits schema-versioned invalid-ancestry negative and valid-descendant recovery records with exact prefix facts, before/after roots, expected/actual outcome, process and resource facts, cleanup, and final state; strict validation rejects missing, stale, contradictory, duplicate, or extra cases. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, UBS, scripts/check.sh, and every currently runnable environment integration lane pass with retained independently validated artifacts. No silent rebase, best-effort merge, mock-only proof, unclassified skip, or deferred listed behavior satisfies closure.

title: fln-env: make extension journals persistently shared and linear-time to replay
description:
Problem: ExtensionState documents a structurally shared append-only journal, but stores Vec<ExtensionEntry>; push_entry clones the whole Vec on every append. Replaying N imported entries performs quadratic pointer copying and every branch duplicates its full journal, defeating the O(1)-snapshot/structural-sharing design at the extension layer. Scope: replace the contiguous Vec contract with an immutable persistent append log whose snapshot is one bounded pointer increment, append performs bounded work independent of total journal length (for example fixed-size shared chunks), and deterministic oldest-to-newest replay is linear without permanently flattening the journal. Because there are no users, revise the internal/public entries slice API directly to explicit len/is_empty/iter accessors; do not add a compatibility shim that reintroduces flattening. Preserve exact payload bytes, replay order, descriptor semantics, logical roots, branch-ancestry validation, and all three merge modes. Design must specify chunk size, iterator lifetime/order, equality, prefix comparison, clone/drop costs, and resource accounting. Verification requirements: model/property tests compare random append/fork/merge histories against Vec; pointer-identity/allocation instrumentation proves snapshots share all journal storage and each append creates only a bounded number of nodes/entry slots; boundary tests straddle empty/one/chunk-full/multi-chunk states; 10k and 100k replay tests demonstrate linear growth with recorded operations/allocations rather than a timing-only claim; all existing root/order/conflict tests remain exact. Extend the shared real-path environment E2E lane with schema-versioned logs recording entry count, chunk count, shared-node count, operations/allocations, expected versus actual order/root, timing/resource facts, and clean final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and the parent verification gates. No global mutable cache, leaked flattening buffer, or semantic reordering.

## Steps to Reproduce

Append successively larger entry sequences through `ExtensionState::push_entry` and count cloned entry slots. The current Vec-backed state copies the full prior journal on each append, producing quadratic cumulative work and no shared journal storage across branches.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Extension journals use a specified immutable persistent append structure with O(1) snapshot clone/non-final drop, append work bounded independently of history length, borrowed oldest-to-newest iteration, deterministic equality/prefix behavior, cached prefix/resource facts, bounded-depth final release, and no flattening compatibility shim or global cache. Exact payload bytes, descriptors, logical roots, ancestry checks, provenance, and all merge modes are preserved. 2. Named unit/property/model tests cover empty/one/chunk-boundary/multi-chunk journals; fork/append/merge histories against a Vec oracle; branch isolation; conflict and resource boundaries; prefix equality; and 10,000/100,000-entry replay. Pointer/node/allocation/copied-slot/operation counters prove structural sharing and linear replay without relying on timing. 3. Mutation-sensitive tests and the real shared environment lane cover positive journal behavior, at least one planted semantic failure, and exact clean recovery; every failure is typed and atomic. 4. The no-mock E2E emits schema-versioned line-oriented records with entry/chunk/shared-node counts, operations, allocations, expected/actual order and roots, resource/timing facts, mutation classification, artifact paths, cleanup, and final state; retained evidence is independently parseable. 5. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, warnings-denied Clippy, E2E shell syntax/ShellCheck, UBS under the documented user-required scanner exclusion, and parent gates are green.
close_reason: Persistent journal semantics and all named unit/model/scaling/E2E/logging/gate obligations are implemented and verified.

title: fln-env: define and enforce SetUnion duplicate semantics without losing replay fidelity
description:
Load-bearing ambiguity: extension replay promises exact ordered preservation of imported payload entries, while MergeSemantics::SetUnion says entries form a byte-keyed set whose duplicates collapse. The current merge only filters entries from the theirs suffix against ours; duplicates already present in base or ours survive, and push_entry always appends. Normalizing blindly could destroy required Reference-observable replay data, while leaving behavior as-is makes the SetUnion contract false and branch-order dependent. Scope: first anchor the intended law to the pinned Reference extension behavior and the generated extension census/fixtures, then define whether set semantics apply to the stored raw journal, branch deltas, a derived semantic view, or merge output. Preserve raw import bytes/order whenever that is observable; if a semantic set view is required, represent it separately rather than laundering data loss. Specify equality/keying, first-occurrence ordering, base duplicates, duplicates within each suffix, duplicates across branches, empty payloads, hash collisions, and deterministic ours/theirs ordering. Verification requirements: table/property tests cover every duplicate location and branch permutation against an explicit model; a regression proves no required replay byte disappears; identical logical inputs yield identical roots and merge products; a deliberate one-sided-only dedup defect is detected. Extend the shared no-mock environment E2E lane with detailed schema-versioned records for raw journal, semantic view, expected/actual merge order, duplicate decisions, roots, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and applicable parent gates. This bead must resolve the contract from evidence before implementation; no guessed normalization and no silent data loss.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut in the description is mandatory. Closure requires the Reference-anchored raw-journal versus semantic-view law; deterministic table/property/model coverage of every duplicate location and branch permutation; the named one-sided-dedup mutant; and a shared real-path no-mock E2E success, negative, exhaustion, exact-restoration, and recovery campaign with detailed schema-versioned logs. The implementation declares independent maximum-entry, cumulative-payload-byte, and per-entry-byte budgets at the environment boundary. All raw entries admitted within those budgets retain exact bytes and order; reaching a budget while projecting or merging yields typed Inconclusive, publishes no journal, semantic view, root, or partial merge, and a later within-budget merge must succeed. Boundary tests cover zero, exact-limit, one-over-limit, empty payload, duplicate-heavy, and unique-heavy inputs, including both branch orders. Evidence records configured and consumed entries/bytes, duplicate decisions, raw and semantic counts, expected/actual order and roots, terminal outcome, mutation identity, separate bounded stdout/stderr artifact paths, cleanup, and recovery state. The manifest inventories every artifact with a content hash and input identity; strict validation rejects missing, extra, stale, swapped, merged-stream, wrong-mutant, and surviving-mutant evidence, and the retained bundle validates independently. The documented cost model must match the chosen exact-byte data structure and be checked on a declared large fixture; no silent truncation, guessed normalization, lossy deduplication, unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead. All stated fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates remain mandatory.

title: fln-env: implement executable extension checkpoint and restore semantics
description:
Missing parent feature: CheckpointSemantics is stored and now enters logical identity, but no checkpoint/restore API consults it. The registry contract is therefore descriptive only, so branch snapshots cannot actually honor JournalSuffix versus FullJournal. Scope: define typed versioned checkpoint values and restore/apply operations. JournalSuffix checkpoints must bind the exact base extension identity, descriptor, journal position, and content/root digest and carry only the deterministic suffix; FullJournal checkpoints must carry the complete ordered raw journal and require no ambient base. Applying a suffix to the wrong name, contract, base length, base digest, or divergent history must fail atomically with a typed diagnostic; replayed checkpoints must preserve payload bytes/order, provenance, logical roots, and merge behavior. Define empty/no-change checkpoints, nested checkpoints, repeated restore, stale checkpoints, cross-branch use, resource limits, and forward schema-version rejection. Use the persistent journal representation from fln-amv.5 rather than flattening or copying the full history for suffix mode. Verification requirements: model/property tests generate fork/checkpoint/restore chains for both modes and compare exact states/roots; negative tables cover every binding mismatch, truncation/version/resource boundary, and recovery; instrumentation proves suffix size/work is proportional to the suffix and FullJournal is explicitly linear. Extend the shared real-path environment E2E lane with detailed schema-versioned logs containing mode, base/checkpoint/restored ids and roots, entry counts, expected/actual outcome, resources, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and parent gates. No advisory enum with ignored behavior and no silent fallback from suffix to full capture.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Versioned JournalSuffix and FullJournal checkpoints have executable capture/apply/restore semantics. Suffix checkpoints bind exact name, descriptor, base identity/length/digest/root and carry only deterministic suffix data through the persistent journal; full checkpoints are self-contained and reject ambient bases. Payload order/bytes, provenance, roots, repeat restore, nesting, branch behavior, and every merge mode are preserved. 2. Wrong name, contract, base length/digest/history, schema version, truncation, stale/cross-branch use, resource excess, and inappropriate ambient base each fail atomically with a typed diagnostic; empty/no-change and exact-boundary cases succeed, and there is no silent suffix-to-full fallback. 3. Named unit/property/model tests generate fork/checkpoint/restore chains for both modes, compare exact state/root against an independent model, cover every positive merge/provenance combination and complete negative/resource tables, and prove suffix work/size is proportional to the suffix while FullJournal work is explicitly linear in its carried history. 4. The real shared no-mock environment E2E covers suffix, full, wrong-base or equivalent planted failure, and pristine recovery. It emits schema-versioned line-oriented records with mode, base/checkpoint/restored IDs and roots, entry counts, resources/operation facts, expected/actual outcome and diagnostic class, artifact paths, cleanup, and final state. 5. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, warnings-denied Clippy, E2E shell syntax/ShellCheck, UBS under the documented user-required scanner exclusion, and parent gates are green.
close_reason: Executable versioned checkpoint/restore semantics and all named positive, negative, model, resource, E2E, logging, and gate obligations are verified.

title: fln-env: add the pinned attribute-state substrate without executing upstream code
description:
Missing parent feature: fln-amv requires Reference-compatible attribute state, but fln-env currently models only constants and generic extension journals. Scope begins with a pin-anchored census of the Reference attribute registry/state shapes and observable queries, then implements the native environment substrate for attribute definitions and assignments as data. Preserve registration identity, target declaration, parameters/payload, priority/order/scoping/persistence behavior present at the pin, import replay, removal or replacement rules, and unknown/custom attribute data without executing Reference Lean code. Reuse the extension registry where the pin models attributes as extensions, but expose typed attribute queries rather than a disconnected second truth. Specify what enters the logical root and provenance graph, how opaque custom data blocks fine invalidation, duplicate/conflicting registrations, missing targets, branch snapshots/merges/checkpoints, and epoch/version behavior. Verification requirements: generated census fixtures and differential tests against the pinned oracle for ordering and query results; unit/property tables for every built-in state shape plus malformed/unknown/duplicate/resource cases; snapshot isolation and schedule-independence at multiple insertion orders; a named defect that drops priority/scope/import order must be detected. Extend the shared no-mock environment E2E lane with real extracted attribute fixtures and detailed schema-versioned logs containing epoch, attribute/target ids, ordered results, roots, evidence grade, expected/actual values, resources, and final state. Run fmt, all relevant tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, contract regeneration where applicable, and parent gates. No hand-transcribed census, hosted Lean implementation, or loss of opaque custom data.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
design: Dependency topology: this bead consumes the exact effective-import contract from fln-amv.9.2 and the atomic module/provenance/invalidation/lifecycle contract from fln-amv.9.3. It does not consume the module fixture-integration result from fln-amv.9.4, so attribute fixtures and module fixtures are parallel extensions of the existing shared environment E2E harness. fln-amv.11 is the join: it remains blocked on both the completed module epic and this attribute-state bead, and therefore still requires both real fixture lanes before global merge can close. Attribute implementation must reuse the same committed module/provenance state and may not construct a parallel import or ownership truth.
acceptance_criteria: A pin-anchored mechanically generated census defines every observable Reference attribute registration/state/query shape; no hand-transcribed or unclassified row is allowed. Native attribute definitions and assignments preserve registration identity, target declaration, parameters/payload, priority, ordering, scope, persistence, import replay, removal/replacement, epoch behavior, and duplicate/conflict semantics without executing upstream Lean implementation code. Attribute state reuses the canonical extension/module/provenance commit and exposes typed queries rather than a second import truth. Exact logical-root and provenance participation is specified per field. Unknown/custom payloads round-trip byte-exact with explicit opaque grade and conservatively block fine invalidation/migration; missing targets, malformed payloads, stale epochs, and resource/cancellation failures are typed and atomic. Snapshots/checkpoints/merges share structure, preserve branch isolation, and remain deterministic across insertion/import order and 1/8/32 threads. Unit/property/model/differential/mutation suites cover every builtin shape and query, duplicate/conflict/removal, priority/scope/order, opaque data, budgets, and recovery; named dropped-priority, scope, import-order, payload, or root/provenance mutants are killed. title: fln-env: implement the environment module-import graph and provenance contract
description:
W2 integration epic for the complete environment module-import graph promised by plan section 7.1. The graph must be native, immutable, structurally shared, pin-anchored, and lossless: module identity is explicitly bound to artifact-resolution evidence; every direct Lean.Import row preserves module, importAll, isExported, isMeta, source order, and duplicates; effective closure reproduces the Reference visibility/data/IR fixed point; and contribution provenance links modules to declarations, extra declarations, and extension entries without executing upstream code.

This epic is deliberately decomposed into four mandatory children: (1) the persistent ordered module-record/DAG substrate over the lossless decoder prerequisite fln-20n.1; (2) the Reference effective-import lattice, closure, and deterministic query policies; (3) declaration/extension contribution provenance, its separate graph identity, snapshot/merge/checkpoint behavior, and Ledger invalidation contract; and (4) decoded real-fixture integration plus the shared no-mock E2E evidence matrix. The integration child is the sole direct completion blocker for this epic; it depends on every component proof.

The plan-defined environment logical root remains declarations plus extension deltas plus options. Import topology and artifact-resolution evidence receive a separate schema-versioned graph/provenance digest and may enter future Ledger query keys and receipts as an explicit additional field; they must not be smuggled into or silently omitted from the logical root. If later trust analysis makes topology part of trusted identity, that requires an explicit plan/epoch amendment.

## Success Criteria

All four child beads and the lossless decoder prerequisite are complete. Exact direct-import order/flags, Reference effective closure, typed cycle/missing/resource outcomes, provenance completeness, structural sharing, deterministic traversal, real pinned fixtures, mutation coverage, and the detailed shared no-mock E2E success/negative/recovery logs all pass with green format, tests, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, contract checks, and parent gates. No name-only graph, generic graph substitute, inferred import flags, opaque provenance, topology hidden in the logical root, partial child closure, or deferred listed feature satisfies this epic.
acceptance_criteria: All four mandatory component and integration children plus the lossless decoder prerequisite are closed without narrowed scope. Exact direct-import rows, Reference effective closure, deterministic queries, typed missing, cycle, resource, cancellation, Inconclusive, and InternalFault outcomes, complete and partial provenance grades, separate logical and provenance roots, structural sharing, and pinned real fixtures match the contracts in the description. Every component contributes named unit, boundary, independent model or property, permutation, mutation-kill, and recovery tests; the integration child supplies the real no-mock positive, negative, resource, interruption, and recovery E2E matrix with concise human output, strict schema-versioned NDJSON, separate bounded streams, exact identities, roots, budgets, first divergences, cleanup, and retained manifests or bundles. Validator negative tests reject malformed, extra-field, stale, wrong-mutant, merged-stream, partial, and surviving-mutant evidence. Format, all relevant tests, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, contract checks, child gates, and W2 parent gates are green. No name-only graph, inferred flag, hidden root change, opaque provenance presented as complete, mock-only claim, partial child closure, or deferred feature satisfies closure.

title: fln-env: build the lossless persistent module-record DAG core
description:
Build the pin-anchored immutable module-record and ordered DAG substrate for fln-amv.9. The input contract is Lean v4.32.0 Import(module, importAll, isExported, isMeta) and ModuleData(isModule, imports, contributions). This bead depends on fln-20n.1 so the environment types must represent every decoded field without invention or normalization.

Define typed ModuleId/ModuleRecord/DirectImport values. Module identity is not stored inside ModuleData, so registration must bind the caller-supplied Name to explicit artifact-resolution evidence (epoch, artifact content identity, producer/source grade) rather than deriving it from a path string or assuming the first import. Preserve each direct-import array exactly, including duplicate rows and all flags. Provide an immutable PMap-backed graph whose clone is O(1), whose module insertion path-copies only bounded structure, and whose direct_imports query returns Reference order. Define exact idempotent re-registration versus conflicting module-record behavior, reflexive-edge and cycle refusal, duplicate-edge classification without deduplication, missing-node/incomplete-closure state, maximum modules/edges/name depth/payload bytes, and atomic failure.

Register deterministic policies separately: ReferenceDirectOrder for observable arrays; ReferenceDiscoveryOrder for import replay; CanonicalNameOrder for semantically free diagnostics/sets. Do not let PMap hash/bucket order become user-visible. The graph must expose typed completeness, conflict dimensions, and deterministic operation/resource facts suitable for later effective-closure and provenance layers.

Crate direction is load-bearing: fln-olean already depends on fln-env, so fln-env must not depend back on fln-olean merely to prove the seam. The concrete ModuleDataView to ModuleRecord adapter and shared real-fixture/no-mock proof belong to fln-amv.9.4. This bead instead proves the environment-side schema field-for-field and leaves no representation in which that adapter could silently discard an import flag, row, identity, or artifact-evidence field.

Verification: exhaustive record-field tables; random DAG model tests against a Vec/BTreeMap oracle; duplicate-row, missing-node, conflicting-record, self-edge, multi-node cycle, malformed identity, and every resource boundary; insertion-order and thread-partition metamorphic checks; pointer/allocation instrumentation for O(1) snapshots and bounded path copying; large sparse/dense DAG operation-count evidence rather than timing alone. Add named mutations that drop and reorder direct rows and require both to be killed. The shared real-fixture/no-mock evidence remains mandatory in fln-amv.9.4 for epic closure. Run scoped format, all fln-env tests, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and contract checks.

No adjacency set that loses duplicates/order, name-only Import, path-derived identity, reverse fln-env to fln-olean dependency, unbounded traversal, partial insertion, silent cycle repair, or generic graph abstraction that hides the Reference contract.

## Acceptance Criteria

The exact persistent module-record/DAG contract above is implemented with complete model, negative, resource, determinism, mutation, operation-count, and sharing evidence. The fln-20n.1 schema is represented field-for-field on the environment side; fln-amv.9.4 retains the concrete decoder adapter plus shared real-fixture/E2E obligation. All scoped gates pass. No partial or mock-only implementation satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ModuleId, DirectImport, ModuleRecord, and registration evidence represent Lean v4.32.0 ModuleData and every Import field exactly, preserving structural Name identity, row order, duplicates, isModule, epoch, content identity, producer, and source grade without path-derived or inferred values. 2. The immutable PMap-backed graph provides O(1) snapshot clone, bounded path-copy insertion, exact idempotence, field-specific conflict diagnostics, typed self-edge/cycle/missing-node/duplicate-row/resource outcomes, atomic failure, and separately registered ReferenceDirectOrder, ReferenceDiscoveryOrder, and CanonicalNameOrder policies; map bucket order is never user-visible. 3. Named unit/property/model/metamorphic tests cover all record fields and flags, duplicate classifications, malformed identity, conflicts, cycles, incomplete closure, every exact/cumulative resource boundary, insertion permutations, thread partitions, snapshot sharing, sparse/dense operation counts, and atomic recovery. Named drop-row and reorder-row mutations are killed with schema-versioned structured evidence. 4. The environment-side schema is field-for-field compatible with fln-20n.1 without adding a forbidden fln-env to fln-olean dependency. The concrete decoder adapter and no-mock real-fixture positive/failure/recovery E2E with detailed schema-versioned logs remain mandatory in fln-amv.9.4 and must block epic closure; this bead does not weaken or duplicate that integration boundary. 5. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, warnings-denied Clippy, UBS under the documented user-required scanner exclusion, and applicable dependency/contract gates are green.
close_reason: Lossless persistent module DAG core and its complete scoped proof matrix are implemented; the decoder adapter and shared real-fixture E2E remain explicitly owned by fln-amv.9.4.

title: fln-env: reproduce the pinned effective-import fixed point and queries
description:
Implement the pinned v4.32.0 importModulesCore fixed point and query semantics over fln-amv.9.1, derived from vendor/lean4-src/src/Lean/Environment.lean:2057-2187. This is not ordinary reachability: the Reference propagates data visibility and IR requirements separately, revisits a module when later paths upgrade effective state, and records first discovery in recursive postorder.

Model the exposure lattice explicitly as none, private, privateAll, public, and all. privateAll and public are incomparable; their join is all. Preserve the exact two-flag Reference representation and equations for each row i: needsData := parent.needsData && (i.isExported || parent.importAll); importAll := globalLevel == private || (parent.importAll && i.importAll); isExported := parent.isExported && i.isExported; needsIRTrans := parent.needsIRTrans || (needsData && i.isMeta); needsIR := needsIRTrans || importAll || globalLevel > exported. Initial root context is importAll=true, needsData=true, needsIRTrans=false, and isExported defaults to (globalLevel < private); the explicit importModulesCore isExported override is supported and is part of request identity.

Represent OLean global levels exported/server/private and IRPhases runtime/comptime/all exactly. A fresh candidate has phases all when importAll, comptime when needsIRTrans, otherwise runtime. On an existing module, importAll/isExported/needsData/needsIRTrans join by OR and unequal IRPhases join to all. Server mode can request an IR artifact while leaving IRPhases runtime; keep IR request, transitive IR need, and phase distinct. Actual optional IR-file availability/loading belongs to the artifact integration in fln-amv.9.4 and must not be invented in fln-env. EffectiveImport.isMeta remains the first-discovery row value because the Reference does not join it during upgrades. The update condition is exactly the five effective fields above; a genuine upgrade replays the entire stored direct-import array.

Preserve direct row order and recursive postorder discovery: on first discovery, traverse the module direct rows before appending the module; on an upgrade, replay its closure without appending it again. Repeated and duplicate imports remain distinct work inputs and can join state. Missing records become row-indexed typed Incomplete findings with an honest partial closure and canonical diagnostic order. Cancellation and work/witness/resource exhaustion are typed Inconclusive outcomes under FL-INV-07; impossible engine invariants are typed InternalFault outcomes, never user rejection and never panic. Anonymous or overflowing root module identities are definitive typed invalid requests before traversal. A direct non-module import from exported/server module mode is the Reference-defined definitive invalid-request outcome and must remain distinct from Incomplete/Inconclusive. Cyclic graphs are refused by fln-amv.9.1 and must be tested at that boundary rather than reintroduced here.

APIs must support direct imports, exact effective state per module, Reference discovery sequence, canonical transitive set, exact reverse import rows plus canonical reverse importers, and witnesses explaining each joined visibility/IR component. Witness steps identify root-or-module source, direct-row index, target, and triggering flags so duplicates remain explainable. Every outcome carries status, partial-or-complete data, completeness findings, and deterministic operation/resource facts. Do not add a cache until its key includes durable graph identity plus the entire root request/profile; pointer-only identities with ABA risk and request-incomplete keys are prohibited. fln-amv.9.3 owns the durable graph-root domain.

Verification: implement an independent worklist/reference model from the pinned equations and compare every flag combination, every exposure and IR join, first-discovery isMeta retention, server IR-load/runtime-phase distinction, and update condition. Generated random DAGs compare exact effective maps, Reference postorder, row-indexed witnesses, reverse queries, and incomplete findings. Cover duplicate/diamond/upgrade-late/missing/non-module/resource/cancellation cases; insertion permutations and thread partitions prove schedule independence while direct-row permutations remain observable when the Reference makes them observable. Named mutations invert each propagation equation, append before recursion, join isMeta, suppress an upgrade replay, or conflate server IR load with phases, and each must be killed. Operation counts prove bounded work in modules plus rows plus monotone state upgrades and witness budget. Cross-check small pinned source/real artifact fixtures only at integration layer fln-amv.9.4. Run scoped format, all fln-env tests, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and parent checks.

No plain DFS that ignores flags, early visited set that suppresses upgrades, traversal-order diagnostics, boolean accepted/rejected collapse for partial data, invented isMeta join, server-phase conflation, pointer-only stale cache key, cycle acceptance, or hidden decoder dependency.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every pinned equation, global-level/IR distinction, postorder/first-edge behavior, deterministic query, row-indexed witness, typed complete/incomplete/inconclusive/invalid outcome, independent-model comparison, metamorphic schedule proof, named mutation, and operation bound above is implemented and green. fln-amv.9.4 retains the real-fixture/no-mock E2E obligation. No simplified reachability substitute or deferred rule satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Effective import computation matches every pinned importModulesCore equation, the five-element exposure lattice, exported/server/private global levels, runtime/comptime/all phases, explicit root-export override, first-discovery isMeta retention, server IR-request versus phase distinction, exact five-field upgrade condition, replay-on-upgrade, recursive postorder, direct-row order, and duplicate work inputs. 2. Reports preserve exact effective state, Reference discovery sequence, canonical transitive/reverse queries, exact reverse rows, row-indexed component witnesses, deterministic operation/resource facts, and distinct Complete, Incomplete, Inconclusive, Invalid, and InternalFault outcomes. Missing/non-module/malformed/cancel/resource/invariant cases never collapse, panic, or silently repair. 3. An independently implemented pinned-equation model and named unit/property/metamorphic tests cover every Import flag combination, exposure/phase join, root override, late upgrade, duplicate/diamond/missing cases, server and meta behavior, witness provenance, reverse queries, generated DAGs, construction permutations, thread partitions, cancellation, every resource bound, and bounded dense-work counts. Individually discriminating mutations invert each propagation equation/order/replay/server/isMeta rule and are killed with schema-versioned structured evidence. 4. No cache is added without durable graph identity and the complete request/profile key. Real artifact availability, decoder cross-checks, and the no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E with detailed schema-versioned logs remain mandatory in fln-amv.9.4 and block epic closure; no mock-only substitute or simplified DFS satisfies the integration obligation. 5. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, warnings-denied Clippy, UBS under the documented user-required scanner exclusion, and applicable parent/contract gates are green.
