title: fln-env: bind module contributions, provenance roots, and invalidation
description:
Completion epic for module contribution provenance and invalidation over the persistent module graph from fln-amv.9.1 and executable extension checkpoints from fln-amv.7. No scope is removed: every module accounts for imported declarations, extra generated declarations, and exact ordered extension contributions; forward and reverse provenance are one immutable truth; partial and opaque completeness remain honest; import application is atomic; graph identity remains separate from the trusted environment logical root; invalidation is deterministic, bounded, witnessed, and conservative at opaque boundaries; snapshots, paired checkpoints, restores, and provenance-state merge plans preserve identity and sharing; conflicting origins are never last-writer-wins.

The design intentionally separates identity from application payload. The canonical schema/root stores module topology, declaration names, extension entry ordinals/digests, evidence, and completeness without copying payload bytes. Atomic application therefore consumes a separately Arc-backed payload transaction carrying actual ConstantInfo values and raw extension entries, and preflights a one-to-one value/name/digest/range/artifact/epoch binding before publishing the combined Environment plus provenance/index state. Digest equality never replaces actual value equality. Metadata-only provenance cannot change logical_root; declaration/extension semantic publication changes logical_root only through existing Environment canonical rules. Every downstream checkpoint, invalidation request, restore, and merge plan binds exact logical and provenance root preconditions.

The work is decomposed into a shared dependency spine followed by two parallel implementation branches: franken_lean-module-provenance-schema-cxn defines the versioned identity schema, dedicated hash domain, canonical root, resource model, and metadata-only logical-root noninterference; franken_lean-module-provenance-atomic-apply-as7 defines the payload transaction and preflighted all-or-nothing combined state plus bidirectional indexes; franken_lean-module-provenance-invalidation-1qv implements typed bounded module cones and canonical witnesses for artifact, topology, declaration, extension, missing, collision, and opaque changes with FL-INV-07 authority rules; franken_lean-module-provenance-lifecycle-vk2 integrates O(1) snapshots, paired provenance checkpoints, atomic restore, and the revalidated provenance-state merge plan consumed later by fln-amv.11. Schema freezes first, atomic apply freezes second, then invalidation and lifecycle proceed independently from the same committed-state contract. This parent directly joins both branches; neither can close or substitute for the other. Real decoded-fixture and shared no-mock E2E coverage remains in fln-amv.9.4, avoiding duplicate fixture lanes.

Acceptance requires every child closed with its full unit, property/model, negative/collision/resource/cancellation, schedule-metamorphic, sharing/allocation, mutation, schema-versioned logging, fln-env/fln-hash/domain-registry, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor evidence. The trusted logical root remains declarations plus extension deltas plus options; ModuleGraph/Provenance root is an explicit additional future Ledger key and receipt input, never operational metadata and never silently folded into trusted identity. No disconnected second truth, flattened opaque payloads, name-or-digest-only application, inferred ownership, partial publication, nondeterministic traversal, hidden logical-root change, advisory-only lifecycle, unpaired checkpoint provenance, global merge duplication, authoritative inconclusive result, or deferred listed feature satisfies this epic.

## Acceptance Criteria

All four dependency-ordered children are closed with every retained original feature plus the explicit identity/payload binding, combined committed state, logical/provenance root transition law, FL-INV-07 authority handling, paired checkpoint, and merge-plan contracts verified. The parent closes only after canonical separate identity, actual-value atomic bidirectional provenance, bounded witnessed invalidation, and snapshot/checkpoint/restore/provenance-plan behavior are complete and all fln-env/fln-hash/domain, model, mutation, collision, sharing, fmt, clippy, and UBS gates are green. fln-amv.9.4 retains the real decoded-fixture/no-mock matrix. No partial child closure or deferred listed feature satisfies this epic.
acceptance_criteria: All four dependency-ordered children must close with canonical separate identity, exact Arc-backed payload binding and atomic combined publication, bidirectional indexes, scenario-correct logical/provenance roots, bounded witnessed FL-INV-07-safe invalidation, paired snapshot/checkpoint/restore, and the revalidated provenance merge plan. Every child and parent unit/model/mutation/collision/resource/cancellation/sharing/fmt/clippy/UBS/domain gate is mandatory; fln-amv.9.4 retains the real no-mock matrix and must prove positive application, typed failure with zero partial publication, exact governed-input restoration, and a subsequent clean recovery run before this parent can close. The parent join must retain and independently validate the child evidence references without duplicating or weakening their detail rows. Partial closure, missing recovery evidence, or deferred scope fails this epic.

title: fln-kernel K1: defeq-eta slice — KR-903 structure eta + KR-315 unit-like eta (residual eliminator-family probe)
description: Follow-up to franken_lean-5p2. After the recursor-reduction slice, the Init.Prelude replay still false-rejects 343 declarations; the dominant family (244 eliminators) is concentrated in typeclass-structure casesOn/recOn/noConfusionType/below (e.g. Membership.casesOn, HDiv.noConfusionType, Lean.Name.below), all TypeMismatch/DefinitionTypeMismatch. HYPOTHESIS (typed, unverified): these need KR-903 structure eta in defeq (try_eta_struct_core: t ≟ mk as fs for a one-constructor, non-recursive, index-free structure holds when the types agree and every field fi is defeq to t.i, both directions) and possibly KR-315 unit-like eta (two terms of the same one-constructor zero-field structure type are defeq when their types are). FIRST TASK: root-cause ONE case from each residual sub-family from the real olean (decode Membership.casesOn, replay with instrumentation, capture the exact defeq query that fails) — no implementation before the gap is evidence-anchored; if the probe refutes the KR-903 hypothesis, re-triage before coding. Implement per pin anchor vendor/lean4-src/src/kernel/type_checker.cpp:793 (try_eta_struct_core) and :1044 (is_def_eq_unit_like), wired into the is_def_eq ladder at the pin's position. Verification: mutation-killed unit tests per rule branch (eta disabled, one-direction-only, Prop-structure exclusion if pin has one, wrong-field-index), kernel replay re-run with before/after census recorded (expect the 244-family to convert if hypothesis holds), fmt/clippy/UBS/structure-guard green, covenant intact.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Real decoded residual probes from at least one member of every identified family establish the actual first divergent defeq query before implementation; any refuted hypothesis is recorded and the bead is re-triaged rather than forced. 2. The post-whnf and lazy-delta quick-defeq ladder, frontier-transitive replay dependencies, KR-903 structure eta, and KR-315 unit-like eta match the pinned contract and preserve every no-fire/over-acceptance guard. 3. Named public-authority unit and mutation tests cover decoded Sort/Pi shapes, one- and two-direction eta, wrong-field indices, type mismatch, recursive/indexed/non-structure exclusions, unit-like eligibility, and over-acceptance seeds; every planted mutant is killed. 4. The real no-mock kernel replay records exact before/after census, residual-family reclassification, zero inconclusive regression, failure artifacts, and clean recovery in schema-versioned NDJSON with fixture/hash, contract rule, budget, expected/actual verdict and diagnostic, consumption, artifact, cleanup, and final state. 5. Determinism assertions compare semantic replay output for repeated identical inputs while excluding only declared monotonic timing/path fields. fln-kernel tests, replay, format, warnings-denied Clippy, UBS, and the kernel covenant/structure guard are green.
close_reason: Landed as three commits (6fbdb26, 538dff7, 1efcf23). The mandated probe REFUTED the KR-903 hypothesis for the 244-member residual family and uncovered two different defects: (1) KERNEL: the defeq ladder ran its head rules (KR-301/302/303) only on the un-reduced pair — pairs becoming Sort≟Sort (equivalent-not-identical levels) or Pi≟Pi only after beta/delta fell through and false-rejected; fixed pin-shaped via quick_def_eq_rules re-run after whnf_core and lazy delta (3 reproduction tests from decoded shapes; mutants M8/M9 killed). (2) HARNESS: replay topo edges ignored references reachable only through frontier declaration types (env outParam = ABSENT at Membership.casesOn); fixed with frontier-transitive dependency expansion. Census across the arc: 1412/1755 → 1744/1755 accepted, 343 → 11 rejected, 0 Inconclusive. KR-903/KR-315 then implemented per pin anchors as ladder-parity completeness (49/49 tests, mutants M10-M13 killed incl. two over-acceptance gate seeds) with the honest note that Prelude shows zero demand for them. All 11 residuals decoded and reassigned to franken_lean-irm (OfNat.ofNat..lit..≟Nat.zero for 6 match auxiliaries; literal arithmetic for 5 ofNatLT/ofNatAux). Probe lanes FLN_REPLAY_PROBE / FLN_REPLAY_PROBE_ENV + bounded expected/actual kernel mismatch messages are permanent triage infrastructure.

title: W3: Certificates, receipts, transparency log — auditable checking
description:
Implement certificates, receipts, and the transparency log (§8.6): every checked declaration can emit a certificate — the compressed, structure-shared elaborated term DAG plus reduction hints that make independent rechecking cheap (unfold order, literal-arithmetic transcripts so a tiny verifier replays accelerated ops without its own bignum trust). Certificates attach to olean-next records, feed fln export (speaking the community established export format for cross-prover checkers), and may ship a warm defeq-cache cartridge (memoized reduction history — third-party rechecking becomes verification rather than search).

The certificate law (D20): the checker must be SIMPLER than recomputation, must reject unknown versions, and must fall back to recomputation on any failure — an accelerator, never a wider TCB. Receipts bind kernel + checker build hashes, the consensus policy, fuel profiles, and both verdicts. Attested receipts append to a transparency log (Merkle Mountain Range over receipt roots, signed checkpoints): third parties verify inclusion and consistency without trusting the logger; equivocation is detectable — "checked" becomes an artifact anyone can audit, not a verb someone witnessed.

Acceptance: certificate emit + independent replay on the corpus with the checker measurably simpler (line count, dependency count) than the engines; fln export output accepted by at least one external checker; MMR inclusion/consistency proofs verified by an independent implementation of the verifier side; PG-1b receipt-emission overhead <= 1 ms/decl amortized. Tests: certificate-version rejection fixtures; corrupt-certificate fallback-to-recompute fixtures; log equivocation detection test (forked checkpoint must be caught).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this bead owns OQ-8 and OQ-13. Freeze the versioned certificate/export schema and its alignment with at least one existing cross-prover checker; separately specify the warm defeq-cache cartridge schema, admissible hints, size/resource bounds, receipt attachment policy, version rejection, privacy/redaction rules, and recomputation fallback. Update the decision log and add compatibility/tamper/resource fixtures for both formats.
acceptance_criteria: 1. OQ-8 is resolved before certificate/export freeze by a versioned reviewed format decision that specifies canonical declaration-certificate framing, structure sharing, replay hints, version negotiation, resource bounds, semantic and byte identities, olean-next binding, and row-by-row alignment or explicit mapping to existing cross-prover checker formats. A real external checker round-trip validates the chosen export; unknown or lossy mappings block the decision. 2. OQ-13 is resolved jointly with K2 by a separate versioned warm defeq-cache cartridge schema and receipt-attachment policy: exact key domain, environment/transparency/epoch/build binding, entry and expanded-weight limits, validation, optionality, privacy/portability, cacheability, expiry, and safe discard/recompute behavior are explicit. Cartridges are accelerators only and cannot carry verdict authority. 3. Emit versioned, structure-shared declaration certificates containing the elaborated term DAG and replay hints, optionally including a conforming warm cartridge; bind certificates to olean-next records and support the reviewed community export format without expanding trusted authority. 4. The independent checker rejects unknown versions and malformed, truncated, non-canonical, mismatched-hash, stale, foreign-build, or over-budget certificates/cartridges. Any certificate or cartridge failure falls back to ordinary recomputation with the same verdict, never acceptance or rejection by the accelerator alone; the verifier remains measurably simpler than recomputation by recorded KLOC and dependency-closure evidence. 5. Receipts bind declaration/environment roots, kernel and checker build hashes, consensus policy, epoch/mode/profile/fuel, both verdicts, certificate version, optional cartridge identity/disposition, and evidence ids. Attested receipt roots append to an MMR transparency log with independently verifiable inclusion and consistency proofs, signed checkpoints, and detectable fork/equivocation. 6. Named unit/property/model/mutation suites cover certificate and cartridge encode/decode/replay, structure sharing, every OQ-8 mapping, version rejection, corrupt/stale/foreign hint and cache fallback, deterministic export, receipt attachment/detachment, MMR append/inclusion/consistency, fork detection, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and failure atomicity. Planted certificate-trust, cartridge-authority, stale-key, receipt-rebinding, and checkpoint-fork mutants must be killed. 7. A shared no-mock E2E lane checks a real corpus declaration, replays its certificate independently, submits export output to at least one real external checker, attaches and discards a real K2 cartridge with identical verdict, verifies inclusion and consistency from a fresh verifier process, demonstrates corrupt-certificate/cartridge recomputation and fork detection, and proves clean recovery. Human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON record ids, OQ-8/OQ-13 decisions, exact redacted argv/cwd, pin/build hashes, declaration/environment/certificate/cartridge/receipt/log roots, versions, expected/actual verdicts, fallback reason, proof/checkpoint data, resources/timings/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state. 8. Reproducible benchmark evidence on the declared corpus/machine shows PG-1b receipt emission at no more than 1 ms per declaration amortized and reports distributions and sampling method. Close evidence archives both reviewed decisions, manifests, certificates, exports, cartridges, receipts, checkpoints, proofs, mutant results, benchmark data, and all named test/E2E artifacts.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W6: Synod instance engine — exact semantics, SearchCards, keyed negative caches
description:
Implement Synod, the instance engine (§10.4): the Reference semantics — priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, default instances, tabling/caching behavior, the results the ecosystem diamond structures depend on — behind a rebuilt engine: instance search compiled to an indexed relational form (discrimination-automaton candidate retrieval, subsumption-checked tabling, cycle detection with franken_networkx diagnostics), with provenance recorded per resolution: every attempted instance, why it failed (unification residue, priority loss, depth), as a Palimpsest tree.

Every search emits a SearchCard (explored candidates, priorities, unification residues, cycle decisions, cache hits, selection) — provenance and negative-cache key in one artifact, with negative entries valid ONLY under their exact dependency key (instance-table identity, transparency, options, local-context fingerprint). Faithful mode pins search order to the Reference; sound mode may reorder only within proven-confluent regions (same result, better time), CGSE-registered. Name resolution, coercion graphs, and discrimination indexes are immutable content-addressed derivations of the environment commit, incrementally maintained and Ledger-cached — faithful iteration order preserved even where internals are faster.

Acceptance: instance-search outcomes match the oracle across the Corpus instance-trace corpus (instrumented-oracle instance traces); SearchCards complete per perturbation; the sound-mode "why is there no instance" answer (§15.3) renders from this engine exhaust with no second implementation. Tests: golden instance-trace replay; diamond/priority fixtures; negative-cache validity mutation tests (a stale negative entry must be impossible under the dependency key); cycle-diagnostic fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: Synod preserves pinned instance priorities, local/global ordering, outParam/semiOutParam, defaults, tabling, cycles, depth, transparency, failure and diagnostic behavior. Candidate retrieval/indexing and tabling are immutable content-addressed derivations but faithful iteration/selection matches Reference traces; sound-mode reordering occurs only in registered proven-confluent regions. Every search emits a complete SearchCard with candidates, priorities, residues, failures, cycles, cache facts and selection plus provenance tree. Negative entries bind exact instance-table identity, transparency, options, local-context fingerprint, epoch and relevant dependencies; stale or incomplete keys cannot hit. Name resolution, coercion graphs and discrimination indexes share the same environment truth, and why-no-instance renders from recorded exhaust rather than a second engine. Unit/property/model/trace/metamorphic/mutation suites cover diamonds, priorities, defaults, cycles, local shadowing, cache keys, incremental changes, budgets/cancellation and recovery; named stale-negative, candidate-drop/reorder, wrong-priority, false-cycle, incomplete-card and unsound-reorder mutants are killed. Real no-mock Corpus instance E2E replays golden traces, perturbs tables/contexts/options, compares outcomes/diagnostics, restores, and retains strict SearchCard/key/root/final-state bundles. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, trace/Mirror/perturbation/mutation lanes pass. No stale negative cache, hidden second resolver, faithful search reorder, incomplete provenance, mock-only oracle, guessed instance, or deferred listed semantics satisfies closure.

title: W1: Tribunal bootstrap — epoch lab, differential harness, Parity Ledger schema
description:
Bootstrap the Tribunal (fln-conformance, §18) FIRST — before the subsystems it will judge — so every workstream is born differential. The Reference runs INSIDE the harness as the standing differential oracle, forever (Oracle-Only Law capacity 1).

Scope: the epoch laboratory (official release archives with hashes; executable/symbol/header inventories; CLI and LSP transcripts; artifact fixtures; quirk/defect ledgers; environment-normalization recipes; exact reproduction commands; per platform; immutable once published — discoveries create reviewed hashed revisions); the oracle-precedence ladder implemented as data (core theory > observed binary behavior > sources/tests > stable downstream dependence > documented divergence > unclassified-blocks); comparison classes as versioned normalizer code with fixtures (a normalizer may strip only declared-nonsemantic fields and can never discard an error body to pass); the corpus family scaffolding C0-C9; upstream test-suite import at the pin with expected-outcome mapping; the Parity Ledger schema (row-per-symbol: surface, symbol, kind, semantic status, L-level, mode, oracle kind, comparison class, fixtures, determinism class, claim state, freshness) with the regeneration + regression CI job; the ORACLE_FALLBACK poison-tag machinery and the release compile-out check (§18.10, D8).

Acceptance: a Reference-vs-Reference smoke differential runs green end-to-end (proves harness plumbing); the Parity Ledger generates and the dashboard aggregates; C1 corpus imported. Tests: the harness tests itself via seeded-divergence fixtures (a planted diff must be detected and triaged, never normalized away).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — the Tribunal remains the first substantive subsystem, but it starts only after the authoritative SUITE.lock/closure audit and shared verification/logging contract exist. This avoids an unpinned oracle lab and a parallel ad-hoc test runner while still allowing contract extraction, fln-core, fln-hash, and platform work to proceed in parallel after workspace scaffolding.
acceptance_criteria: The Tribunal bootstrap is complete only when one pin- and platform-bound epoch laboratory records cryptographic archive/binary/tree identities, executable plus symbol/header inventories, CLI and LSP transcripts, artifact fixtures, quirk/defect ledgers, environment-normalization facts, exact reproduction commands, and explicit expected-outcome mappings; all C0-C9 corpus families are schema-defined and C1 is imported with self-containment and exclusion classifications. Generation occurs in a new scratch snapshot under fixed locale/timezone/path normalization, validates completeness before publication, never overwrites an existing epoch, and treats published labs as check-only; discoveries create reviewed hashed revisions. Oracle precedence, oracle authority, comparison class, normalizer id/version, determinism class, claim state, mode, L-level, and freshness are typed closed vocabularies. Every Parity Ledger row parses against those types, names real fixtures, remains at an evidence-honest level, and rejects missing, duplicate, stale, unknown, path-escaping, or overclaiming rows. Versioned normalizers may remove only declared nonsemantic fields, preserve all errors and semantic bytes, and are covered by metamorphic laws plus planted divergences at line, sub-line, diagnostic, exit, and artifact levels. ORACLE_FALLBACK poison is traceable in development and proven absent from release source and built artifacts across every reviewed Cargo workspace member and every compiled target family, including tools, build scripts, examples, benches, tests, and feature combinations; planted leaks in each location are detected. The real Reference-vs-Reference no-mock E2E runs through the shared causal supervisor and evidence contract, proves twice-run byte identity and baseline agreement, detects each named planted divergence with exact first-divergence evidence, exercises cancellation/resource/fault refusal where supported, restores exact input bytes, and finishes with a clean recovery. Human logs and strictly validated schema-versioned NDJSON retain run/scenario/step ids, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/cache/host facts, commands, canonical roots, expected versus actual values, bounded separate stdout/stderr artifacts, resource/process outcome, cleanup, and final state; manifests and committed bundles validate independently. The Parity Ledger regeneration/regression job, epoch checks, Reference smoke differential, poison compile-out/artifact scan, dashboard aggregation, scripts/check.sh, unit/property/mutation suites, cargo test, clippy with warnings denied, fmt, and UBS are permanent CI obligations and green from a cold checkout. No ad-hoc logger, mutable published epoch, source-regex census substitute, mock-only Reference, normalized-away error, unclassified divergence, hidden exclusion, skipped platform presented as passed, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Bootstrap implementation started by session agent (claim blocked by rur/xwf held elsewhere). Surface: crates/fln-conformance, scripts/tribunal/, tribunal/epochs/, ci/PARITY_LEDGER.txt.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Early read-only acceptance review of the active bootstrap: (1) ledger::validate_fixtures does root.join(fixture).exists() without rejecting absolute paths, .. traversal, symlink escape, or non-regular targets, so a purported repo-relative evidence row can cite arbitrary host files. Enforce lexical and canonical confinement to the workspace and retain source line numbers for exact diagnostics. (2) Normalizer::paths_v1 accepts arbitrary strings and globally replaces them. A caller can pass error, x, or an entire diagnostic body; the three substring markers only catch some deletions and one surviving marker can mask another discarded diagnostic. Validate absolute path prefixes and normalize structured path fields/boundaries, with multiple-error/warning/unsolved-goal mutation tests. (3) OracleFallback::use_poisoned_diagnosis_value_gate_inert returns raw T, structurally laundering away the poison tag; a method name cannot enforce gate/cache inertness. Keep the taint in the return type or expose diagnostic-only operations that cannot yield an unpoisoned product. Also, the current L0-no-fixture test comment expects acceptance but asserts parse error; cargo test should catch that transient inconsistency.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Second acceptance pass on the newly added ledger/tests: the real ledger currently promotes p8a observables to L2 OBSERVED even though p8a is open and its cited core_observables lane has known unbounded-Nat/Int, corruption-validation, and corpus-totality gaps (p8a comment 9). Plan 4.2 defines L2 as a gated corpus pass with exclusions explicit; either keep these rows at honest L0/L1/TARGETED or record explicit exclusions and only advance them when the owning evidence closes. The String.hash row also cites BLAKE3 vectors, which are fln-hash identity evidence rather than evidence for Lean String.hash. Separately, poison_compile_out::the_scanner_detects_a_planted_leak only calls str.contains on a literal; it does not exercise collect_rs_files or the real violation scanner, so a broken traversal survives the planted test. Factor the scanner and seed a real isolated source tree. The cfg!(feature) default test proves only the test build omitted the feature, not yet the promised release-artifact absence check.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Epoch-lab/E2E acceptance review: gen_epoch_manifest.sh generate mode mkdir -p plus redirects directly into an existing published epoch, so it can overwrite an allegedly immutable lab and leave stale transcript files when the slice changes. Generate in a new scratch snapshot, validate completeness, then require an explicit reviewed first publication; existing epochs should be check-only. Set LC_ALL/TZ for the whole generator so glob/tool inventory ordering is not inherited-locale dependent. The comment claims a self-contained slice, but select_slice only chooses the ten smallest files and performs no self-containment classification. More importantly, ref_vs_ref.sh invents a minimal fln-e2e/1 logger instead of extending the shared fln.e2e/2 evidence family: early failures have no terminal record or committed manifest/bundle; JSON strings are printf-unescaped; there are no canonical input roots, epoch/mode/profile/thread/seed/cache facts, per-step supervisor/resource/exit artifacts, bounded output, cancellation/recovery, or failure-atomic final-state proof. Under rur and this bead mandatory DoD, a passing diff alone cannot close the E2E claim; route the oracle commands through the shared supervisor/evidence contract and validate/commit the bundle.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Post-commit acceptance audit of 06ba84b (bead correctly remains open): (1) check.sh only adds gen_epoch_manifest.sh/ref_vs_ref.sh to governed inputs and shellcheck; neither `gen_epoch_manifest.sh --check` nor `ref_vs_ref.sh` is executed by scripts/check.sh or .github/workflows/ci.yml, so the required regeneration/regression CI job and permanent Reference-vs-Reference obligation do not exist. (2) Ledger rows store oracle_kind and comparison_class as unchecked strings, disconnected from OracleAuthority/ComparisonClass/NormalizerId; typos or invented authorities/normalizers pass the gate. Make these parsed typed/versioned values and reject unknown entries. (3) The committed epoch lab has executable hashes and 10 plain-invocation transcripts, but the bead also mandates symbol/header inventories, CLI and LSP transcripts, artifact fixtures, explicit quirk/defect ledgers, per-platform labs, C0-C9 scaffolding, and upstream expected-outcome mapping. The comment classifying those as follow-on cannot close this bead because its acceptance explicitly forbids deferring or narrowing listed scope. Earlier comments 27-29/33 also remain unresolved (fixture escape, arbitrary normalizer prefixes, poison laundering, overclaimed L2 rows, fake scanner mutation, mutable published epoch, and ad-hoc unbundled E2E).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Review sweep (Claude Fable session): two reporting defects fixed in committed fln-conformance code. (1) ledger.rs validate_fixtures rendered the row INDEX as 'PARITY_LEDGER.txt:{line}' — a missing-fixture error pointed at the wrong line for any ledger with comments/blank lines. Row now carries its 1-based source line and validate_fixtures reports it (regression: ghost-fixture error asserts line 2). (2) normalize.rs compare(): when transcripts differed only below line granularity (e.g. trailing newline), the finding read 'length divergence: ours N lines, oracle N lines' with equal N — self-contradictory triage output. Now reports 'sub-line divergence (line endings or trailing bytes): ours X bytes, oracle Y bytes' (regression added). title: W1: Evidence-runner bootstrap — causal supervision, durable bundles, sealed toolchain
description:
Extract the trustworthy process/evidence substrate that fln-8mj needs in order to close without making the entire permanent quality-system bead an operational prerequisite. This bead is a strict prerequisite slice of franken_lean-rur, not a replacement: RUR retains the machine-checked verification registry for every implementation bead, the growing scenario family, permanent Tribunal/CI obligations, archive policy, and all future subsystem integration.

Implement the Linux evidence runner and the three bootstrap wrappers as a coherent trusted result boundary: target-before-exec causal containment; exact process identity; PID-reuse-safe signalling; bounded setup, execution, cancellation, cleanup, and finalization; one typed terminal outcome; durable write-once bundle publication and read-only independent validation; an evidence envelope beginning immediately after fresh artifact-directory creation; per-attempt isolated build state; and exact locked-toolchain execution sealed against ambient Cargo/rustc configuration.

The initial consumers are scripts/check.sh, scripts/e2e/structure_gate.sh, and scripts/e2e/closure_audit.sh. They must emit concise human logs plus bounded schema-versioned NDJSON and independently validatable bundles for success, semantic failure, unexpected failure, cancellation, resource exhaustion, setup fault, concurrent source drift, and internal fault. Release-level claims use real processes, filesystems, compiler, and structure-guard binaries; mocks may support unit tests but never close this bead.

## Acceptance Criteria

All behaviors and boundaries in this description, Design, and the structured Acceptance Criteria field are mandatory. The bead closes only with the named unit, fault, stress, hostile-environment, full-gate, and no-mock E2E evidence; no case may be silently deferred, weakened to a mock, or promoted from an inconclusive/internal result.
design:
The supervisor launches a tiny stopped-exec gate as the actual session leader and future target PID. The child arms parent-death containment, publishes or exposes an identity-bound inert T/t state, and cannot execute user argv until the parent has verified direct parentage, session/process-group identity, start ticks, pidfd, and readiness. Only then does the parent resume the same PID into exec. Setup/readiness has a distinct budget and execution timing begins after successful release. Fast leader exit, detached descendants, PID churn/reuse, pidfd exhaustion, lost-wakeup ordering, never-stops, stop-then-dies, and guardian failure all fail closed without signalling unrelated processes.

Shell-to-guardian and finalizer launches use the same stopped-child admission law. HUP/INT/TERM transition atomically into one finalization handler. Finalization is bounded and mode-aware, records any cleanup uncertainty, and never promotes Inconclusive or InternalFault. Bundle decisions are durable and write-once; validate-bundle is side-effect-free, while a separate explicitly named adoption operation may recover a valid pre-marker decision using exclusive marker publication, fsync through the artifact-root ancestor chain, and full revalidation. Concurrent adoption is idempotent and cancellation cannot be adopted as pass.

The evidence envelope starts once a fresh artifact directory is atomically accepted. Inventory, vendor binding, governed-root hashing, host/argv capture, source snapshotting, compiler-environment sealing, and initial event creation all run under the terminal/finalizer state machine. The compiler lane resolves SUITE.lock before repository-controlled compilation, invokes the exact rustup channel, isolates Cargo home and target directories without deleting user caches, and excludes or rejects rustflags, encoded rustflags, rustc/wrapper overrides, alternate toolchain selection, target-specific rustflags, and repository/ancestor/Cargo-home configuration. Evidence records the effective compiler path/version/commit and admitted environment.
acceptance_criteria:
All cases have deterministic unit tests and real CLI E2E scripts with detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON logging. The built-in self-test and a separate source-stable external matrix run at least 100 iterations each for immediate exit 0, registered semantic nonzero, unexpected nonzero, and signal termination with zero false internal faults, zero hangs, exact classifications, and zero survivors. Named tests cover delayed-stop/early-CONT ordering, never-stops, stop-then-dies, detached descendants, PID reuse/churn, pidfd exhaustion, guardian failure, setup timeout versus execution timeout, output/resource budgets, and cancellation storms.

Deterministic HUP/INT/TERM injection at spawn, readiness, running, terminal publication, finalizer entry, decision write, marker link, and post-fsync yields exactly one schema-valid terminal event and durable bundle matching the linearization point. Relative and absolute publication paths terminate identically; escaping/reused paths fail before mutation; validate never creates files; adoption is crash-recoverable and concurrently idempotent. Faults at every step from artifact-directory creation through first stage readiness, including mutation during initial hashing, still finalize a typed durable partial bundle.

A hostile compiler-environment matrix injects cap-lints allow via ordinary/encoded/target rustflags, fake rustc and workspace wrappers, alternate toolchain, and repository/ancestor/Cargo-home config. Each is rejected before repository compilation or proven absent from the effective compiler argv, no hostile marker executes, and a clean recovery follows. Finally, one stable-source scripts/check.sh run plus both real no-mock structural E2Es pass, their committed bundles validate independently, logs are bounded and secret-redacted, stderr is retained, exact argv/cwd/hashes/timings/resources/exit/signal/cleanup/final state are present, and all named artifacts are recorded in bead close evidence.
notes:
This bead exists to remove the operational dependency cycle between fln-8mj and franken_lean-rur. It may close only the reusable evidence-runner/bootstrap substrate. It does not close or narrow RUR, which remains responsible for the verification-manifest schema, coverage registration across every implementation bead, archive/retention policy, permanent CI integration, and the expanding Tribunal/E2E scenario system.

MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead own Tests and Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane with real compiler, processes, filesystems, artifacts, and wrappers. A mock may support a unit test but may not close a gate or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, and gate ids; exact argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected versus actual; monotonic start/end/duration; setup/execution/cancellation/cleanup/finalization budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; first-divergence data; publication linearization point; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI and closeout: archive logs, fixtures, stress results, hostile-environment results, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, the bead-specific unit/property/mutation/fault/stress suites, both shared no-mock structural E2E scenarios, and independent validation of every named closing bundle. Close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W3: fln check-olean — the Independent Judge CLI and the G1 evidence bundle
description:
Ship fln check-olean — the Independent Judge (§8.6, G1): the standalone auditor that re-checks an entire olean set (all of mathlib in minutes) on a kernel that shares no code, no compiler, and no bignum with the thing it is checking — the program FIRST public artifact, shipped before FrankenLean has elaborated a single file.

Composition: Grimoire read (full-recheck validation level) + Crucible K1/K2 + fln-checker per consensus policy + receipts + optional certificate emission. CLI contract: stable exit codes, --json output (versioned schema), per-declaration verdicts with stable error classes, a summary receipt binding the input set hash, engine build hashes, policy, and totals; --receipts writes the receipt set + transparency-log append. Performance: PG-1 — full mathlib olean set <= 5 min on the 32-core profile, <= 25 min single-thread.

This bead includes the G1 gate evidence assembly: zero acceptance divergence against the Reference toolchain across the full mathlib olean set at the pin, every divergence (if any) triaged under the §8.2 oracle-or-spec-investigation-first law. Acceptance: G1 declared with the evidence bundle (run logs, receipts, Parity Ledger rows, PG-1 numbers on the baseline machine). Tests: CLI transcript fixtures (exit codes, --json schema); interrupted-run drills (partial output never masquerades as complete); determinism — two runs produce identical receipts.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: fln check-olean is a standalone air-gap-capable auditor composed of full-recheck Grimoire decode, K1/K2, fln-checker consensus, receipts, optional certificates, and transparency append; it never invokes the Reference or another Lean implementation at runtime. Human and versioned robot interfaces have stable documented exit codes and per-declaration records with input/declaration/root hashes, verdict/error/resource class, engines/builds/fuel/policy, trace/evidence links, and explicit complete/incomplete run status. The summary receipt binds the exact manifest-complete input-set hash, epoch, modes, policy, engine builds, totals and result; --receipts atomically writes a complete set and log entry. Interrupted, cancelled, resource-exhausted, disk-full, corrupted, or disagreement runs cannot masquerade as complete, publish partial authority, or collapse Inconclusive. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover CLI parsing/rendering, schema/exit mapping, manifest ordering, receipt/certificate tamper, consensus outcomes, partial output, retry/idempotence, budgets and recovery; named skipped-file, false-summary, forged-receipt, disagreement-outvote, and interrupted-success mutants are killed. Real no-mock G1 E2E rechecks every pinned mathlib olean with zero acceptance divergence, runs 1/8/32-thread determinism, plants corruption/disagreement/interruption, restores, and retains strict per-row/summary/receipt/transparency/final-state bundles. PG-1 is no more than 5 minutes on the 32-core profile and 25 minutes single-thread with raw host/cache/sample evidence. All dependencies, G1 assembly, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, kernel/codec/differential/mutation/fault/platform/performance lanes pass. No sampled set presented as all mathlib, aggregate-only verdict, partial run presented complete, runtime Reference fallback, mock-only corpus, unstable robot schema, waived divergence, or deferred listed CLI/evidence feature satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W7: Anvil & Verdict — simp core, rewrite indexes, owned SAT, grind
description:
Workstream W7 "Anvil & Verdict" (§12): rewrite and decision engines under the single constitutional rule FL-INV-06 — every engine is untrusted; every output enters the environment only through a kernel-checked artifact. That rule buys unlimited freedom below it.

Scope: simp faithful core (simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree retrieval, congruence interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors) implemented natively end-to-end behind obs-class Mirror facade rows, fidelity-tested against Corpus simp-heavy files + instrumented-oracle rewrite traces; compiled per-library rewrite indexes shipped as content-addressed Ledger artifacts (staleness impossible by construction); norm_num/omega native cores (fln-bignum-backed, certificate-producing, kernel-replayed); Verdict — owned CDCL SAT (watched literals, VSIDS/CHB, modest honest inprocessing) with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker so bv_decide sheds the external CaDiCaL TCB (PG-9: 100% agreement, wall <= 2x Reference at G5, ratcheting); grind reimplemented natively (congruence closure, e-matching, theory hooks) entering at L1/L2 and ratcheting to L3 on the grind corpus by G4 — its semantics still move upstream, the census pins the coupling per epoch; e-graph saturation lane (frontier; honest scoping — conditional rewrites and binder-crossing rules restrict the eligible subset; completeness fallback to the faithful core; PG-A measured, not assumed); speculative tactic portfolios (frontier). New lanes promote only by shadow-run matrix protocol (§12.6/§18.9) with auto-demotion on breach.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — full W7 closure includes franken_lean-engine-backed-tactic-integration-c90. Engine implementations are not user-compatible merely because direct APIs pass; they execute through the production Mirror facade and Golem-hosted tactic path, preserve TacticM state and InfoTree behavior, and kernel-check every result. Ledger publication of compiled rewrite indexes remains the W8 integration owned by fln-publish-rewrite-indexes-gca.

Stage topology: G4 consumes the production engine-backed integration needed by full mathlib, including faithful simp, certificate-backed arithmetic/Verdict, and grind at its required L-level. The full W7 epic additionally retains frontier e-graph and tactic-portfolio shadow promotion and therefore blocks G6 rather than delaying G4. No frontier feature is dropped, and no G4 core engine claim is deferred to G6.
acceptance_criteria: W7 closes only when every Anvil/Verdict engine remains untrusted and no result enters an environment without a complete kernel-checked artifact under FL-INV-06. Faithful simp matches the pinned simp-set, dsimp/simp-only, discrimination retrieval, congruence, firing order, proof term, diagnostic, and trace behavior on the full registered corpus. Versioned compiled rewrite indexes are deterministic, content-addressed, root-bound, stale-proof, published through the owning W8 integration, and fall back to faithful recomputation. norm_num and omega emit compact kernel-replayable certificates over owned arithmetic. Verdict provides owned CDCL, proof logging, and an independently implemented checker; bv_decide has 100 percent corpus agreement and PG-9 evidence. Native grind reaches the required L3 rows by G4 with epoch-pinned semantics. Frontier e-graph saturation and tactic portfolios implement the full scoped feature set, including conditional/binder eligibility and faithful completeness fallback, and promote only through versioned shadow-run matrices with automatic demotion on breach. Unit/property/model/differential/trace suites cover simp rules, indexes, certificate versions/checkers, SAT state, grind theory hooks, e-graph extraction, portfolio arbitration, cancellation and budgets; named mutants skip proof checking, accept stale indexes, alter firing/tie-break order, corrupt SAT proof, overclaim grind rows, cross an ineligible binder, or bypass demotion, and each is killed. Real no-mock Golem/Mirror E2E executes production tactics on mathlib files, plants stale/corrupt/wrong-proof/engine divergences, restores exact inputs, and proves recovery with strict bundles and per-row evidence levels. G4 core integration plus full frontier obligations, all W7 children, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, Tribunal/Mirror/mutation/fuzz/fault/thread/performance lanes are green before W7 closes and feeds G6. No external solver, unchecked certificate, direct-API-only tactic proof, stale cache acceptance, frontier leakage into faithful/sound artifacts, timing-only claim, mock-only Golem, or deferred listed W7 feature satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W1: UPSTREAM_LEDGER + suite upgrade protocol — deliberate, Tribunal-diffed lock bumps
description:
Establish UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md + the suite upgrade protocol (§2.11, R16): primitives that belong in a foundation crate land THERE, tracked per-entry — seed entries from the plan: asupersync (deterministic work-stealing merge combinator — ordered join of speculative results — if the existing quorum/pipeline combinators do not already express it), franken_networkx (incremental dominator maintenance for dynamic DAGs — the live-edit invalidation cone), frankensearch (incremental index commit hooks matching Ledger transaction boundaries), franken_markdown (the Lean lexer profile for the shared highlighter), frankensqlite (any blob-store access pattern the CAS proves out). fln-bignum stays in-repo (kernel-adjacent code answers to the kernel covenant) with the recorded revisit trigger: a second suite member needing arbitrary precision.

The suite upgrade protocol (R16 mitigation): SUITE.lock upgrades are deliberate and Tribunal-diffed — an upgrade PR must carry the closure-audit delta (any new transitive dep is a reviewed allowlist change, D1), the conformance run against the new pin, and a rollback note; per-component acceptance gates before a suite feature becomes load-bearing; if a needed component is immature under this load, the fallback is extracting the narrow algorithm into an fln-* crate (design-level reuse) or rejecting the component — the only-Franken rule holds.

Acceptance: UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md exists with the seed entries, statuses, and owning-repo links; the upgrade-protocol checklist is enforced by CI on any SUITE.lock diff (a lock change without the audit artifacts fails). Tests: the CI enforcement check itself (a seeded bare lock bump must fail with the missing-artifact list logged).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md records each required foundation capability, owning repository/link, rationale, current status, acceptance gate, load-bearing consumers, fallback, and revisit trigger; it includes the plan seed entries and keeps fln-bignum in-repo until the documented second-consumer trigger.
2. Any SUITE.lock change triggers a required protocol that validates exact component/reference/corpus pins, full transitive closure and allowlist delta, license/source provenance, per-component acceptance evidence, Tribunal conformance diff, determinism/security implications, migration notes, and an executable rollback note before the new component becomes load-bearing.
3. CI fails closed with a complete deterministic missing/stale-artifact list for a bare lock bump, a hidden transitive dependency, an unapproved external component, mismatched evidence roots, or a component whose gate is incomplete. A correctly evidenced bump passes without manual bypass; emergency waivers are public, typed, scoped, and expiring.
4. Named unit/property/golden/mutation suites cover ledger schema/links/status transitions, lock parsing/canonicalization, closure diffs, artifact binding, rollback validation, unchanged-lock behavior, cancellation, and failure atomicity. Bare-bump, suppressed-dependency, stale-evidence, and non-expiring-waiver mutants must be killed.
5. A shared real-repository E2E scenario creates an isolated candidate lock bump, demonstrates the exact failure report, attaches valid closure/Tribunal/rollback evidence and passes, plants a transitive dependency and fails, then restores the original candidate state. Human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON include run/change/component/artifact ids, bead/claim/gate rows, redacted commands/cwd, old/new canonical lock and closure hashes, pins, allowlist deltas, expected/actual checks, timings/status, artifact paths, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives baseline/candidate manifests, dependency graphs, conformance diffs, rollback proof, mutant outcomes, and E2E bundle. No suite bump or ledger row closes on prose-only intent or mocked dependency closure.

title: W10: native_decide Tier 2 — trace-guided kernel replay discharges ofReduceBool
description:
Implement native_decide Tier 2 — trace-guided kernel replay (§11.6): on certified paths, ofReduceBool is DISCHARGED, not trusted — the Tier 1 replay receipt (function-entry digests over the registered reflection set, branch decisions, literal-arithmetic transcripts) is replayed through Crucible as the DEFAULT for certified profiles, so the compiler and VM leave the trusted base for every evaluation the tier covers.

Scope and honesty rules: the tier holds only where the receipt covers the full evaluation (the registered reflection set; evaluations escaping it remain Tier 1 and are marked so); the trust ledger records per build which tier was achieved, because "we no longer trust the compiler" is a claim with a scope; the cost multiple of replay vs native evaluation is measured and published (an OBSERVED number, per §20.4 — never asserted); Tier 3 (a verified evaluator proven against the Judgment Specification) remains explicitly research and is NOT this bead. Certified --reproducible builds enable Tier 2 by default; standard builds keep Tier 1 sampling policy.

Acceptance: the reflection corpus discharges through kernel replay on certified profiles with zero trust extension to Golem/Iron for covered evaluations; the trust ledger and fln audit --tcb reflect the tier per declaration; the published cost-multiple table lands in the evidence bundle for G6. Tests: e2e certified-build test where a native_decide theorem verifies with the compiler deliberately perturbed (a wrong compiled result MUST be caught by replay — the whole point, proven by mutation); receipt-coverage boundary fixtures (an evaluation outside the registered set must degrade to Tier 1 with the degradation logged and marked, never silently claimed as Tier 2); replay determinism across the certified matrix.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. In certified profiles, Tier 2 is the default for every native_decide evaluation wholly covered by the registered reflection set: the complete Tier 1 receipt is deterministically replayed through Crucible and ofReduceBool is discharged by a kernel-checked artifact, removing Golem/Iron from the trusted base for that evaluation.
2. Coverage is computed per evaluation and provenance-bound. Any escape, unknown operation, incomplete/tampered receipt, replay budget exhaustion, or unsupported version degrades explicitly to Tier 1 with a typed reason and trust-ledger mark; it can never silently inherit Tier 2. Standard builds retain the Tier 1 sampling policy and Tier 3 remains out of scope.
3. fln audit --tcb and the build/declaration trust ledger report achieved tier, covered operations, receipt/proof roots, fallback reason, and evidence. Covered compiler perturbations that change native results are caught by replay before theorem admission.
4. Named unit/property/differential/mutation suites cover full/partial reflection coverage, replay semantics, receipt binding, certified/default policy, explicit degradation, audit aggregation, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and bit-deterministic replay across the certified matrix. Wrong-compiled-result, missing-coverage-edge, silent-Tier1, skip-certified-replay, and false-audit-tier mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E certified build runs real native_decide corpus theorems, deliberately perturbs a compiled result, proves replay rejection, verifies covered theorems with no compiler trust extension, exercises an out-of-set evaluation and its logged Tier 1 degradation, then rebuilds cleanly across certified platforms. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/build/declaration/evaluation/receipt/replay/proof/tier ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical term/environment/reflection/receipt/proof roots, coverage and expected/actual verdicts, cost/timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. Reproducible evidence publishes replay-to-native cost multiples as observed distributions, not assertions, and archives cross-platform matrices, perturbation mutants, trust ledgers, receipts/proofs, and E2E bundles. Close wording is scoped per covered evaluation and never claims Tier 3 or universal compiler independence.

title: W7: Frontier lanes — e-graph saturation + tactic portfolios, shadow-run promotion
description:
Implement the frontier rewrite lanes (§12.3, §12.7): the e-graph equality-saturation lane over simp-set subsets with proof extraction (rewrites recorded as justifications, extracted proofs replayed through the ordinary lemma-application path, kernel-checked like everything else — honestly scoped: conditional rewrites and binder-crossing rules restrict the eligible subset; the lane is an accelerator with a completeness fallback to the faithful core, never a replacement; its win condition is MEASURED via the PG-A profile, not assumed), and speculative tactic portfolios (first-with-work-stealing, deterministic winner selection by registered CGSE policy, losers cancelled through their asupersync regions; snapshot-cheap try trees — the search-side dividend of O(1) environment snapshots, exposed as tactics and through Envoy).

Both lanes promote ONLY by shadow-run (§12.6/§18.9): dual-run on the same demand nodes, products diffed, divergence and latency distributions recorded, promotion per workload/epoch/platform cell with deterministic sampling continuing afterward and auto-demotion on any soundness, fidelity, or tail-latency breach — promotion is a matrix, never a global flag. Frontier features never leak into faithful/sound artifacts (D18).

Acceptance: e-graph lane produces kernel-checked results agreeing with the faithful core on its eligible subset, with the measured win condition published; portfolio winner selection deterministic under the lab at any thread count; shadow-run promotion machinery generic enough for the other promotion consumers (K2 optimizations, Iron tiers, epoch bridges). Tests: extraction-proof replay fixtures; eligibility-boundary fixtures (ineligible rewrites must route to the faithful core); cancellation-correctness under portfolio racing (obligation-leak oracle clean); auto-demotion drill (planted breach must demote and file the incident bundle).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this bead owns OQ-5. Before promotion, record the exact eligible e-graph rewrite subset, exclusion semantics for conditional and binder-crossing rules, the pinned PG-A workload/profile, minimum win and tail-regression thresholds, proof-extraction cost limits, fallback behavior, and the decision-log amendment. Shadow-run data is evidence, not a substitute for the kernel-checked extraction law.
acceptance_criteria: 1. OQ-5 is resolved by a versioned reviewed row-per-rewrite-class decision table before e-graph promotion: it enumerates the eligible simp subset, conditional and binder-crossing boundaries, proof-construction rule, fallback, resource envelope, corpus, and measured win condition with latency/tail and proof-cost thresholds. Unknown or unmeasured classes remain ineligible. The frontier e-graph lane accepts only that explicitly evidenced subset; every equality-saturation step records a justification, extracted proof replays through ordinary lemma application, and the kernel checks the result before admission. Failure or budget exhaustion falls back without changing semantics. 2. Speculative tactic portfolios fork O(1) snapshots, execute in separate asupersync regions, cancel losers without obligation leaks, and select a winner through a registered deterministic CGSE policy whose result is schedule-independent at threads 1, 8, and 32. 3. Both lanes use a generic shadow-run promotion matrix keyed by workload, epoch, platform, mode/profile, and evidence version. Promotion requires semantic equality plus the OQ-5 measured latency/tail/proof-cost criteria, continues deterministic sampling, auto-demotes and files an incident bundle on any soundness/fidelity/tail breach, and cannot leak frontier products into faithful/sound artifacts. 4. Named unit/property/model/DPOR/differential/mutation suites cover e-graph congruence/rebuild/extraction, every OQ-5 eligibility boundary, proof replay/fallback, saturation limits, portfolio cancellation/winner ties, schedule perturbations, matrix transitions, stale evidence, and auto-demotion. Unchecked-extraction, ineligible-rewrite, nondeterministic-winner, leaked-obligation, false-win, and failed-demotion mutants must be killed. 5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs real eligible/ineligible Lean goals through faithful and frontier lanes, kernel-checks extracted proofs, races a real tactic portfolio under schedule perturbations, gathers shadow distributions, promotes one qualified cell, proves a below-threshold cell stays unpromoted, plants a semantic/tail breach, verifies immediate demotion and incident creation, and confirms faithful artifacts remain unchanged. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/goal/egraph/rewrite/proof/portfolio/candidate/promotion-cell/incident ids, bead/claim/invariant/Parity/OQ-5 rows, redacted argv/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical input/result/proof/artifact roots, winner policy and shadow diffs, latency/resources/status, cleanup, and final state. 6. CI archives the reviewed OQ-5 decision, proof traces, schedule/crashpacks, shadow/promotion matrices, incident bundles, mutant outcomes, PG-A measurements, and evidence manifests. Any speed win is reported with corpus/machine/distribution; no global promotion or frontier-to-faithful leakage is permitted.

title: W11 integration: Terminal InfoView + WASM Judge completion join
description:
Completion and release-integration join for the two plan §16.4 products. franken_lean-terminal-infoview-client-9q6 owns fln goals, Lantern RPC parity, terminal rendering, OQ-7 widget behavior, and the real PTY/tmux failure-recovery lane. fln-wasm-judge-browser-kvj owns the integrity-bound offline stock-browser Judge, sealed-capsule verification, native/WASM vectors, accessibility, adversarial browser matrix, and OQ-12 package implementation. Their different code, dependencies, and evidence remain separate.

This join owns only cross-product release coherence: both products are installed and discoverable under the same epoch/release manifest; fln identity/help/docs report their independent versions, support tiers, evidence links, and capability requirements honestly; shared receipt/capsule/snapshot vocabulary does not drift; robot schemas use the common evidence envelope without conflating browser verdicts with live proof-state grades; packaging or failure of either product cannot silently masquerade as success for the other. The front-door bead may integrate fln goals after the terminal child closes and does not wait for browser implementation, while W11 and G6 require this join and therefore both products.

Verification cross-checks release manifests, CLI discovery, schema/version links, support-tier reporting, offline assets, and independent failure isolation. A combined no-mock release smoke runs the installed terminal client against a real daemon and the installed browser package against a real sealed capsule, then exercises one product failing while the other remains correctly reported. It archives concise human logs and bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with both child evidence ids, release/epoch/build hashes, expected versus actual support state, commands/assets, first divergence, cleanup, and final state.

## Acceptance Criteria

Close only when both child beads are closed without narrowed scope and the integrated release exposes both products with correct independent identities, support tiers, schemas, evidence, failure isolation, and combined no-mock release smoke artifacts. The terminal client cannot stand in for the browser Judge; a core-only WASM artifact cannot stand in for the stock-browser package; browser delay cannot block unrelated front-door work; and no child test, negative/recovery lane, logging field, or user-visible feature may be dropped at this join.
design: Decomposition law: terminal RPC/TUI and offline WASM/browser implementation are parallel children because they share neither runtime nor primary user journey. This bead is a join only. franken_lean-eb3 consumes the terminal child directly; the W11 epic consumes this join and therefore retains the complete browser requirement. OQ-7 remains owned by franken_lean-v2p and consumed by the terminal child; OQ-12 remains owned by the packaging contract and consumed by the browser child.

The release manifest and installed surfaces expose terminal and browser products independently with correct epoch/version/support/evidence links and failure isolation. The combined release smoke proves both real paths and one-product-fails behavior. No child substitution, hidden coupling, narrowed scope, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.

title: W5/W10: Iron target/profile contract spike — resolve OQ-4 before FIR freezes
description:
Resolve plan OQ-4 before the FIR pipeline or Iron implementation freezes an expensive backend boundary. This is a contract and feasibility spike, not a substitute for fln-y55 or fln-o0j. It must determine: the supported-target tier beyond x86-64 and aarch64; whether static emission and Iron-JIT share one backend with certified/frontier profiles or require separated implementations; the target-independent FIR/FLBC-to-machine contract; relocation, calling-convention, register-allocation, executable-memory, unwind, and object/executable ownership boundaries; how target features enter cache keys and receipts; and how faithful, sound, frontier, and certified artifacts prevent JIT or host-specific behavior from leaking across modes.

The decision must preserve the closed dependency universe and sovereign-backend goal: no LLVM, Cranelift, system assembler/linker, or hidden C compiler on the Iron path. The optional C backend remains only the D2 parity/exotic-target lane. Use a thin real vertical prototype and measurements to price the alternatives before selecting one; do not ratify from prose alone.

## Acceptance Criteria

A versioned backend contract and decision-log row resolve every OQ-4 branch with evidence, rejected alternatives, migration consequences, safe fallback, and explicit target support tiers. Representative real lowering probes cover closures, calls/tail calls, allocation/RC boundaries, exceptions/effects, literals/bignum calls, relocations, debug/source mapping, and target-feature selection. The selected boundary is consumed by both fln-y55 and fln-o0j; neither may invent a second incompatible machine interface.

Named deterministic unit/property/mutation tests cover IR validation, calling-convention/relocation rules, unsupported features, malformed machine inputs, resource limits, and byte-stability. A real no-mock E2E probe emits and executes supported-host code and validates a non-host target artifact structurally; negative/recovery scenarios prove unsupported targets, unavailable executable memory, invalid relocations, and interrupted publication fail typed and leave no trusted artifact. Human logs and schema-versioned bounded NDJSON record exact inputs, target/profile/features, toolchain/host facts, byte hashes, timings/resources, expected versus actual, first divergence, exit/signal, and artifact references. All prototype claims are labeled at their honest evidence level; no benchmark or target-support claim is promoted from a mock or unmeasured design.
acceptance_criteria: OQ-4 is resolved before FIR or Iron interfaces freeze by a versioned reviewed backend contract and decision-log row covering support tiers beyond x86-64/aarch64, shared-versus-separated static/JIT architecture, the target-independent FIR/FLBC machine contract, calling conventions, register allocation, relocations, object/executable ownership, executable memory, unwind/debug mapping, target-feature cache/receipt identity, and faithful/sound/frontier/certified isolation. Rejected alternatives, migration consequences, safe fallback, and owner are explicit. A thin real vertical prototype lowers and executes closures, direct/tail calls, allocation/RC, exceptions/effects, literals/bignum calls, feature variants, and relocations on a supported host and structurally validates a non-host artifact without LLVM, Cranelift, system assembler/linker, or hidden cc; the C lane remains D2-only. fln-y55 and fln-o0j consume the identical frozen boundary. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover IR validation, ABI/relocation rules, deterministic bytes, unsupported/malformed features, budgets, cancellation, executable-memory denial, and publication atomicity; named host-leak, profile-leak, wrong-relocation, nondeterministic-layout, and hidden-tool mutants are killed. The real no-mock E2E includes positive host execution, non-host structural proof, unsupported target, invalid relocation, unavailable executable memory, interruption, exact restoration, and recovery with strict evidence containing target/profile/features/toolchain/host/input/output hashes and resources. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, prototype differential/mutation/fault lanes pass. No prose-only decision, mock benchmark, second machine interface, hidden external backend/tool, unmeasured support claim, JIT leakage into certified artifacts, unresolved branch, or deferred listed contract item satisfies closure.
