title: G0: The Laws of the Machine — pre-construction spike gate (§22.1)
description:
Gate G0 "The Laws of the Machine" (§22.1): ten compile-tested spikes and executed decisions that retire the load-bearing unknowns BEFORE any W2-W12 interface freezes. No later gate may begin with its unknowns unresolved (Declaration of Intent commitment 7).

The spikes price the central bets early: ABI resurrection of a real mathlib .olean (feeds Marrow/Grimoire layout methodology), independent kernel check vs lean4checker, Golem execution of real user-class compiled code on ABI values, hygiene/syntax fidelity on macro-heavy corpus files, byte-identical olean re-emit (FL-INV-04 feasibility), heartbeat/fuel-parity calibration, the defeq tail (twenty nastiest Corpus problems on the K2 memoization design), the Mirror-facade viability spike (Bet B2 priced before interfaces freeze), the instrumented-oracle spike (golden decision traces — mitigation for the R2 unifier-fidelity risk), and the full dependency-closure audit with SUITE.lock.

Exit criteria: every spike green or its finding folded into an amended interface decision recorded in the plan (§25 decision log); open questions OQ-1/OQ-2/OQ-3 resolved or explicitly owned by a named workstream. Each spike bead states its own acceptance test. This epic closes only when all ten spike beads close.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
acceptance_criteria: G0 closes only when all ten named spikes execute against the exact SUITE.lock Reference/Corpus and each produces compile-tested code, real no-mock fixtures, a typed result, retained reproducible evidence, and either a green feasibility result or an explicit amended interface/plan decision with owner, blast radius, and downstream dependency updates. The matrix is complete: real-mathlib olean ABI resurrection; independent kernel check against foreign witnesses; Golem execution of real user-class compiled code on ABI values; macro-heavy syntax/SourceInfo/hygiene fidelity; byte-identical olean re-emission feasibility; heartbeat/fuel parity calibration; the twenty registered defeq-tail cases against K2 design; Native Mirror facade viability with real mathlib tactics; instrumented-oracle trace schema/volume/replay; and full transitive dependency closure plus authoritative SUITE.lock. OQ-1, OQ-2, and OQ-3 are resolved in the reviewed decision log or assigned to a named workstream with a non-load-bearing boundary; no later W2-W12 interface may freeze atop an unpriced unknown. Each spike has table-driven unit/property/model and named mutation coverage for its extraction/parsing/validation logic, plus positive, planted-failure, exact-restoration, and recovery E2E phases using real artifacts and the shared evidence contract. Every spike declares input, time, output, memory/work, and subprocess budgets applicable to its boundary; zero/exact/one-over tests, deterministic cancellation points, typed Inconclusive/InternalFault separation, no partial publication, bounded logs, and clean recovery are required wherever the spike can exhaust or be cancelled. Cross-spike integration proves all outputs agree on epoch, ABI/OLEAN roots, symbol identities, platform facts, comparison classes, claim states, and resource accounting. Failures remain findings and block the gate; they are never normalized, benchmarked, or scoped away to obtain green. All child gates, scripts/check.sh, fmt, cargo check/test, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and strict bundle validation pass. No handwritten contract constant, mock-only Reference, synthetic substitute for the named real corpus, hidden exclusion, unresolved load-bearing unknown, unbounded spike, or deferred spike satisfies G0.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W9: Lantern & Palimpsest — daemon server, snapshots, causal proof graph
description:
Workstream W9 "Lantern & Palimpsest" (§14, §15): the single-daemon server behind the unchanged wire dialect, and the provenance substrate that makes the system explain itself.

Lantern: LSP + $/lean/* byte-compatible (plainGoal, plainTermGoal, rpc/* sessions, waitForDiagnostics, semantic tokens, widget delivery) — vscode-lean4 connects unmodified; one daemon, one shared immutable import heap (kills the ~60s x N-workers x GBs multiplication; PG-4/PG-6); snapshot machinery: immutable DocumentSnapshots with publication generations (stale-diagnostics race structurally impossible), sub-command incrementality from the finest enclosing checkpoint, cancel-correct under typing bursts, stable-identity diagnostics dedup, idempotent precondition-checked code actions, progressive proof-state negotiation, per-root epoch capsules, session replay bundles (server bugs become deterministic objects); the machine session API — fork a proof state, apply a tactic, read goals, O(1) per branch, thousands of branches per daemon (§14.4, the number that embarrasses the 18-735s per-branch literature).

Palimpsest: always-on ring-buffer traces + opt-in archives (frankensqlite); the causal proof graph (Bet B7) with typed edge families and completeness classes — ONLY Complete/Conservative edges may drive invalidation or trust (the welding law); unification forensics (minimal disagreement), instance why-not trees, semantic blame slices; semantic diff levels + semantic merge (blocks silent semantic conflicts Git cannot see; nothing merges around the kernel); fragility signals (advisory, D3); time travel, fln replay, repro bundles; build forensics as SQL.

Exit: gate G5 — vscode-lean4 smoke matrix green, PG-4/5/6/L met, session API live.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design: Stage topology: G5 consumes the concrete Lantern, snapshot/session, and replay/protocol stack needed for the daily-driver editor claim. Full W9 additionally closes Palimpsest, causal proof graph completeness, forensics, semantic diff/merge, why-trusts, time travel, repro bundles, and SQL observability, and therefore blocks G6. W9 consumes the G4-complete toolchain and Ledger core through its component graph, not W8 federation/distribution bookkeeping. Every W9 feature remains mandatory before the final sovereign release.
acceptance_criteria: W9 closes only when Lantern and Palimpsest share one immutable snapshot/provenance truth. Lantern is byte-compatible with the pinned LSP and $/lean dialect for initialization, document lifecycle, diagnostics, goals, terms, RPC sessions, semantic tokens, completions, code actions, progress, and widgets; unmodified vscode-lean4 passes. One daemon shares one import heap while DocumentSnapshots, publication generations, regions, stable diagnostic identities, preconditioned idempotent actions, progressive goals, epoch capsules, and cancellation prevent stale or cross-document publication. Sub-command incremental processing equals clean processing. ProofStateSnapshot and the machine session API fork O(1), enforce capability/budget/epoch ownership, support thousands of branches, release deterministically, and never treat provisional state as verified. Replay bundles reproduce protocol failures exactly. Palimpsest records typed causal nodes and edges for declarations, instances, simp, macros, elaboration, effects, and kernel verdicts with explicit completeness classes; only Complete or Conservative evidence drives invalidation or trust. It provides minimal unification disagreement, why-not instance trees, blame slices, semantic diff/merge that cannot bypass the kernel, fragility signals, why-trusts, time travel, repro bundles, and bounded SQL forensics. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation/lab tests cover protocol ordering, generation races, cancellation storms, snapshot/session lifecycle, capability denial, replay, causal completeness, welding law, graph queries, merge conflicts, storage crash/corruption, budgets, and recovery; named stale-publication, missing-edge, overclaimed-completeness, and merge-around-kernel mutants are killed. Real no-mock editor, terminal/session, replay, causal-query, semantic-merge, crash/fault, and long-soak E2E lanes emit strict bundles. G5 daily-driver evidence plus full Palimpsest scope, all W9 children, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, protocol/editor/lab/thread/platform/performance gates are green before G6. No patched editor, per-file duplicated import heap, mock-only RPC, stale diagnostic, incomplete edge used as complete, advisory signal used as authority, silent semantic merge, unbounded trace/query, or deferred listed W9 feature satisfies closure.

title: W5: Intrinsic table — the extern census implemented natively, row-per-symbol evidence
description:
Implement the intrinsic table from the extern census (§11.4, Appendix C): every @[extern] symbol in Init/Std/Lean at the pin — Nat/Int/Array/ByteArray/String/Float ops, IO, Task, ST, environment access, compiler-API entry points — as native Rust intrinsics beneath the broader Mirror facade. The table is GENERATED from the census (mechanical extraction, W1), and the census diff is the first artifact of every epoch advance.

Semantics: Float arithmetic is IEEE-754 binary64 with the Reference printing/parsing conventions; transcendentals route through fln-libm (§6.8) — no platform libm on any path that can reach an artifact or receipt (D21); UInt*/USize follow the ABI width rules; @[implemented_by] swaps honored at the boundary the ecosystem respects. Every row carries fixtures and a Parity-Ledger L-level; unclassified rows do not exist (census totality).

Acceptance: the census fully populated with an implementation status per row (G2 requirement: fully populated with fixtures); differential parity on the intrinsic fixture corpus vs the Reference (bit class where applicable, obs class where documented); missing-intrinsic calls produce typed unsupported errors naming the row, never UB or a guess. Tests: per-row fixture families generated alongside the table; boundary-value sweeps for numeric ops; String UTF-8 position-convention fixtures; Float printing round-trips.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: The generated intrinsic table is row-complete over every pinned extern in Init/Std/Lean, including Nat/Int/UInt/USize, arrays/byte arrays, strings, floats, IO, Task, ST, environment and compiler APIs. Each qualified row binds exact universes/telescope/signature, ABI widths, ownership/RC, effects/capabilities, implemented_by behavior, mode/profile support, implementation, fixtures, comparison class, L-level and evidence; missing, duplicate, stale, unknown or unclassified rows fail generation/build. Native implementations match Reference bit behavior where required and explicit observational classes elsewhere. Floats are IEEE binary64 with exact parse/print; transcendentals use fln-libm and no platform libm can affect artifacts/receipts. Unsupported calls return typed row-naming errors, never guess, panic or UB. Unit/property/model/differential/mutation suites are generated per row and include numeric boundaries, UTF-8 conventions, object/ownership, effects, cancellation/resources, and epoch diffs; named missing-row, wrong-width, platform-libm, implemented_by, ownership, effect and placeholder-success mutants are killed. Real no-mock Golem E2E executes the complete fixture families through production dispatch, plants unsupported/mismatch/fault cases, restores, and retains strict row/input/output/root/resource bundles. Census totality and epoch diff are permanent CI; dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, ABI/Mirror/differential/platform lanes pass. No hand-authored parallel table, census gap, platform libm, hidden oracle, stub success, direct-call-only evidence, overclaimed L-level, or deferred listed extern satisfies closure.

title: W10: Outbound ABI door — our plugins in the Reference host, per-platform matrix
description:
Implement the outbound ABI door (§17.4, §6.6, risk R5): FrankenLean-built plugins loading into the REFERENCE host — the harder direction of the bidirectional plugin promise, staged deliberately to G5 with its own Tribunal matrix per platform (inbound landed with W2).

Why harder: the Reference host has internal assumptions (allocator behavior, initialization ordering, symbol resolution details) we do not control; our emitted artifacts must satisfy them without any cooperation from upstream. Method: build against the generated ABI contract; validate through the C4 cross-load matrix outbound lanes; every discovered host assumption becomes a contract row (or an honest per-platform tier in the Parity Ledger rather than blocking G5 — the R5 fallback).

Acceptance: FrankenLean-built precompileModules artifacts and @[extern] libraries load and run in the Reference toolchain on the covered platforms; the outbound matrix green per platform or tiered honestly; mixed pipelines (Reference host importing our plugin importing our oleans) exercised. Tests: the outbound C4 lanes per platform; symbol-resolution and initializer-ordering fixtures in the Reference host; a soak with the Reference test suite driving our plugin surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. title: W6: Native tactic framework + bootstrap-safe primitive tactics behind facade rows
description:
Implement the native tactic framework + core tactic set (§10.5): goal management, metavariable assignment, evalTactic dispatch, elab_rules/macro_rules registration and matching, focusing/structuring combinators, evalExpr-class services, and the InfoTree hooks as Athanor code; the core tactic set (rfl-class closing, intro/apply/cases/induction machinery, simp via Anvil, decide via Crucible, omega/bv_decide via Verdict, norm_num-class evaluation) native behind Mirror facade rows — each with equivalence class, golden-trace fixtures, and its own Parity-Ledger L-level.

USER tactics — mathlib, aesop, project elab blocks — run on Golem and call this framework through the facade, receiving GoalState views and capability contexts. No tactic, native or user, can construct a trusted declaration except through Crucible (FL-INV-02). The framework observable semantics (state threading, backtracking, error propagation, InfoTree shape) are conformance surface, proven by the metaprogram-corpus rig running the ecosystem REAL tactic code.

Acceptance: the curated mathlib-tactic slice (G0-8) executes through the production framework with environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, and generated names diffing clean against the oracle at declared L-levels; core tactics pass their per-row fixture families. Tests: tactic-framework state-threading property tests; backtracking/error-propagation fixtures; facade-row differential families per core tactic; FL-INV-02 mutation test (a tactic attempting direct environment admission must be structurally impossible — compile-time — or caught).

title: G0-8: Mirror-facade viability spike — census + stubs vs real mathlib tactics
description:
G0-8 Mirror-facade viability spike (§22.1-8, feeds §4.3/§18.2): generate the builtin census and facade stubs for a metaprogram-heavy slice (Lean.Meta core + the tactic-framework entry points); compile a curated set of REAL mathlib tactic files against the facade with native services stubbed at prototype depth; enumerate every API whose semantics resist native backing and assign each an honest L-level.

This is the plan CENTRAL bet (B2) priced before interfaces freeze, and the direct mitigation of Critical risk R1 (Meta-API dependence effectively unbounded — packages compile against Lean.* internals that may expose object identity or allocation order the native facade cannot reproduce). The census partition (toolchain-api native / library-code source-elaborated / user-facing-data) must prove TOTAL on the slice: no unclassified symbol, ever — a symbol the census cannot classify blocks the L-level, never gets guessed.

Acceptance: (a) census extraction runs mechanically over the pinned Init/Std/Lean sources for the slice and emits symbol, signature, telescope, effect class, partition class; (b) generated facade stubs compile against real mathlib tactic code; (c) a written resistance list: every symbol whose observable semantics leak implementation detail, each with equivalence class (bit/obs/perf), honest L-level, and Behavior Note candidate; (d) the ordered closure plan for the facade L-level ratchet (resolves OQ-3). Tests: the slice + tactic files seed the metaprogram-corpus rig (§18.2 Mirror conformance).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this G0-8 spike owns the feasibility half of OQ-3. It produces the initial symbol-by-symbol census partition, dependency-closure ordering, generated facade schema, and evidence for real tactic/metaprogram probes. It must identify load-bearing unknown rows and hand a versioned decision artifact to franken_lean-epx; it does not claim the production facade L-level ratchet complete.

title: W2: Marrow object model + tri-state RC — the lean_object ABI twin
description:
Implement the Marrow object model + tri-state reference counting (§6.1, §6.1b, §6.2; crates fln-rt + fln-unsafe-abi): the Reference runtime object model, exactly, under the exported lean_* names — object header (RC word, memory-kind/other byte, tag byte), constructor objects with scalar-field packing, arrays/scalar arrays/strings (UTF-8 with byte-length/capacity semantics), closures (arity, fixed args), thunks, tasks, IO.Refs, external objects with class vtables, bignum objects (fln-bignum-backed mpz-shim view), small Nats as odd tagged pointers ((n << 1) | 1). Every layout constant comes from the W1 generated contract tables.

Two heaps, one membrane: CompatHeap (exact ABI, membrane-only allocation, all unsafe confined to fln-unsafe-abi with ledger rows per site) vs NativeHeap (typed asupersync region handles); generated converters with declared ownership and failure semantics; lazy boundary-specific conversion (a metaprogram that never inspects a term never forces its Compat projection — memory-doubling risk R10 is the named gate PG-6, not an accident). Tri-state RC: single-threaded fast path, atomic shared, persistent-never-counted — matching upstream so ABI-crossing borrow/inc/dec behaves identically. Hardened builds: debug ownership shadows (double-release + foreign-pointer misuse detection, quarantine, deterministic replay events).

Structural laws (D3): no fln-unsafe-* crate depends on fln-kernel/fln-checker; no unsafe export launderable into a checked declaration; panic law at the ABI boundary (§6.5). Acceptance: G0-1/G0-3 findings folded; C4 probes pass both directions on the implemented subset. Tests: layout unit tests generated FROM the contract module; RC balance property tests; ownership-shadow mutation tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: Marrow implements every generated lean_object layout and exported ownership contract required by the pinned ABI: headers, tags, scalar packing, constructors, arrays/scalar arrays, UTF-8 strings with byte length/capacity, closures and fixed args, thunks, tasks, IO.Refs, external class/vtable objects, bignum views, and odd-tagged small Nats. All offsets/tags/widths come from generated contract rows and compile-time/layout tests. CompatHeap and NativeHeap are type-separated; generated lazy converters are the only membrane and declare ownership, allocation, failure, capability, and no-claim boundaries. Tri-state RC matches single-threaded, atomic-shared, and persistent-never-counted behavior across borrow/inc/dec and concurrent transitions. Every unsafe site lives only in fln-unsafe-abi, has a ledger row and safe fallback, cannot depend on kernel/checker or export a checked-declaration capability, and catches panics at the ABI boundary. Debug ownership shadows detect/quarantine/replay double release, foreign pointers, wrong heap, stale external class, and RC imbalance. Unit/property/model/mutation suites cover every generated layout, tag/packing boundary, object lifecycle, conversion, RC interleaving, external callback, malformed pointer/class, resource limit, and recovery; named drop/retain/tag/layout/converter/unsafe-laundering mutants are killed. Real no-mock C4 E2E loads Reference-built plugins and upstream-generated objects, calls both directions, exercises faults and cleanup, and retains strict ABI/ownership/root/process evidence across certified platforms. G0 findings, dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, unsafe/dependency guards, stage0/plugin/soak/thread/platform lanes pass. No handwritten layout, raw pointer outside membrane, eager whole-world conversion, unchecked foreign object, leak/double-free waiver, hidden upstream runtime, unsafe authority laundering, or deferred listed object family satisfies closure.

title: W10: Iron backends — owned native codegen + JIT, C backend as parity apparatus
description:
Implement the Iron backends (§17.3, §11.3): the sovereign path — FLBC to native objects/executables via an owned baseline code generator (NO LLVM, no cranelift, no cc), x86-64 + aarch64 first, so lake build of executables needs no external toolchain (the G6 headline; resolves weakness census #12 partially and the mandatory-cc contract entirely). Iron-JIT (frontier, standard-mode only, fln-unsafe-jit): a baseline template JIT from FLBC with inline caches burned in — the tier that makes hot tactic loops approach precompiled speed; certified builds never enable it; results bit-identical to the interpreter by construction, spot-checked by a standing dual-run rig (PG-7: JIT >= 0.5x Reference-precompiled on the corpus).

Also: --backend c — emit C in the Reference idiom and drive the USER toolchain exactly as upstream leanc does (D2 tool #1, optional), for parity studies and exotic targets; never the anchor (D16). Both backends dual-run governed: Iron pinned to interpreter results, interpreter pinned to Reference observable evaluation semantics. OQ-4 (target set beyond x86-64/aarch64; JIT/static emitter sharing) is resolved inside this bead and recorded in the decision log.

Acceptance: real executables (incl. a Lake-built project binary) produced by Iron run correctly with zero cc present; JIT dual-run rig green; C-backend output compiles under the Reference leanc contract on the parity corpus. Tests: differential execution interpreter-vs-Iron-vs-C-backend on the corpus; executable-page management ledger rows audited (fln-unsafe-jit, D3); JIT-vs-interpreter bit-identity spot checks in CI; cross-arch CI lanes.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OQ-4 HANDOFF — consume the versioned contract from fln-iron-target-profile-contract-ba9 transitively through fln-y55. Iron static emission and JIT must use the selected shared/separate profile architecture, supported-target tiers, target-feature cache-key law, and mode/certification boundaries. Evidence that contradicts the contract reopens OQ-4 before interface drift is landed.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The owned Iron baseline compiler lowers validated FLBC into native objects/executables for x86-64 and aarch64 without LLVM, Cranelift, a system compiler, or any unapproved external tool. It implements target ABI, linking/runtime metadata, relocations, unwind/error behavior, deterministic emission, and executable closure required by the resolved OQ-4 target/profile contract. 2. Iron-JIT is frontier/standard-only in fln-unsafe-jit with every executable-memory operation ledgered and narrowly scoped; certified builds structurally cannot enable it. JIT and static outputs are pinned to interpreter semantics and bit-identical observable results for covered programs, with safe fallback on unsupported code or allocation failure. 3. --backend c emits the pinned Reference idiom and invokes only the optional D2 C compiler capsule with full provenance; absence yields typed capability guidance. It remains parity/exotic-target apparatus, never the sovereign anchor. OQ-4 final target and emitter-sharing decisions are recorded before interface freeze. 4. Named unit/property/golden/differential/mutation suites cover instruction selection, registers/spills, calls/returns/closures, GC/RC/FBIP edges, literals/control flow/exceptions, relocations/linking, deterministic objects, executable-page lifecycle, unsupported target/fallback, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and interpreter-Iron-JIT-C equivalence. Miscompile, bad relocation, W+X, JIT-in-certified, hidden-cc, and nondeterministic-object mutants must be killed. 5. A shared no-mock E2E lane builds and runs real standalone and Lake project executables with compiler tools absent from the environment, repeats on real x86-64/aarch64 runners, compares interpreter/Iron/JIT and optional real C-backend results, exercises unsupported/JIT-failure recovery, and scans processes/binaries for forbidden dependencies. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/program/target/backend/function/object/link/page ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/env/cwd, build/epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/tool-availability facts, canonical FLBC/object/executable/output roots, expected/actual, fallback and unsafe-ledger ids, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state. 6. Reproducible PG-7 evidence shows JIT at least 0.5 times pinned Reference-precompiled performance on the declared corpus/machine and publishes distributions. CI archives cross-arch byte/equivalence matrices, C parity outputs, unsafe audits, mutant outcomes, benchmarks, and E2E bundles; no sovereign claim closes with cc present or JIT enabled in certified mode. 7. This bead owns the W10 half of risk R12: it consumes the exact Golem/Mirror profile handoff, demonstrates which measured hot paths are removed by static Iron, JIT, or native-facade fast paths, re-runs fidelity and determinism after each change, and leaves any remaining ecosystem-tolerance miss as an explicit BLOCKED or downgraded performance claim for G6. No optimization may alter a verdict, diagnostic, artifact, effect order, or Mirror row to obtain the target.

title: Gate G1: The Independent Judge — all of mathlib re-checked, zero divergence, PG-1
description:
Convergence gate G1 "The Independent Judge" (§22.3): Marrow + Grimoire + Crucible check EVERY mathlib olean at the pin with zero acceptance divergence against the Reference toolchain; PG-1 met (<= 5 min on the 32-core profile, <= 25 min single-thread); fln check-olean ships as a standalone auditor.

Why this gate is first and why it matters strategically: the program FIRST public artifact is the strongest independent verification the Lean world has ever had — a foreign-blooded kernel (no shared code, no shared compiler, no shared bignum) re-checking the entire corpus in minutes — shipped BEFORE FrankenLean has elaborated a single file. It is simultaneously the deepest de-risking event available: it proves the ABI/olean/kernel spine (the layout extraction methodology, the region codec, the judgment inventory) against the full Corpus at production scale.

Evidence bundle required to declare the gate: full-corpus run logs on the baseline machine; receipts + transparency log entries; the kernel differential rigs green (lean4checker/lean4lean agreement); Parity Ledger rows for the kernel/olean/ABI surfaces at their achieved L-levels; PG-1 and PG-8 numbers with claim states; every divergence (if any occurred) closed through the §8.2 triage law with fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: G1 depends on the concrete Independent Judge product and evidence surfaces, not on completion of umbrella workstreams. fln-fur supplies the full-recheck olean reader, K1/K2/checker consensus, receipts, CLI, and corpus evidence; franken_lean-3j8 supplies the standing foreign-witness differential rigs; fln-a6q and fln-544 supply calibrated PG-1/PG-8 measurement. Unrelated W2 scope such as olean-next, plugin doors, effects, libm, and the full ABI workstream must not delay the first public auditor. The W3 epic depends on this gate as its integration exit, never the reverse.
acceptance_criteria: G1 closes only on a manifest-complete no-mock recheck of every mathlib olean at the pinned Corpus commit, with no hidden exclusion and zero acceptance divergence among FrankenLean K1/K2, fln-checker, the Reference kernel, lean4checker, and lean4lean; every pairwise disagreement halts publication and retains minimized fixtures plus traces. fln check-olean ships as a standalone air-gap-capable auditor with stable human and versioned robot schemas, typed malformed/corrupt/resource/cancellation/InternalFault outcomes, no panic, no external Lean implementation in the runtime path, and clean recovery after every refusal. Canonical judgments, receipts, logical roots, transparency-log entries, and Parity Ledger rows bind exact toolchain, Corpus, input closure, engine/fuel policy, mode, determinism class, and evidence level. Full-corpus artifacts and diagnostics are bit-identical at 1, 8, and 32 threads; the certified platform matrix is explicit. PG-1 is met on valid baseline runs at no more than 5 minutes on the 32-core profile and 25 minutes single-thread, and PG-8 read throughput is reported at its honest claim state with raw samples, host/isolation/cache facts, tails, invalid-run retention, and no timing-only correctness claim. Unit/property/model tests cover every judgment and codec boundary; mutation campaigns kill skipped kernel rules, corrupted relocation/layout data, stale cache acceptance, wrong engine consensus, receipt tampering, and Inconclusive-to-rejection promotion. The real full-corpus E2E includes positive, planted divergence/corruption, exact restoration, and recovery phases under the shared evidence contract with bounded separate logs and independently validated committed bundles. All owning bead gates, scripts/check.sh, fmt, cargo check/test, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, differential rigs, codec rigs, thread matrix, mutation lanes, and fault drills are green. No sampled-corpus substitute, unclassified divergence, missing witness, mock-only oracle, performance waiver presented as success, or deferred listed feature satisfies G1.

title: W8: Publish compiled rewrite indexes — Ledger CAS + olean-next attachment integration
description:
Integrate W7's deterministic compiled rewrite-index format with the W8 Ledger and Grimoire artifact planes. Publish each per-library discrimination automaton as a typed demand-node product and olean-next attachment keyed by the exact simp-set semantic hash, implementation/schema version, epoch, mode, and CGSE policy; hydrate it through local/remote CAS validation; project it into daemon sessions without rebuilding; and invalidate or reject it structurally whenever any key input changes.

This bead resolves the stage boundary cleanly: W7 implements and proves the faithful simp driver plus deterministic index builder/reader equivalence; W8 owns transactional publication, retention, hydration, invalidation, quarantine, and cache provenance. No W7 component may claim a Ledger-published artifact before this integration exists.

Acceptance: a mathlib slice builds indexes once, publishes atomically, reloads from a fresh process and from a validated remote-cache fixture, and yields candidate sets and simp results identical to a fresh in-memory index; unchanged simp sets reuse byte-identical artifacts; changed rules/options/epoch/schema cannot load stale artifacts; promotion crashes leave no half-published attachment.

Verification: unit schema/key tests, builder-vs-loaded representation equivalence, stale-key and poisoned-cache mutations, kill-at-every-promotion-step drills, and shared no-mock E2E scripts for build -> publish -> hydrate -> simplify with complete CAS keys, manifests, hashes, receipts, expected/final states, and first-divergence logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. title: W6: Unifier/defeq — the approximation ladder, implemented against golden traces
description:
Implement unification + defeq against golden traces (§10.2): transparency-aware whnf/isDefEq mirroring the Reference approximation ladder — syntactic fast paths, first-order approximation, Miller patterns, delayed assignments, postponement, unfolding by reducibility and definitional height, structure-eta awareness. Mathlib DEPENDS on the precise power of the unifier (proofs elaborate because a particular approximation fires); this is where bug-for-bug earns its keep and where Critical risk R2 lives.

Method (the R2 mitigation): implemented AGAINST the instrumented-oracle golden decision traces (G0-9 -> §18.3) — which approximation fired, what postponed, what unfolded, in what order — at Corpus scale, with trace-replay rigs running continuously. Every T3 divergence is triaged with the strong prior that it is a fidelity defect; a persistent divergence class becomes a faithful-mode blocker and a published erratum with quantified Corpus blast radius, never a quiet Behavior Note (the epoch ships R3, not R4, until closed). Failures are recorded as typed constraint cells (HasType, IsDefEq, SynthesizeInstance, Coerce, ResolveName, ElaboratePattern, ProveTermination) with origins, dependencies, attempts, and outcomes — the substrate Palimpsest blame slices are computed from.

Acceptance: trace-replay agreement on the pilot corpus, expanding toward Corpus scale with the divergence ledger empty or fully classified; the G0-7 tail suite green through the production engine; constraint-cell recording complete per the perturbation validator. Tests: golden-trace replay rigs per approximation stage; postponement-order fixtures; defeq differentials vs the oracle on C0-C2.

title: W6: Term elaboration — coercions, match compilation, recursion elaborators, exact generated names
description:
Implement term elaboration (§10.3): coercion insertion, implicit/strict-implicit/instance-implicit binder handling, auto-bound implicits, structure instances and anonymous constructors, dependent match compilation into auxiliary matchers with generated equation lemmas and splitters MATCHING the Reference generated-name and shape conventions (metaprograms pattern-match on these artifacts — they are API, not internals), deriving handler dispatch, attribute application, variable/section/namespace resolution with the Reference ambiguity rules, and the recursion elaborators (structural, well-founded with the same measure-synthesis surface, partial, unsafe) lowering to EXACTLY the kernel-visible constructions of §1.2 (recursors, WellFounded.fix, unfold equations — the kernel sees only these).

Termination elaboration records a machine-readable TerminationCard (call graph, candidate measures, generated obligations, chosen theorem dependencies) — the artifact the Bloodhound repair lane consumes when a termination proof breaks under migration (§16.1).

Acceptance: generated-name conventions byte-agree with the oracle on the match/equation-lemma corpus (C2 metaprogramming-weight packages); recursion lowerings kernel-check identically; TerminationCards emitted and schema-validated. Tests: match-compilation differentials incl. nested/overlapping patterns; equation-lemma shape fixtures; deriving-handler corpus; ambiguity-resolution fixtures per the Reference rules.

Match compilation emits auxiliary matchers, splitters, equation lemmas, source maps and generated names/shapes byte-equivalent to the oracle where observable. Recursion lowers to exactly the kernel-visible recursors, WellFounded.fix and unfold equations. Every recursion emits a schema-validated TerminationCard with call graph, candidate measures, obligations, selection and theorem dependencies. Unit/property/model/differential/mutation tests cover every form, ambiguity, coercion chain, matcher shape, generated name, deriving handler, termination success/failure, budgets/cancellation, rollback and recovery; named wrong-name/shape, missing-obligation, ambiguity-order, unchecked-lowering, and card-drop mutants are killed. title: W1: Typed error taxonomy + faithful diagnostic renderers — D8 values, R9 epoch projection
description:
Implement the typed error taxonomy + the faithful diagnostic renderers (D8 normative taxonomy, risk R9): errors cross crate boundaries as TYPED, VERSIONED VALUES — SyntaxFailure, MacroFailure, ElaborationFailure, KernelRejection, KernelInconclusive (resources/cancellation, FL-INV-07), ArtifactCorrupt, ArtifactEpochMismatch, AbiViolation, CapabilityDenied, PluginCrashed, BuildFailure, ProtocolFailure, ReplayDivergence, InternalInvariantViolation — defined once in fln-core, consumed everywhere. Panics are invariant failures, never user diagnostics; malformed source, artifacts, protocol messages, and plugin output must not panic anywhere in the workspace.

The renderer half (R9: faithful diagnostic text/ordering can depend on incidental internal traversal order upstream): faithful-mode frontends render the typed values into Reference-compatible text and JSON via a per-EPOCH renderer — the typed causal model is the source of truth, the renderer is a projection, and receipts retain the typed cause even when the rendered text is upstream-identical. Message TEXT is equivalence-classed per §4.1 (the Parity Ledger carries separate semantic-vs-exact rows); where exact text depends on incidental upstream order, the row holds at L2/L3 (semantic) with the exact-renderer gap NAMED, and CGSE policies pin our own rendering order. Sound mode renders the richer Palimpsest bodies (BN-02) with positions and severities unchanged.

Acceptance: the taxonomy crate compiles with exhaustive match enforcement (adding a variant breaks every consumer until handled — no catch-all arms in authoritative crates); the faithful renderer reproduces Reference diagnostics on the transcript corpus at each row declared class (exact or semantic); every panic path in a fuzz sweep of malformed inputs surfaces as a typed value, never an abort. Tests: renderer golden tests against the C-family diagnostic transcript corpus with per-row comparison classes logged; a taxonomy-versioning test (an old serialized error decodes or fails typed, never garbles); panic-freedom fuzzing across every decoder entry point; equivalence-class regression tests so a wording change in sound mode can never leak into faithful output.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: One versioned exhaustive fln-core taxonomy represents SyntaxFailure, MacroFailure, ElaborationFailure, KernelRejection, KernelInconclusive, ArtifactCorrupt, ArtifactEpochMismatch, AbiViolation, CapabilityDenied, PluginCrashed, BuildFailure, ProtocolFailure, ReplayDivergence and InternalInvariantViolation with typed causes, positions, resources and evidence. Authoritative consumers use exhaustive matches with no catch-all; new variants fail compilation until handled. Panics remain internal invariant failures and malformed source/artifact/protocol/plugin input never panics or aborts. Per-epoch faithful renderers project typed values to Reference-compatible human/JSON text, ordering, positions, severities and exit classes at each row declared exact/semantic comparison level; receipts retain typed causes. Sound mode may add Palimpsest bodies under BN-02 without changing acceptance/position/severity or leaking wording to faithful. Old serialized versions decode losslessly or fail typed, never garble. Unit/golden/property/fuzz/mutation tests cover every variant/version/renderer/mode, ordering, malformed payload, bounded adversarial details, panic freedom and equivalence classes; named catch-all, Inconclusive-to-rejection, order drift, sound-text leak, swallowed-cause and garbled-version mutants are killed. Real no-mock diagnostic E2E compares C-family transcripts, corrupts/ages inputs, restores, and retains strict typed/rendered/exit/root/final-state bundles. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, decoder fuzz/Mirror/protocol/platform lanes pass. No stringly cross-crate error, panic-as-user-error, exact-text overclaim, swallowed stderr/cause, unbounded rendering, mode leakage, or deferred listed variant satisfies closure.

title: W1: Mode system + certified axis — structural faithful/sound/frontier, D18 leak prevention
description:
Implement the mode system + determinism/evidence axis plumbing (§4.2, D17, D18): the machinery that makes faithful / sound / frontier a STRUCTURAL property of every artifact rather than a flag convention.

Scope: mode selection threading through every subsystem (one type, one source of truth, no per-crate booleans); the orthogonal determinism axis — standard (FL-INV-01 always holds) vs certified (--reproducible: the complete content-hashed input closure — sources, toolchain, SUITE.lock, options, plugin hashes, mode, CGSE policy epoch — yields bit-identical artifacts across the certified matrix, with the sidecar closure manifest; D2 tool products excluded from certification BY CONSTRUCTION); the evidence axis wiring (per-surface L-levels and per-release R-levels read from the Parity Ledger, never asserted). The D18 leak-prevention law is the heart: frontier features NEVER leak into faithful/sound artifacts — every artifact carries its producing mode in provenance, faithful/sound artifact sets are CI-scanned for frontier products (a frontier-marked object in a sound artifact set is a build break), and frontier-only code paths (olean-next emission, e-graph lanes, Iron-JIT, MCP write-tools, epoch bridges) are gated at compile time or capability level, not by if-statements convention.

Why this is its own bead: a dozen other beads say "faithful mode does X, sound mode does Y, frontier never leaks" — this bead builds the mechanism they all assume, early enough that no subsystem invents its own mode plumbing. Acceptance: mode + axis types land in fln-core with the provenance-marking scheme; the leak-scan CI check catches a seeded frontier artifact in a sound set; a --reproducible fixture build produces the sidecar manifest and bit-identical outputs on two runs. Tests: mode-propagation property tests (no code path observes a mode other than the session mode); leak-scan mutation tests; certified-closure completeness test (omitting any closure input from the manifest fails); Behavior-Note linkage checks (every sound-vs-faithful divergence point names its BN id, CI-verified against the register).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: One fln-core mode value threads through every subsystem and artifact; no per-crate booleans or unrecorded defaults exist. Faithful, sound and frontier semantics plus standard/certified determinism and evidence axes are orthogonal typed contracts. Every artifact, cache key, receipt and bundle binds producing mode, CGSE epoch, determinism class and complete input closure. Certified --reproducible manifests cover sources, toolchain/SUITE.lock, options, plugins, target/profile and policies; D2 products and nondeterministic external inputs are structurally excluded. Frontier-only olean-next, e-graph/portfolio, Iron-JIT, MCP write and epoch-bridge code/artifacts are compile/capability gated and CI rejects any frontier marker, dependency or transitive product in faithful/sound sets. Every intentional sound/faithful divergence names a registered Behavior Note and per-row evidence; evidence levels are read from the ledger, never asserted. Unit/property/model/mutation tests traverse propagation, serialization/cache/receipt keys, closure completeness, mode combinations, feature/capability gates and recovery; named dropped-input, per-crate-mode, frontier-leak, D2-certification and missing-BN mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E builds each mode, plants frontier contamination and omitted closure inputs, proves rejection/restoration, and performs two isolated certified builds with byte-identical outputs and strict manifests. Dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, source/artifact/leak/reproducibility/platform lanes pass. No runtime if-convention in place of structural isolation, mixed-mode cache hit, incomplete closure, external-tool certified product, overclaimed evidence, hidden divergence, or deferred listed axis satisfies closure.

title: W1: Tribunal campaign machinery — mutation, fuzzing, drills, delta-debugger, thread matrix
description:
Build the Tribunal campaign machinery as a reusable framework (§18.2, §18.3) — the apparatus the per-subsystem rigs plug into, distinct from the W1 bootstrap (epoch lab + differential harness):

(1) The mutation-campaign framework: seeded-defect injection (skipped positivity check, inverted universe condition, leaked child-transaction assignment, omitted effect read, corrupted olean bounds check, dropped retain, bypassed capability, stale publication generation accepted, cache receipt trusted without hash validation) with the kill-tracking ledger — every critical mutant must be killed by a NAMED test or check; a surviving critical mutant blocks the gate. (2) The fuzzing fleet: grammar-based Lean source generation (syntax-directed + Corpus mutation), kernel term storms, region/olean structure fuzzing, VM opcode fuzzing — all under budgets, all crash-triaged to fixtures automatically. (3) The fault-and-recovery drill framework: kill -9 at every CAS/manifest promotion step, corrupted local/remote cache objects, disk-full mid-build, compiler-child crashes, server crashes with coordinator restart — each drill declaring an EXPECTED FINAL STATE and proving which products committed and which rolled back ("the process restarted" is not a pass). (4) The delta-debugger: automatic shrinking of any divergence or crash into a minimal checked-in fixture. (5) The no-mock lane enforcement: release-level claims close only against real binaries, real filesystems, real editors, real corruption — the evidence gate rejects mocked boundaries. (6) The thread-matrix determinism runner: {1,8,32} per commit, {96}+ weekly, diffing environments/diagnostics/artifacts bit-for-bit (PG-5 infrastructure).

Acceptance: each framework demonstrated on a seeded target with detailed structured logging; the kill-ledger, drill-expectation, and no-mock policies are machine-checked in CI, not conventions. Tests: framework self-tests (planted mutant survives -> gate blocks; drill final-state mismatch -> failure with the state diff logged).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: INVARIANT CAMPAIGN OWNER — bind campaign ids directly to FL-INV-01 through FL-INV-07 and their owning beads. In particular, the thread matrix is the standing FL-INV-01 enforcement lane; facade perturbations feed FL-INV-03; codec corruption/round-trip campaigns feed FL-INV-04; stale/forged/cache-input and epoch-bridge mutants feed FL-INV-05; engine-admission bypasses feed FL-INV-02/06; cancellation/resource/error promotion feeds FL-INV-07. A campaign run that merely executes without killing its named mutant is a failure.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable Tribunal framework provides six machine-enforced lanes: named seeded-mutation campaigns with kill ledger; grammar/source, kernel-term, region/olean and VM-opcode fuzzing under budgets with automatic fixture triage; fault/recovery drills with declared product-by-product final states; automatic divergence/crash delta-debugging to checked-in fixtures; no-mock evidence enforcement for release claims; and deterministic thread matrices at 1/8/32 per commit and 96 or more weekly. Campaign ids bind directly to FL-INV-01 through FL-INV-07 and owning beads. Critical mutants include kernel rule bypass, transaction leak, omitted effect read, codec bounds, RC drop, capability bypass, stale generation/cache/epoch bridge, forged evidence, and outcome promotion; execution without the named kill is failure. Drills inject kill/crash/corruption/disk-full/network/process faults at every registered boundary and verify exact committed/rolled-back state, not mere restart. Unit/property/model/mutation self-tests cover harness schemas, seed/replay, timeouts/budgets, shrink preservation, expected-state comparison, mock detection, artifact retention and thread diffs; planted survivor and wrong-final-state cases block. Real no-mock E2E demonstrates every framework on a seeded real target, restores it, and retains strict run/campaign/mutant/seed/resource/process/final-state bundles. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and CI policy validators pass. No convention-only kill ledger, mock release lane, crash without shrink, restarted-process success, missing invariant owner, nondeterministic unreplayable fuzz failure, surviving critical mutant, or deferred listed campaign family satisfies closure.

title: W7: grind reimplementation — congruence + e-matching, honestly staged L-levels
description:
Reimplement grind natively (§12.6): grind is core-toolchain machinery at the pin, so under the Oracle-Only Law it is reimplemented — congruence closure, e-matching, and its theory hooks as Anvil engines behind facade rows — developed against instrumented-oracle decision traces and Reference-as-oracle differentials.

Staging is deliberate and honest: its Parity-Ledger rows ENTER at L1/L2 and ratchet to L3 on the grind corpus by G4 rather than pretending day-one exactness, because grind semantics are still moving upstream and the census pins our coupling per epoch. Mathlib-layer automation (aesop, polyrith, ...) is user code on Golem and needs NO reimplementation. All outputs kernel-checked per FL-INV-06.

Acceptance: the grind corpus at L3 by G4 (goal-closure agreement with classified exclusions); decision-trace replay green on the covered fragment; epoch-diff tooling flags upstream grind changes as census deltas requiring row review. Tests: congruence-closure property tests (equivalence-class correctness vs a simple oracle implementation); e-matching trigger fixtures from the traces; differential goal families with the exclusion ledger explicit.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

1. Reimplement the pinned grind core natively: congruence closure, e-matching, theory hooks, and Native Mirror facade rows are project code developed from instrumented-oracle traces and differential observations. Upstream grind implementation never executes as a component; mathlib-layer automation remains user code on Golem.
2. All grind-produced proof artifacts are replayed and kernel-checked before environment admission. Unsupported, exhausted, cancelled, malformed, or divergent cases yield classified refusal/Inconclusive and cannot be cached or rendered as rejection/acceptance.
3. Parity rows begin honestly at L1/L2 and reach L3 on the declared grind corpus by G4 with goal-closure agreement and an explicit, reproducible exclusion ledger. Covered decision traces replay; epoch census-diff tooling flags every upstream grind/facade change for row review and prevents stale evidence promotion.
4. Named unit/property/model/differential/fuzz/mutation suites cover equivalence-class correctness against a simple independent oracle, congruence explanations, e-matching triggers/substitutions, theory-hook boundaries, trace replay, facade dispatch, budgets, deterministic ordering, and exclusion classification. Unsound merge, missed trigger, unchecked output, hidden exclusion, and stale-epoch mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs real grind corpus goals and user automation through Golem, compares closure/refusal and covered traces with the pinned Reference, replays proofs through the kernel, exercises an excluded and over-budget case, applies a synthetic epoch census delta, and verifies clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/goal/trace/eclass/ematch/proof/Parity-row ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/epoch/mode/profile/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical goal/result/proof roots, expected/actual closure/refusal and first divergence, exclusion reason, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives golden traces, corpus/exclusion manifests, proof receipts, epoch diffs, mutant outcomes, and E2E bundles. The bead cannot claim L3 beyond covered rows or treat a classified exclusion as agreement.

title: W11: WASM Judge browser — offline sealed-capsule verification
description:
Implement the WASM Judge half of plan §16.4b according to the versioned OQ-12 packaging contract. Ship the certified K1 and structurally independent fln-checker seats, bounded sealed-capsule decoder, certificate and receipt verification, transparency-log inclusion/consistency checks, and an integrity-bound stock-browser shell. A user can open the package offline, select or drag a real .flnpack, re-check declarations and dependency closure against the same canonical vectors as native execution, inspect verdicts and graph slices, and export a verifiable result without trusting a network service.

Preserve the trust boundary exactly. Unknown package/capsule/certificate versions fail closed; UI, worker, core WASM, and fixture assets are content-bound by the selected packaging contract; no JavaScript result can manufacture kernel acceptance; disagreement halts; certificate failure falls back to bounded recomputation where the contract permits; resource exhaustion/cancellation is Inconclusive; no filesystem, network, platform libm, native plugin, or hidden oracle participates. Full browser elaboration remains explicitly outside Tier 1 and cannot inflate the Judge claim.

The browser surface is accessible by keyboard and screen reader, gives progressive but honestly graded progress, renders stable typed errors for corrupt/epoch-mismatched/unsupported/oversized inputs, and survives worker restart, refresh, retry, and interrupted capsule streaming without remembering false success. Declaration and dependency browsing is bounded and paginated. Browser-local state is versioned and never treated as attestation without fresh integrity and receipt verification.

Verification includes unit/property/model tests for manifest binding, version negotiation, streaming decode, budgets, certificate fallback, receipt/log consistency, result export/import, worker isolation, accessibility state, and native/WASM vector identity. Named mutants bypass an asset hash, accept an unknown version, trust a UI verdict, skip one engine seat, promote Inconclusive, drop a dependency, ignore a byte flip, reuse stale browser state, or report success before terminal verification. The no-mock E2E runs offline in every declared stock-browser engine against a real sealed mathlib-slice capsule, verifies native-vector identity, rejects tampered capsule and mismatched UI/core assets, exercises budget exhaustion and worker crash, refreshes/retries, and proves clean recovery and final state.

Emit concise accessible human status and retained bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate, browser/platform/epoch/mode/profile/build and asset hashes, package/capsule/receipt/certificate/input/output ids, policy and engine seats, expected versus actual verdict/error/consumption, monotonic timings, memory and decode budgets, worker/exit status, console and artifact references, first divergence, recovery, cleanup, and asserted final state.

## Acceptance Criteria

The Judge closes only when the OQ-12 package is shipped as an integrity-bound offline stock-browser experience; real sealed-capsule verification, native/WASM vector identity, two-seat disagreement behavior, bounded decoding/recomputation, accessible browsing, every named negative and mutation, worker/refresh recovery, and the declared browser matrix pass with archived reproducible human and NDJSON evidence. A core-only WASM file, mock browser, network service, UI-trusted result, skipped browser, hidden oracle, or deferred listed Tier-1 feature fails closure.
acceptance_criteria: The Judge closes only when the OQ-12 package is shipped as an integrity-bound offline stock-browser experience; real sealed-capsule verification, native/WASM vector identity, two-seat disagreement behavior, bounded decoding/recomputation, accessible browsing, every named negative and mutation, worker/refresh recovery, and the declared browser matrix pass with archived reproducible human and NDJSON evidence. For every declared browser engine, two cold-state runs of the same package, capsule, epoch, mode, policy, and budgets must produce byte-identical exported verification payloads and identical canonical semantic records: declaration order, dependency closure, seat verdicts, errors, consumption, receipt/certificate ids, and final state. Timings, browser-generated run ids, and artifact paths are explicitly excluded from equality; worker scheduling, refresh, and retry may not change semantics. Package assembly also repeats from the same locked inputs and must yield identical asset order, hashes, manifest, and distributable bytes. A core-only WASM file, mock browser, network service, UI-trusted result, skipped browser, hidden oracle, nondeterministic semantic/export output, or deferred listed Tier-1 feature fails closure.

title: W2: Compacted regions — construction, mmap load, relocation, page sharing
description:
Implement compacted regions (§6.4): region construction (object-graph serialization with pointer relocation tables), region loading (mmap, relocate-or-copy fallback, page-sharing across consumers — the enabler of the Lantern shared import heap §7.5), and region hygiene (immutability enforcement, persistent-RC marking).

Design law: this is deliberately THE SAME code path the Grimoire olean codec uses — olean fidelity and runtime-region fidelity are one test surface, so every codec round-trip test also exercises the runtime region machinery and vice versa. mmap and arena primitives beyond what asupersync region heaps provide live in fln-unsafe-region with ledger rows (D3).

Acceptance: a real mathlib olean region loads via mmap with relocation and is traversable as live objects (G0-1 promotion to production code); page-sharing verified across two consumers of one loaded region (RSS accounting shows shared pages, the PG-4/PG-6 mechanism); immutability violations trap in hardened builds. Tests: relocate-or-copy fallback forced and verified; corrupted-relocation-table fuzzing under resource budgets (hostile olean is a DoS surface even in safe Rust — R18); crash-during-construction drills leaving no half-published region (atomic staging).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: One compacted-region implementation owns object-graph construction, relocation-table emission/validation, immutable persistent marking, mmap loading, relocate-or-copy fallback, page sharing and atomic publication; the olean codec uses this exact path. Every pointer/offset/tag/length/reachability/allocation/expanded-weight boundary is validated under budgets before live exposure. A real pinned mathlib olean region loads through mmap and is traversable as Marrow objects; two consumers share immutable pages with retained RSS/page evidence. Fallback yields identical objects/roots, and hardened writes to immutable regions fail typed. Corrupt/truncated/future relocation data, unavailable mmap, cancellation, resource exhaustion and crash during construction leave no visible half region and recover cleanly. All project-authored memory-map primitives remain isolated and ledgered in fln-unsafe-region with structural no-authority laws. Unit/property/model/fuzz/mutation tests cover layout/relocation permutations, aliasing, fallback, sharing, immutability, budgets and every promotion boundary; named wrong-relocation, bounds-skip, private-copy-disguised-as-share, partial-publish and unsafe-launder mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E performs load/share/fallback/corruption/fault/restoration and retains strict region/page/root/process/final-state bundles. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, unsafe/dependency/codec/fuzz/fault/platform lanes pass. No second codec region path, unchecked relocation, copy presented as sharing, mock-only mmap, mutable published region, partial publication, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.

title: W1: Windows functional CI — early platform honesty, certification deferred
description:
Stand up windows-x86-64 functional CI from W1 (§18.3, D17): windows is FUNCTIONAL from the start (build, unit tests, the growing conformance subset), while bit-certification under --reproducible remains a separate declared decision made later (the manim precedent).

Why early: platform assumptions fossilize fast — path handling, UTF-16 position conversions at the LSP boundary, dlopen vs LoadLibrary in the plugin door, mmap semantics in region loading, process spawning in the D2 subprocess protocol. Discovering these at W12 would mean re-opening frozen interfaces. The certified matrix (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64) runs the determinism closure; windows runs the functional lanes and reports honestly in the Parity Ledger platform column.

Acceptance: CI matrix includes windows building the workspace and running the unit + conformance-smoke lanes; platform-conditional code carries explicit capability errors (never silent substitution); the Parity Ledger platform column reports windows rows at their honest L-levels. Tests: the CI lane itself, plus a fixture asserting that platform-gated features fail closed with typed CapabilityDenied errors rather than degrading silently.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. The lane exercises Windows paths and separators, Unicode/UTF-16 LSP conversions, dynamic-library loading, mapped-region semantics, D2 subprocess behavior, atomic replace/rename, cancellation, and process exit/signal equivalents. title: W5: FIR pipeline — validated IR stages, pass witnesses, RC/FBIP disciplines
description:
Implement the FIR pipeline (fln-comp, §11.1): elaborated terms -> FIR (ANF/LCNF-class IR keeping the Reference-established compilation disciplines: specialization of instance/closed arguments, simplification, closure conversion, explicit boxing, reference-count insertion with borrow inference and constructor-reuse/FBIP analysis — kept because FBIP-style reuse is the performance model the ecosystem code is written against and is observable through performance cliffs) -> FLBC register bytecode.

Engineering law: every pipeline stage has a versioned IR schema, a deterministic printer, and a validator; every optimization pass is a pure ValidatedIr -> (ValidatedIr, PassWitness) transformation with declared preconditions, a preservation argument or executable validator, a resource-growth bound, deterministic ordering, and a fallback to its input. The RC-insertion pass runs an ownership validator (no use-after-consume, balanced joins and exception edges, capture correctness, ABI ownership contracts) deliberately SIMPLER than the optimizer it audits. Executable-closure selection records WHY each declaration is retained (direct call, closure capture, initializer, reflection, exported symbol, conservative opaque edge) so dead-code questions have answers.

Acceptance: the G0-3 micro-corpus compiles through the full pipeline with every pass witness validating; compiled results observably identical to interpretation of the unoptimized form (pass-preservation differential). Tests: IR printer/parser round-trips; ownership-validator mutation tests (a planted dropped-retain must be caught); pass-ordering determinism at {1,8,32} threads.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OQ-4 HANDOFF — this FIR implementation depends on fln-iron-target-profile-contract-ba9 and must implement its selected target-independent lowering and machine-boundary contract. FIR validation and pass witnesses must reject backend-specific leakage not authorized by that contract; any incompatible change reopens the decision bead rather than creating a second implicit backend interface.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement the complete deterministic elaborated-term to FIR to FLBC pipeline with versioned schemas, parsers/printers, and validators for every stage, preserving specialization, simplification, closure conversion, boxing, RC insertion with borrow inference, constructor reuse/FBIP analysis, and executable-closure selection with explicit retention reasons.
2. Every optimization is a pure ValidatedIr to ValidatedIr plus PassWitness transformation declaring preconditions, preservation evidence or executable validation, resource-growth bound, deterministic ordering, and failure fallback to its input. The independent ownership validator checks consumes, joins, exception edges, captures, and ABI ownership contracts and remains structurally simpler than the optimizer it audits.
3. Named unit/property/metamorphic/differential/mutation suites cover schema compatibility, printer/parser round trips, validator rejection, pass witnesses and fallback, closure-retention reasons, RC/borrow/FBIP edges, exception paths, resource bounds, cancellation, and compiled optimized behavior equal to unoptimized interpretation. Dropped-retain, use-after-consume, bad join, missing capture, false-retention, and pass-order mutants must be killed.
4. G0-3 micro-corpus programs compile through every stage and validate every witness. Pipeline outputs, diagnostics, witnesses, and FLBC are bit-identical at thread counts 1, 8, and 32 for the same inputs and profile.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane compiles and executes representative user-class programs and a real plugin, compares optimized FLBC results/effects with unoptimized interpretation, injects a planted ownership defect and rejected pass, verifies input fallback, and then proves clean recovery. Human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON include run/program/stage/pass/witness ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/cwd, schema/build/profile/thread/seed facts, canonical IR/program/output hashes, retention/ownership decisions, expected/actual, growth/resource/timing/status facts, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives stage IRs, witnesses, validator reports, deterministic thread matrices, mutant results, and evidence manifests; close evidence names every suite and may not claim semantic preservation, ownership safety, or performance from mocked execution.
