Adversarial review tightened the design: default RequireSource must still fail when .beads is missing; explicit ManifestOnly is permitted only with both the projection digest and exact manifest-artifact digest supplied from a source-bound local preflight, and a postflight source recheck closes concurrent drift. No automatic missing-source fallback. Rust loader edits are waiting on OliveAnchor's active broad crates/fln-conformance/** reservation; high-priority narrow/release request 6397 is pending, and active kernel-replay edits show the lane is not abandoned, so no takeover is justified. scripts/check.sh and scripts/evidence.py remain excluded by current user direction.

title: Reconcile plan §15 '(fln-trace, fln-graph)' vs §21 crate map (no fln-graph crate)
description:
Plan §15's section title names two crates — fln-trace, fln-graph — but the authoritative cycle-free crate map in §21 (and AGENTS.md's layering chain) lists only fln-trace; the workspace scaffold (bead fln-8mj) follows §21. Either §15's title is stale (Palimpsest's causal-graph store lives inside fln-trace on franken_networkx per §15.3c) and the plan text should drop fln-graph, or a real fln-graph crate is intended and must be added to §21's map + WORKSPACE_GRAPH.txt with a rank and charter. Per the spec-first workflow, amend the plan first, then the workspace. One-line decision; blocks nothing today.

## Acceptance Criteria

The plan is amended before any workspace change. §15 names only the selected owner crate (`fln-trace`); §15.3c still states that Palimpsest stores the causal graph in process using franken_networkx over frankensqlite journals and preserves the optional frankengraphdb export lane; §21, AGENTS.md, ci/WORKSPACE_GRAPH.txt, and the scaffold all agree that no `fln-graph` crate exists. A repository-wide literal search finds no stale `fln-graph` reference. This is a documentation-only architecture decision, so code unit/E2E tests are not applicable; verification is the exact-text search plus cross-document crate-map consistency review recorded in the close reason.
acceptance_criteria: The plan is amended before any workspace change. §15 names only the selected owner crate (`fln-trace`); §15.3c still states that Palimpsest stores the causal graph in process using franken_networkx over frankensqlite journals and preserves the optional frankengraphdb export lane; §21, AGENTS.md, ci/WORKSPACE_GRAPH.txt, and the scaffold all agree that no `fln-graph` crate exists. A repository-wide literal search finds no stale `fln-graph` reference. This is a documentation-only architecture decision, so code unit/E2E tests are not applicable; the verification is the exact-text search plus cross-document crate-map consistency review recorded in the close reason.
close_reason: Decision: no fln-graph crate. §15.3c already states the causal graph lives in-process on franken_networkx over frankensqlite-backed journals — i.e., inside Palimpsest (fln-trace); §21's authoritative crate map and the fln-8mj scaffold agree. Amended §15's section title to '(fln-trace)' — the single fln-graph reference in the repo (verified by grep). Docs-only decision bead; no code, no workspace change needed. The optional frankengraphdb export remains the frontier lane per §15.3c.

title: W11: Leapfrog surfaces — Bloodhound, Folio, Envoy, terminal InfoView, WASM Judge
description:
Workstream W11 "Leapfrog surfaces" (§16): the products that make FrankenLean more than parity — each strictly downstream of the trust core, each degradable without touching the trust story.

Bloodhound (fln-hound, frankensearch): in-toolchain hybrid lexical+semantic search over the live environment, indexed incrementally at Ledger commit; exact?/apply?-class premise retrieval as a candidate generator whose candidates are VERIFIED by actual elaboration (ranking is UX, proofs are proofs); CandidateCards in three grades (initial/refined/verified); proof repair consuming the causal graph (diff environments, find earliest failed constraint cell, hole the blame slice, search replacements, validate through the full pipeline, emit patch transaction with rollback — the migration autopilot engine); counterfactual analysis and minimization along declared objective vectors. Folio (fln-doc, franken_markdown+fmd-math): doc-gen4-surface-compatible docs as an incremental Ledger facet — native HTML/PDF, native math, receipt-bound pages that go stale honestly rather than lying. Envoy (fln-mcp, fastmcp_rust): the agent door — ProofStateSnapshot as the round-trippable session primitive; budgeted, macaroon-scoped tools (goal_state, apply_tactic, search_lemmas, check_term, eval, get_certificate, ledger_query, trace_query); transactional multi-agent orchestration (patch proposals, never direct trusted mutations; mission receipts with rollback roots). Terminal InfoView (fln goals over frankentui). The WASM Judge (§16.4b): K1 + fln-checker compiled to WASM — sealed-capsule verification in a browser tab, the public face of the trust story.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
acceptance_criteria: W11 closes only when all five leapfrog products ship as independently usable, evidence-linked surfaces downstream of the trust core. Bloodhound incrementally indexes the live Ledger with deterministic hybrid retrieval; every CandidateCard is explicitly initial, refined, or verified, and apply/exact/proof-repair candidates become trusted only after full elaboration and kernel checking. Proof repair uses causal blame, validates replacements end to end, emits rollback-capable patch transactions, and never edits trusted state directly; counterfactual/minimization objectives are declared. Folio generates doc-gen-compatible incremental HTML/PDF with native math, receipt-bound freshness, accessibility, and no external LaTeX/browser service. Envoy exposes versioned macaroon-scoped MCP tools over immutable ProofStateSnapshots, typed budgets, transactional multi-agent proposals, mission receipts, revocation, and rollback roots; search/ranking and eval never widen authority. fln goals uses the same Lantern snapshot/RPC truth as editors, supports SSH/tmux/narrow/no-color/assistive text, and keeps ANSI out of robot schemas. The WASM Judge verifies sealed capsules offline in stock supported browsers with K1 plus the independent checker, integrity-bound assets, native/WASM vector identity, explicit TCB/resource/version facts, accessible failures, and no network service. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation suites cover ranking verification, repair rollback, document freshness, MCP auth/capabilities, terminal rendering/generation, capsule decoding/integrity/browser limits, cancellation/resources, and independent product failure; named provisional-to-verified, stale-page, authority-escalation, ANSI-leak, and browser-asset-mismatch mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E runs search/repair, docs, MCP sessions, PTY terminal, and offline browser capsule verification, then injects one failure at a time and proves exact recovery: index rebuild without false verification, stale-page regeneration, revoked/expired capability refusal followed by a fresh authorized session, terminal disconnect/reconnect from the same snapshot, browser worker/refresh restart from the sealed capsule, and one product failure never masquerading as another success. Strict combined release bundles retain each product identity/evidence tier, failure and recovery transition, separate bounded streams, hashes, resources, cleanup, and final state in human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts that validate independently. All W11 children and terminal/WASM join, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, accessibility/security/browser/editor/platform/fault/performance lanes are green before G6. No candidate ranking presented as proof, direct trusted mutation, stale receipt hidden, mock-only MCP/browser/editor, terminal substitute for WASM, core-only WASM substitute for browser packaging, network-required verification, or deferred listed W11 feature satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W6: Athanor, Synod & the Mirror — elaborator, instances, scheduler, facade
description:
Workstream W6 "Athanor, Synod & the Native Mirror" (§10, §4.3): the elaborator — the program-defining workstream carrying the two Critical risks (R1 Meta-API dependence, R2 unifier fidelity tail).

Scope: the monadic tower semantics (CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM state shapes) as NativeHeap structures with explicit ElabTxn transactions (five outcomes, leak-audited rollback) and an explicit metavariable dependency graph with targeted wake-up; unification/defeq mirroring the Reference approximation ladder EXACTLY, implemented against the instrumented-oracle golden decision traces (§18.3) — the fidelity burden is entirely ours (no hosted fallback exists); term elaboration (coercions, binders, structure instances, match compilation matching generated-name conventions — metaprograms pattern-match on them, they are API; deriving; recursion elaborators lowering to exactly the kernel-visible constructions); Synod instance resolution (Reference semantics — priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, defaults, tabling — behind an indexed relational engine emitting SearchCards + TerminationCards); the NATIVE tactic framework (goal management, evalTactic dispatch, core tactic set behind Mirror facade rows); the deterministic dataflow scheduler (Bet B4: speculative parallel elaboration, canonical-order commit, effect summaries validated by perturbation — FL-INV-01 by construction); the builtin census + generated Mirror facade v1 (census totality: no unclassified symbol, ever — R1 mitigation).

Exit: gate G3 "The Mirror Holds" — Init/Std elaborate end-to-end from source with T2 identity, stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint bit-stable, metaprogram-corpus rig green at declared L-levels, ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out check passes.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — interpret core tactic set behind facade rows by stage: W6 completes the native framework and bootstrap-safe primitive rows; W7 supplies simp/norm_num/omega/bv_decide/grind and their full facade integration. The W6 epic may close with those future engine-backed rows at an explicit L0/L1 only if their generated shapes, typed Unsupported behavior, owners, fixtures, and promotion prerequisites are present. No placeholder success result or hidden oracle is permitted.

Closure topology: all W6 components feed the strengthened franken_lean-26y integration rig; that rig feeds Gate G3; this epic depends on G3 as its declared exit while retaining every component obligation. W6 consumes the proven G2 runtime rather than the broader W5 aggregation epic. This removes empty umbrella hops without weakening parser, kernel, Golem, elaborator, scheduler, Mirror, T2/T3, fixpoint, or Oracle-Only evidence.
acceptance_criteria: W6 closes only when Athanor, Synod, deterministic scheduling, and the Native Mirror elaborate pinned Init/Std from source and drive real metaprograms through Golem with no hosted upstream implementation. CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM states are exact typed native structures; ElabTxn has five explicit outcomes and leak-audited rollback; metavariable dependencies wake only affected work. Unification and definitional equality reproduce the complete Reference approximation ladder against versioned golden decision traces, including fuel and transparency behavior. Term elaboration covers coercions, binders, structures, matches, deriving, recursion, diagnostics, InfoTrees, and exact generated names. Synod preserves priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, defaults, tabling, termination and negative-cache keys while emitting replayable SearchCards and TerminationCards. The native tactic framework and bootstrap-safe primitives preserve goal/TacticM state; engine-backed W7 rows remain explicit L0/L1 Unsupported until their owning integration promotes them, never fake success. The builtin census and generated facade are total, typed, evidence-leveled, and reject missing/stale/unknown rows. Speculative declaration work commits only in canonical order under validated effect summaries and registered CGSE policies, with identical outputs at 1, 8, and 32 threads. Unit/property/model/trace-replay/mutation suites cover every monad transaction, mvar graph edge, unifier approximation, elaborator form, instance rule, tactic transition, facade row, schedule/effect class, cancellation, and resource boundary; named mutants kill rollback leaks, stale wakeups/caches, search-order drift, generated-name drift, hidden oracle paths, and schedule-dependent publication. The real no-mock Mirror rig executes Init/Std and curated mathlib tactics, compares environments/InfoTrees/diagnostics/names/effects, proves the double-elaboration fixpoint and ORACLE_FALLBACK absence, and retains strict positive/divergence/fault/recovery bundles. G3, all W6 children and integration rig, scripts/check.sh, full cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, differential/trace/Mirror/thread/mutation/fault/performance lanes are green. No Lean.Elab source standing in for native implementation, unclassified facade symbol, placeholder tactic success, mock-only tactic corpus, schedule-sensitive output, normalized-away divergence, or deferred listed W6 feature satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W12: fln library crate — the embeddable prover, capability-first API
description:
Implement the fln embeddable library crate (§17.2, the library door): embed the prover — parse, elaborate, check, query — in Rust programs with the SAME engine and the same guarantees as the toolchain binaries; verification inside build tools, CI services, and agents becomes a Cargo.toml line, the posture the Reference architecture makes awkward (process-spawning, stage0, C toolchain) and this one makes trivial.

API design laws: capability-first — an embedder hands the engine a Cx and receives determinism, cancellation, and budget enforcement STRUCTURALLY (no global state, no ambient authority; the embedder controls filesystem/network/clock exposure); typed errors per the D8 taxonomy crossing the API boundary as versioned values, never panics (a panic across the embedding boundary is an invariant failure); the toolchain binaries (fln-cli) are thin shells over this crate so the library IS the tested product, not a parallel implementation; epoch selection explicit (Engine::builder().toolchain_epoch(...)); results carry receipts and logical roots so embedders can persist and re-verify.

Acceptance: an example embedder (checked into examples/) parses, elaborates, and kernel-checks a small project and retrieves a receipt, under a restricted Cx that denies network — demonstrated in CI; fln-cli refactored to consume the library surface; API documented with the semver-per-epoch policy. Tests: embedding e2e test with detailed logging (each API call logged with inputs/outcomes); a panic-containment test (an induced internal fault surfaces as InternalFault, never unwinds across the boundary); Cx-denial tests (denied capabilities produce typed CapabilityDenied); thread-safety smoke under the lab runtime.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — this bead owns the complete capability-first library API and conformance tests over the real parser, Golem, elaborator/kernel, and Ledger engines. It exposes the API that toolchain front doors must consume, but does not claim to refactor nonexistent binaries. franken_lean-eb3 depends on this bead and proves that every CLI personality is a thin shell over the library with a structural no-bypass check.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Expose a documented Rust API for parse, elaborate, check, environment/query, receipts, and logical roots using the same engine/configuration as toolchain binaries; fln-cli is a thin consumer rather than a parallel implementation. Epoch, mode, profile, consensus policy, and resource budgets are explicit builder inputs with semver-per-epoch policy.
2. Every entry requires an embedder-supplied Cx/capability set; no global mutable engine state or ambient filesystem/network/clock/process authority is consulted. Cancellation, deadlines, memory/weight/heartbeat limits, and deterministic scheduling are structural and propagate typed outcomes.
3. All user/malformed/resource/capability failures cross the public boundary as versioned typed D8 taxonomy values with source/evidence context. Internal invariant faults are contained and reported as InternalFault; no unwind/panic escapes the embedding boundary and no partial declaration publishes. Results bind receipts and environment/logical roots suitable for persistence and independent revalidation.
4. Named unit/property/model/DPOR/fuzz/mutation suites cover every builder/API operation, configuration/epoch mismatch, capability allow/deny, cancellation/resource limits, concurrent isolated engines, deterministic outputs, receipt/root binding, malformed inputs, panic containment, and transaction rollback. Ambient-authority, CLI/library-drift, escaped-panic, partial-publish, and hidden-default-epoch mutants must be killed.
5. A checked-in real example embedder uses the published crate API to parse, elaborate, kernel-check, query, persist/reload, and retrieve/reverify a receipt for a small project under restricted Cx with network denied. A shared no-mock E2E compares its outputs/diagnostics/roots with the real CLI, triggers denial and internal-fault containment, runs concurrent sessions under the lab, and proves clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/embedder/API-call/engine/transaction/declaration/receipt ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted arguments/cwd, crate/build/epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget/capability facts, canonical input/environment/output/receipt roots, expected/actual typed outcomes, timings/resources/status, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI builds docs/examples and archives API/schema goldens, CLI equivalence matrices, DPOR schedules, denial/fault artifacts, mutant outcomes, and E2E manifests. No mock engine or process-spawn wrapper can satisfy the embeddable-same-engine contract.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):

title: G0-3: Golem execution spike — user-class code + real plugin on ABI-valued VM
description:
G0-3 Golem execution spike (§22.1-3, feeds §11): execute REAL user-class compiled Lean code end-to-end on the prototype VM — an #eval/IO/Task parity micro-corpus compiled from source by the prototype pipeline, plus loading a precompiled Reference-built plugin at the pin — through the prototype intrinsic table operating on ABI values.

The claim being priced: FLBC values ARE Marrow ABI objects, so interpreted code, native intrinsics, and Reference-built plugins share one calling convention with zero conversion. If that identity does not hold in practice (hidden boxing mismatches, RC discipline drift at the call boundary, initializer ordering surprises), the whole membrane design must be amended BEFORE W5/W6 freeze interfaces.

Acceptance: (a) the parity micro-corpus (pure computation, Nat/String/Array ops, closures, IO println/file ops, Task spawn/bind/get ordering, panics, partial non-termination guard) produces observably identical results to the Reference running the same corpus; (b) a real precompiled plugin dlopens, resolves lean_* imports against prototype Marrow exports, runs its initializers in Reference order, and its functions are callable from interpreted code; (c) RC balance verified (no leak, no double-free) under the debug ownership shadow. Tests: the corpus + plugin harness become the seed of the C4 native-ABI probe family and the PG-7 interpreter benchmark baseline.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: A real user-code micro-corpus compiled from source executes end to end on prototype FIR/FLBC whose registers are actual Marrow ABI values. Pure Nat/String/Array operations, closures, IO printing/files, Task spawn/bind/get ordering, panics, exception paths, partial-nontermination guards, cancellation, and budgets match the pinned Reference observably. A real precompiled Reference plugin loads through the platform loader, resolves lean exports from prototype Marrow, runs initializers in Reference order, and is callable from interpreted code with zero conversion layer. Debug ownership shadowing and soak evidence prove balanced retain/release, no leak, double free, stale use, boxing/tag drift, or partial initialization across interpreted/intrinsic/plugin boundaries. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover ABI calls, object/RC transitions, intrinsic signatures, task/effect order, initializer DAG, plugin failure, and recovery; named boxing, ownership, init-order, hidden-conversion, and fallback mutants are killed. The real no-mock C4 E2E compares the corpus to Reference, loads the plugin, injects malformed/missing symbol/crash/resource/cancellation cases, restores exact state, and retains strict output, root, ownership, process, cleanup, and final-state bundles. The result either ratifies the one-calling-convention membrane or records an amended interface decision before W5/W6 freeze; dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and platform lanes pass. No host-language execution substitute, mock plugin, upstream runtime fallback, hidden value conversion, leak accepted as prototype debt, or deferred listed spike feature satisfies closure.

title: W4: Error recovery + speculative boundaries — repair without changing acceptance
description:
Implement error recovery + the speculative boundary map (§9.3): specified recovery — recovery productions per syntax category, resynchronization tokens, and the RULE that recovery may add diagnostics but never change acceptance. Two boundary products serve the editor: the exact command stream (Reference parser semantics, sole authority over meaning) and a speculative boundary map (a conservative scanner recovering enough structure after malformed edits to schedule work and show syntax diagnostics); speculative boundaries can never commit declarations, and disagreement between the two is journaled as a recovery event.

One law rules everything: no recovered node reaches Athanor without an explicit RecoveredSyntax marker, and no declaration dependent on an unacknowledged repair is ever published (Behavior Note BN-05: extra diagnostics after a syntax error are possible in sound mode; acceptance is unchanged; faithful mode reproduces upstream recovery behavior).

Plus sub-command incremental relex/reparse feeding Lantern snapshots (§14.3): edits inside a command re-lex and re-parse below command granularity where the GrammarEpoch permits. Acceptance: malformed-input corpus keeps serving diagnostics/completions downstream of errors; acceptance-invariance verified (recovery on/off changes no accept/reject verdict on the corpus); incremental reparse products identical to full reparse on the C8 edit sequences. Tests: fuzzed malformed inputs (grammar-mutation fuzzing) never panic and never change acceptance; recovery-event journaling fixtures; RecoveredSyntax-marker propagation tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Preserve two distinct products: the exact command stream is the sole semantic authority, while the conservative speculative boundary map may schedule/editor-highlight only and can never commit a declaration. Every disagreement is journaled with both products and source spans.
2. Recovery productions and resynchronization are specified per syntax category. Every recovered node carries an explicit RecoveredSyntax marker through downstream handling, and no declaration depending on an unacknowledged repair is published. Recovery on versus off changes no accept/reject result; faithful mode reproduces Reference recovery behavior and sound mode may add only documented BN-05 diagnostics.
3. Sub-command relex/reparse respects GrammarEpoch and produces byte-/structure-equivalent authoritative results to a full reparse for all certified C8 edit sequences; stale or epoch-mismatched memo entries cannot publish.
4. Named unit/property/fuzz/mutation suites cover every recovery category, marker propagation, speculative/authoritative disagreement, malformed and adversarial input without panic, acceptance invariance, incremental/full equivalence, cancellation/resource exhaustion as Inconclusive, and failure-atomic snapshot publication. Mutants that publish speculative or unmarked repaired nodes must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock editor/daemon E2E scenario applies real malformed edit sequences, verifies downstream diagnostics/completions, compares recovery on/off acceptance and incremental/full products, exercises a planted invalid repair and restart/recovery, and confirms no repaired declaration escaped publication. Concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON include run/request/snapshot/edit/epoch ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, source/input/product hashes, expected/actual acceptance and diagnostics, disagreement/repair markers, threads/seed/budgets/timings/status, artifacts, cleanup, and asserted final generation.
6. CI archives deterministic fixtures, edit sequences, journals, mutation results, and evidence manifests; close evidence names each suite and no editor compatibility or acceptance claim is earned from mocked boundaries.

title: G0-6: Fuel-parity spike — calibrate the heartbeat law tick-for-tick
description:
G0-6 Fuel-parity spike (§22.1-6, feeds §4.2/§6.3/§8.5): calibrate the heartbeat-counting law — upstream counts heartbeats in allocation-linked units (maxHeartbeats default 200000, units of thousands) — on an instrumented corpus until faithful-mode timeout behavior matches the Reference tick-for-tick, and document any irreducible divergence class.

Why it matters: determinism of FAILURE is part of determinism. Mathlib and downstream CI depend on maxHeartbeats/maxRecDepth limits firing on the same inputs; a reimplementation that times out differently silently changes which proofs are accepted in practice. The heartbeat hook lives in the owned allocator (§6.3), which is why the allocator is not an off-the-shelf component. This spike also prices risk R11 (heartbeat parity chaotic at fine grain) and resolves OQ-2 (the achievable tolerance class).

Acceptance: (a) an instrumented corpus where Reference heartbeat consumption is captured per declaration (instrumented-oracle apparatus); (b) prototype counting law reproduces timeout/no-timeout verdicts on every corpus entry, with tick deltas quantified; (c) a declared tolerance class (exact / bounded-drift / law-level parity per BN-08) ratified into the plan; (d) maxRecDepth accounting verified on deep-recursion fixtures. Tests: fuel-parity fixtures join the faithful-mode differential rig; any future allocator change must keep them green.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: A manifest-bound instrumented corpus records pinned Reference heartbeat and maxRecDepth consumption per declaration, including exact-bound, one-under/over, allocator-heavy, reduction-heavy, recursion-heavy, cancellation, and fault cases. The prototype allocator/kernel counting seam reproduces timeout versus completion on every row, records exact tick deltas without saturation/wrap, and never turns exhaustion into rejection or acceptance. OQ-2 closes with a reviewed exact, bounded-drift, or law-level tolerance class, quantitative evidence, affected modes, rendering/cache rules, Behavior Note BN-08 implications, and an interface decision before allocator/kernel freeze; faithful claims cannot exceed the class. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation tests cover charging sites, batching, nested allocation, recursion, thread partitions, boundaries, cancellation, and recovery; named dropped/double charge, overflow, schedule-dependent count, timeout-promotion, and hidden-clock mutants are killed. The permanent real no-mock fuel E2E compares Reference and prototype across 1/8/32 threads, plants charge-law mutations, restores exact inputs, and retains strict counters, verdicts, roots, processes, cleanup, and final-state evidence. Future allocator changes run the same lane. Dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and differential/thread/resource lanes pass. No timing proxy, verdict-only aggregate without tick data, silent tolerance widening, sound-mode result presented as faithful, Inconclusive collapse, unresolved OQ-2 branch, or deferred listed requirement satisfies closure.

title: W11: Envoy — MCP tools, ProofStateSnapshot, macaroon authority, multi-agent orchestration
description:
Implement Envoy (fln-mcp, §16.3): the agent door — MCP tools over the Lantern session API: goal_state, apply_tactic (against forked snapshots, budgeted), search_lemmas (Bloodhound), check_term, eval (capability-scoped), get_certificate, ledger_query, trace_query (Palimpsest). Budgets (heartbeats, wall, memory, expanded weight) are first-class parameters; every tool result carries provenance; read tools are sound-mode; write-to-file tools are explicit and default-off.

The session primitive: ProofStateSnapshot — a versioned, schema-registered, canonical serialization of an elaboration point (goals with local contexts, metavariable assignments, environment logical root, journal position, option set) — ROUND-TRIPPABLE (a snapshot IS a resumable state), stable across releases, structurally shared with the live store. Authority is macaroon-scoped (asupersync tokens): a credential names readable environment roots, the budget envelope, the consensus policy it may request, and whether write-tools exist — attenuable delegation, every check receipted. Robot responses carry schema/epoch/profile versions, request + snapshot ids, resource facts, data grade (provisional/verified), evidence links; mutating tools carry idempotency keys + preconditions.

On top: transactional multi-agent orchestration — fork a branch per agent, assign disjoint impact regions from Palimpsest cones, stream scoped context packs, accept PATCH PROPOSALS (never direct trusted mutations), re-elaborate + kernel-check each, detect semantic conflicts via §15.3b, merge deterministically, emit a mission receipt with a rollback root — agent success measured by accepted kernel-checked commits. Plus the remote check service (sealed capsule delta in, full-policy consensus verdict + signed transparency-logged receipt out; workers may lie, results validate locally). Acceptance: the tool surface validated against a frozen JSON schema contract; snapshot round-trips bit-stable; a two-agent orchestration scenario end-to-end with a semantic-conflict block demonstrated. Tests: budget-exhaustion typed results (FL-INV-07 over the wire); macaroon attenuation fixtures (a narrowed token cannot widen); idempotency/precondition replay tests; schema-freeze contract tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OQ-11 HANDOFF — Envoy depends explicitly on franken_lean-05g and consumes its semantic-merge v1 allowlist, effect/read-write/generated-name proof rules, replay requirements, and mandatory human-resolution boundary. Multi-agent orchestration may propose patches, but it must re-elaborate and kernel-check them and must block any semantic conflict outside the proven commutative subset; no agent vote or textual cleanliness can bypass that dependency.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Expose versioned MCP contracts for goal_state, apply_tactic, search_lemmas, check_term, capability-scoped eval, get_certificate, ledger_query, and trace_query over the real Lantern/session engines. Heartbeat, wall, memory, expanded-weight, cancellation, and output bounds are first-class; read tools are sound-mode and file-mutating tools are explicit, separately authorized, preconditioned, and default-off. 2. ProofStateSnapshot canonically and round-trippably serializes goals/local contexts, metavariable assignments, environment logical root, journal position, options, epoch/profile/schema, and evidence links. Resume preserves semantics and stable identity across supported schema releases while sharing immutable live storage; malformed/stale/foreign snapshots fail typed without publication. 3. Macaroons scope readable roots, tools, budget envelope, consensus policy, expiry, and write authority; attenuation can only narrow and every authorization decision is receipted. Robot results include schema/epoch/profile, request/snapshot ids, resource facts, provisional/verified grade, roots, and evidence; mutations require idempotency keys and atomic preconditions. 4. Orchestration consumes the exact versioned OQ-11 effect-class decision from franken_lean-05g, forks isolated branches, assigns impact regions, streams scoped context, accepts patch proposals only, re-elaborates and kernel-checks each, and auto-merges only rows proven commutative under satisfied Complete/Conservative preconditions. Proposal-only, human-required, unknown, Opaque, and weak-evidence effects block automatic application with a typed explanation. Successful deterministic merges emit a mission receipt plus rollback root. Remote check workers consume sealed deltas and return signed full-policy receipts that are locally validated; workers are never trusted. 5. Named unit/property/model/DPOR/fuzz/mutation suites cover every schema/tool, snapshot round-trip/upgrade/resume, budget exhaustion as Inconclusive, macaroon attenuation/expiry/root scope, idempotency/preconditions, branch isolation, every OQ-11 merge/refusal class, deterministic merge/conflict, rollback, and remote-result validation. Authority-widening, direct-mutation, unchecked-patch, unknown-effect-auto-merge, weak-grade-merge, stale-precondition, and trust-worker mutants must be killed. 6. A shared no-mock E2E lane uses real MCP clients against Envoy for every tool, round-trips and resumes snapshots, proves narrowed credentials cannot widen, runs real concurrent agent branches through one eligible safe merge and proposal-only/human-required/unknown-effect blocks, validates a remote-check receipt, injects failure, rolls back, and confirms recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/client/request/tool/snapshot/token/branch/patch/effect-class/mission/receipt ids, OQ-11 version, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted args/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical input/environment/output/rollback roots, authorization/grade/kernel/merge decisions, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state. 7. CI archives frozen schemas, conformance transcripts, snapshot corpora, DPOR schedules, the consumed OQ-11 table/hash, auth/remote fixtures, mission receipts, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. Mocked clients/workers may support units but cannot close MCP, authority, orchestration, semantic-merge, or remote-trust claims.

title: W2: stage0 ABI gauntlet — upstream generated C runs the runtime suite against Marrow
description:
Stand up the stage0 ABI gauntlet as a standing Tribunal lane (§6.6, §18.2): compile the Reference own stage0-generated C (test apparatus only, via the optional D2 cc) against Marrow exported lean_* surface, and run the upstream runtime test suite through the membrane.

Why this is the strongest ABI rig available: stage0 is the exact code the ecosystem ships — millions of lines of generated C exercising every corner of the object model, RC discipline, boxing rules, and exported function census. If the membrane is wrong ANYWHERE, upstream own code says so, loudly and early, with a concrete failing call site. This displaces guesswork about ABI corner cases with an executable oracle. Note the D8 boundary: stage0 here is TEST APPARATUS (fixture corpus), never a runtime component; the lane runs in the Tribunal sandbox and its products are conformance evidence only.

Acceptance: the gauntlet builds and links against Marrow on linux-x86-64 first (other platforms staged); the upstream runtime suite passes on the implemented ABI subset with every failure triaged to a contract row (fix or honest Unsupported status); the lane runs in CI on membrane-touching changes. Tests: this IS the test; additionally a seeded-defect mutation (perturb one exported function ownership convention — the gauntlet must catch it, proving the lane kills mutants per §18.2).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: The Tribunal compiles the pinned Reference stage0-generated C through the sanctioned D2 compiler capsule against Marrow exports and runs the upstream runtime suite as test apparatus only. The exact source/archive/compiler hashes, generated contract root, platform, commands and ABI symbol/ownership coverage are retained; no stage0 product enters release binaries or runtime dependencies. Linux x86-64 is complete first and every additional platform has an explicit matrix status. Every implemented-subset failure maps to one contract/census row and is fixed or remains precise Unsupported; no pass aggregate hides rows. Unit/harness/mutation tests validate build/link/run classification, symbol coverage, output bounds, interruption/failure cleanup and evidence parsing; named ownership, tag/layout, retain/release and exported-signature defects are each caught by the gauntlet. The real no-mock E2E includes successful suite, planted ABI defect, exact restoration and recovery with strict compiler/source/binary/symbol/process/final-state bundles. CI runs the lane on membrane changes; dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and platform/artifact D8 scans pass. No handwritten ABI substitute, mock generated C, stage0 runtime component, compiler outside D2, unsupported failure counted as pass, unresolved implemented-row failure, or deferred listed platform status satisfies closure.

title: fln-kernel K1: nested-inductive translation — the _nested.* auxiliary block machinery (Lean.Syntax to full ruleset)
description:
Follow-up to franken_lean-ap6, which admits every Init.Prelude declaration kind but applies a documented PARTIAL ruleset to the one nested block (Lean.Syntax, num_nested=1): well-typedness of types/ctors/recursor types/rule RHSes and arity cross-checks run, but strict positivity (KR-606) and recursor regeneration (KR-800..803) are skipped, because both are defined on the pin's auxiliary translation: elim_nested_inductive_fn (inductive.cpp:800-1100) replaces nested occurrences (e.g. Array Syntax) with _nested.* auxiliary types, validates the resulting mutual block, and translates results back (KR-608). Implement that translation so nested blocks get the FULL ruleset: reconstruct the auxiliary mutual block from the decoded post-elaboration rows (the olean does not carry the pre-elaboration declaration — the reconstruction inverts the translation, instantiating the nested type's own spec from the environment), run positivity + regeneration on it, and map the regenerated recursors back for the byte-exact cross-check (Syntax.rec/rec_1/rec_2 with 3 motives). Priority note: Init.Prelude has exactly ONE nested block and it already admits under the partial ruleset with its recursors typechecked — this slice matters at G-scale (mathlib is full of nested inductives), not for the current census. Verification: Syntax block moves from nested_partial_blocks=1 to full admission in the replay census (ratchet the e2e assertion when it lands); unit fixtures for a nested block (e.g. Tree with List Tree field) with byte-exact regenerated recursors; mutation kills on the translation's occurrence-replacement and the translate-back mapping; census + gates per the standard DoD.

## Acceptance Criteria

Complete the pin-anchored nested-inductive auxiliary translation so every nested block, beginning with Lean.Syntax, receives the same full positivity, constructor, universe, recursor-generation, and decoded-rule cross-check as non-nested blocks; no partial-ruleset admission satisfies closure. Unit tests must cover successful single and mutually nested families, nested occurrences under multiple type constructors, parameters and indices, recursive and reflexive shapes, universe and Prop boundaries, malformed translations, exact resource limits, cancellation, InternalFault, failure atomicity, and recovery. Independent model and property tests must verify occurrence replacement, auxiliary naming and hygiene, translate-back identity, canonical ordering, and byte-exact regenerated recursors across insertion and declaration permutations. Named mutants must remove or misroute nested occurrences, alter auxiliary names, skip positivity, trust decoded rules, corrupt motive or minor mapping, and break translate-back; each must be killed by a discriminating test. The real pinned no-mock replay must move nested_partial_blocks from 1 to 0, exercise positive, deliberately invalid, resource, cancellation, and recovery phases at threads 1, 8, and 32, and preserve byte-identical authoritative verdicts, roots, diagnostics, and counters. Retain concise human logs and strict schema-versioned NDJSON with exact input and fixture hashes, expected and actual rule results, first divergence, budgets and consumption, separate bounded streams, authority and cacheability, cleanup, and final state; validator self-tests must reject malformed, extra-field, stale-input, wrong-mutant, merged-stream, and surviving-mutant evidence. No oracle runtime, unchecked or partial fallback, acceptance or rejection of Inconclusive, unsafe code, or covenant overrun satisfies closure. All focused tests, replay mutation and recovery lanes, scripts/check.sh, fmt, cargo test, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, structure and line-count guards, the bc7 matrix must contain zero uncovered nested-inductive rows and all currently runnable zht integration checks that do not require unfinished sibling slices must be green with retained artifacts recorded. Final zht and G1 aggregation remains downstream and is not a circular prerequisite for this child.

title: W11: Folio — native docs facet, receipt-bound pages, incremental rendering
description:
Implement Folio (fln-doc, §16.2): doc-gen4-surface-compatible documentation as a Ledger facet — docstring markdown through franken_markdown, mathematics through fmd-math rendered natively to HTML and PDF (zero browser-side math stack), syntax highlighting via a Quill-fed lexer, cross-reference and instance graphs via franken_networkx, INCREMENTAL (only changed declarations re-render — mathlib docs become minutes, local, offline).

Documents are receipt-bound: a rendered page embeds the logical root and receipt links for every checked excerpt, and a later source change marks the artifact STALE rather than leaving a verified badge lying (the Witness discipline of §20.4 applied to documentation). The frontier lane: franken_manim consumes Palimpsest event sequences to render animated proof explanations (goal transformations under a tactic, unification constraints resolving, induction structure, before/after of a repair) — explanatory projections that link to checked declarations and are never themselves certificates.

Acceptance: doc output for a mathlib slice matches the doc-gen4 surface conventions (URLs, anchors, structure) so existing links and tooling survive; incremental re-render touches only the changed-declaration cone; receipt-staleness demonstrated (edit a source, the page marks stale). Tests: rendering golden tests (HTML structure + PDF layout smoke); math-rendering fixtures vs known TeX corpus; incremental-facet metamorphic tests (doc-irrelevant edits re-render nothing); link-integrity checks across the generated set.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Generate doc-gen4-surface-compatible URLs, anchors, page structure, cross references, instance graphs, and searchable metadata as a Ledger facet. Render docstring markdown with franken_markdown, TeX mathematics with native fmd-math to HTML and PDF, and syntax highlighting from Quill; no browser-side math, LaTeX process, network, or external renderer is required.
2. Every checked excerpt/page embeds its declaration/environment logical roots and receipt/certificate links. Source, proof, dependency, epoch, or evidence changes invalidate the exact facet cone and mark previously published pages stale before replacement; stale content cannot display a verified badge. Doc-irrelevant edits render nothing.
3. Optional animated explanations consume Palimpsest events and link to checked declarations while being labeled explanatory projections, never certificates or trust evidence. Missing/partial events degrade honestly without changing page validity.
4. Named unit/property/golden/metamorphic/mutation suites cover markdown/HTML structure, native math and PDF layout, URLs/anchors/links, lexer highlighting, graph data, deterministic rendering, receipt binding/staleness, exact incremental cones, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and atomic publication. Stale-badge, excess-rerender, broken-anchor, external-renderer, and projection-as-proof mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane builds real docs for a mathlib slice fully offline, compares surface conventions with doc-gen4 fixtures, validates HTML/PDF and all links, applies doc-irrelevant and declaration-changing edits, proves exact rerender/staleness behavior, renders an available event animation, injects a broken receipt, and verifies safe recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/facet/page/declaration/render/link/receipt/generation ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget/network facts, canonical source/environment/page/PDF/receipt roots, invalidation/render decisions, expected/actual, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives HTML/PDF goldens, math corpus, link reports, cone witnesses, stale/recovery fixtures, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. No link compatibility, offline, incremental, or verified-page claim closes from mocked rendering or unbound receipts.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W8: Ledger & Lake — content-addressed build fabric, invalidation law, cache
description:
Workstream W8 "Ledger & Lake" (§13): the build fabric (Bet B1) — the environment as a Merkle DAG of content-addressed declarations; builds as memoized queries; the ecosystem quality-of-life complaint #1 (file-granular invalidation) deleted.

Scope: Ledger records (decl_hash over name, elaborated statement, value class, direct dep hashes, options-that-matter, epoch, mode) generalized to demand nodes across five planes (source/language/trust/build/service) with retention tiers T0-T4; the incremental law (§13.2): true-dependency-cone invalidation on recorded edges, early cutoff (unchanged output digest stops the cascade; statement-hash vs value-hash separation), and the published, fixture-tested, perturbation-validated invalidation matrix per change class with conservative fallbacks (FL-INV-05: anything not proven irrelevant is relevant); the Lake surface (lakefile.lean executes on Golem — the config DSL is user code, so Golem IS the compatibility mechanism; lakefile.toml fast path; require via D2 git; exit codes and --json pinned; fln build explain shows Reference-decision vs native-decision); cache federation (CAS sync over atp multi-donor fountain-coded with HTTPS fallback; remote entries are untrusted until validated — malformed entry = miss + security event; fln cache shim preserves community muscle memory); epoch-stable caching + bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) + the migration autopilot (§13.6); watch mode + distributed elaboration (results re-verified by local Crucible — distribution without distributed trust).

Exit feeds gates G4 (mixed-build matrix) and G6 (PG-3 cone, PG-10 cache).

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — W8 closure includes fln-publish-rewrite-indexes-gca so the plan claim that per-library indexes ship through CAS and olean-next is proven at the subsystem boundary. The migration autopilot itself is downstream integration in franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w; W8 provides sound cache/bridge/invalidation primitives without a placeholder repair engine.

Stage topology: G4 consumes the concrete Ledger core needed for full-Corpus builds: records/CAS, FL-INV-05 invalidation and early cutoff, Lake, and their real evidence. Full W8 additionally includes rewrite-index publication, watch/distributed elaboration, cache federation, and epoch bridges, so the epic blocks G6 Ledger GA rather than delaying G4. W8 consumes the G2-proven runtime through its concrete Lake dependency and does not wait on W5 aggregation bookkeeping. Every W8 feature remains mandatory before G6.
acceptance_criteria: W8 closes only when Ledger and Lake implement one deterministic, crash-safe build fabric over a typed Merkle DAG. Declaration and demand-node records bind name, statement/value class, direct dependency hashes, relevant options, epoch, mode, plane, determinism class, evidence, and retention tier across source/language/trust/build/service planes. FL-INV-05 is enforced: the perturbation-validated change-class matrix proves every irrelevant change and conservatively invalidates anything not proven irrelevant; true-cone invalidation, statement-versus-value separation, dominators/SCCs, and digest early cutoff never accept a stale result. CAS promotion and frankensqlite journals are atomic under kill-9, corruption, disk-full, partial download, duplicate work, and restart. Lake runs lakefile.lean on Golem, supports the compatible TOML path and D2 git capsule, preserves CLI/exit/robot schemas, and explains Reference versus native rebuild decisions. Rewrite indexes publish through CAS/olean-next with exact roots. atp multi-donor federation and fallback validate all remote bytes before use; malformed data is a miss plus security event. Watch and distributed elaboration preserve schedule independence, cancellation, local Crucible re-verification, and no distributed trust. Epoch bridges are allowlisted, versioned, conservative, reversible, and never silently repair. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation suites cover record/key identity, retention, invalidation classes, early cutoff, Lake resolution, CAS transactions, remote validation, watch events, distribution, bridge refusal, budgets, and recovery; named stale-hit, dropped-edge, partial-promotion, unverified-remote, and cross-epoch mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E builds mathlib/Lake projects cold and warm, performs leaf and interface edits, publishes/loads indexes, hydrates across clean/lossy paths, injects crashes/corruption/network loss/disk full, and proves exact recovery in strict bundles. G4-required core plus full W8 federation/distribution/bridge scope, all children, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, fault/metamorphic/thread/platform/performance lanes including PG-3/PG-10 are green before G6. No file-cone substitute, heuristic stale acceptance, mock-only cache/Lake, external trust, hidden repair, unvalidated distributed result, unclassified bridge, or deferred listed W8 feature satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W4: GrammarEpoch + memoization — grammar as versioned data, typed parser effects
description:
Implement GrammarEpoch versioning + parse memoization (§9.1): the grammar state as versioned DATA, not hidden globals — a GrammarEpoch content-hashes the active syntax declarations, parser descriptors, precedence tables, scoped notation, token parsers, and relevant options; every parse node binds the epoch it was recognized under; memoization keys on (position, category, precedence, GrammarEpoch); a syntax-changing command invalidates exactly the parse products that read the changed components.

Parser registrations emit typed effect summaries (AddsSyntax, AddsToken, ChangesPrecedence, OpensScope, RegistersMacro, OpaqueParserEffect) — an opaque effect is legal for fidelity but forces the conservative suffix barrier and disables distributed parsing past that point (precision is earned by evidence, never assumed; the system starts conservative and ratchets, §10.6).

Why load-bearing: this is what makes sub-command incremental reparse (Lantern), the Ledger notation/syntax invalidation-matrix row, and distributed parsing sound rather than hopeful. Acceptance: epoch hashing deterministic and stable across hosts; memoization hit/miss behavior verified against the invalidation matrix fixtures (a notation change invalidates exactly the affected epoch descendants after the activation point); opaque-effect barrier demonstrated. Tests: property tests — memoized parse == fresh parse under any epoch-respecting edit sequence; C8 incremental edit-sequence corpus with expected invalidation cones.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. GrammarEpoch is deterministic versioned data that content-hashes all active syntax declarations, parser descriptors, precedence tables, scoped notation, token parsers, and relevant options; every parse product records the epoch under which it was recognized. Cross-host/profile fixtures yield the same epoch for the same canonical grammar state.
2. Memoization keys include position, category, precedence, and GrammarEpoch. Syntax changes invalidate exactly products that read changed components after activation, matching the Ledger invalidation matrix; stale or foreign-epoch products cannot publish.
3. Registrations emit typed summaries for AddsSyntax, AddsToken, ChangesPrecedence, OpensScope, RegistersMacro, or OpaqueParserEffect. Opaque effects are fidelity-legal but impose a conservative suffix barrier and disable distributed parsing beyond it until stronger evidence exists.
4. Named unit/property/metamorphic/mutation suites cover canonical epoch hashing, each effect summary and dependency edge, notation/precedence/scope/token invalidation, activation boundaries, cache hit/miss facts, opaque barriers, malformed registrations, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and memoized-parse equals fresh-parse over generated epoch-respecting edit sequences. Over-invalidation, under-invalidation, stale-hit, and omitted-barrier mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane applies the C8 incremental edit corpus to a real parser session, demonstrates exact invalidation cones and cache behavior for a syntax change, demonstrates the opaque suffix barrier, compares incremental with cold full parses, and verifies clean recovery after a rejected registration. Human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON record run/session/edit/epoch/component ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, canonical grammar/input/product hashes, cache and invalidation decisions with reasons, expected/actual outputs, thread/platform/seed/budgets/timings/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives cross-host determinism, edit fixtures, invalidation witnesses, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests; close evidence names every suite and may not claim distributed-parse precision beyond the measured typed-effect coverage.

Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state.
