title: G0-9: Instrumented-oracle spike — golden decision traces schema, volume, replay rig
description:
G0-9 Instrumented-oracle spike (§22.1-9, feeds §18.3): patch the Reference IN THE TRIBUNAL SANDBOX (build-time-only, test-only, never shipped, never linked — D8) to dump decision traces at the points our native machinery must match — unifier approximation choices, postponements, unfoldings and their order, instance-search candidate orders and outcomes, macro expansions, simp rewrite firings, heartbeat consumption — on a pilot corpus, and prove the trace schema, volume, and replay-rig design.

Why this exists: under the Oracle-Only Law there is NO hosted upstream engine to fall back to, so the unifier/instance/simp fidelity burden (Critical risk R2) is carried entirely by evidence. Golden decision traces at Corpus scale are the strongest available mitigation: Athanor, Synod, and Anvil are implemented AGAINST the traces, not against vibes, and trace-replay rigs run continuously thereafter.

Acceptance: (a) the patched-oracle build recipe is scripted, hashed, and reproducible in the sandbox; (b) trace schema versioned and validated (typed events, source anchors, stable ids); (c) volume measured on the pilot corpus with a stated projection to Corpus scale (storage in the Tribunal frankensqlite store); (d) a replay rig prototype diffs a hand-written toy unifier against traces to prove the comparison machinery. Traces are fixtures, never components; the generator lives behind the D8 wall. Tests: the pilot traces + replay rig land in fln-conformance as the seed of the continuous golden-trace lanes.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: A scripted, hashed, reproducible, build-time/test-only Reference patch in the Tribunal sandbox emits versioned typed traces for unifier approximations/postponements, unfold choices/order, instance candidate order/outcomes, macro expansions, simp firings, heartbeat/fuel consumption, source anchors, stable declaration/event ids, and explicit truncation/resource facts on a reviewed pilot corpus. The patch and traces remain fixtures/generators only, never linked, shipped, or callable as a FrankenLean component; source and artifact scans prove the D8 wall. Schema validation rejects unknown/duplicate/out-of-order/impossible events and binds exact pin, patch, binary, corpus and environment roots. Volume, compression, indexing and replay costs are measured with raw pilot data and a justified Corpus-scale frankensqlite projection plus bounded retention policy. A replay prototype drives a hand-written toy unifier and detects planted choice/order/outcome divergences with exact first-divergence output, proving comparison machinery rather than mere logging. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover schema/events, stable ids, ordering, truncation, resource/cancellation/fault behavior, storage/replay, and D8 compile-out; named dropped-event, reordered-candidate, unstable-id, over-normalizer, hidden-oracle, and surviving-divergence mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E builds the patched Reference, records/replays pilot traces, plants/restores a divergence, handles interruption/corruption, and retains strict build/trace/store/process/cleanup bundles. The traces and replay lane enter permanent CI; dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and artifact scans pass. No runtime oracle component, unversioned ad-hoc text log, sampled trace without manifest, mock-only Reference, silently truncated trace, divergence normalized away, unsupported volume claim, or deferred listed event family satisfies closure.

title: W3: Crucible K2 — NbE accelerator, memoized whnf, cross-checked against K1
description:
Implement Crucible engine K2, the NbE accelerator (§8.3): normalization-by-evaluation with environment-aware closures, memoized whnf keyed on (term-hash, transparency), lazy delta with definitional-height comparison — the engine that makes decide, big Fin computations, and mathlib defeq-heavy corners fast (the PG-1/PG-2 arithmetic depends on it; the design was priced by spike G0-7).

Laws: K2 implements the SAME judgments as K1 (one KERNEL_CONTRACT); K2 verdicts are spot-audited against K1 continuously (sampled per run, exhaustively in Tribunal soaks) and any disagreement fails loudly and ships as a fixture; every fast-lane shortcut carries exactly one registered justification — a local proof argument in the kernel design note, a certificate checked by a simpler routine, or differential evidence (which licenses an optimization and never impersonates a proof). The size covenant counts K2 lines too. Cache keying uses the alpha-canonical digest lane (§7.1b) so alpha-varied duplicates share reduction work invisibly to metaprograms.

Acceptance: the G0-7 twenty-problem tail suite meets the envelope the PG-2 model requires; K1/K2 continuous cross-check wired and green on the full fixture corpus; PG-1 trajectory measured (full mathlib olean recheck time on the 32-core profile). Tests: differential K1-vs-K2 on generated term storms; memoization-correctness property tests (cached verdict == fresh verdict under transparency changes); fuel-parity fixtures shared with G0-6 stay green on both engines.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-02 ACCELERATOR BOUNDARY — K2 may accelerate the same judgment but cannot expose a second admission API or mint an accepted state through unchecked memoized data. Cache entries and NbE shortcuts remain internal evidence; the common fln-kernel authority gate validates status, epoch, environment root, declaration hash, resource outcome, and required K1 cross-check policy before any opaque checked result exists. Unknown/stale cache versions recompute and disagreement halts.
acceptance_criteria: K2 implements exactly the K1/KERNEL_CONTRACT judgments using NbE with environment-aware closures, alpha-canonical term digests, memoized whnf keyed by term/environment/transparency and schema, and lazy delta ordered by definitional height. Every optimization has one registered local proof, simpler checked certificate, or clearly scoped differential justification; none can mint acceptance outside the common kernel authority. Cache entries are internal evidence, versioned and root-bound; stale/unknown/mismatched entries recompute. Configured deterministic content-seeded K1 cross-checking runs continuously and exhaustive fixture/soak cross-checking finds zero disagreement; any mismatch halts, retains both traces and shrinks a fixture. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation suites cover cached-versus-fresh equivalence under transparency/environment/epoch changes, binders, alpha variants, universes, fuel, budgets, cancellation, eviction, concurrency, and authority; named stale-key, unsound-sharing, wrong-delta, skipped-cross-check, forged-cache-success, and fuel-drift mutants are killed. The twenty G0-7 tail fixtures meet the ratified PG-2 envelope with identical judgments and raw tail evidence, and fuel-parity fixtures agree across engines. Real no-mock K1/K2 E2E runs corpus and generated term storms, plants cache/cross-check divergences, restores, and retains strict key/root/verdict/trace/resource bundles. Dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, line covenant, differential/mutation/fault/thread/performance lanes pass. No second admission API, unchecked memoized verdict, runtime-random sampling, speed-for-semantics trade, timing-only validation, hidden stale reuse, unresolved disagreement, or deferred listed K2 behavior satisfies closure.

title: W3: fln-checker — structurally independent checker, WASM-clean, consensus seat
description:
Implement fln-checker, the independent kernel checker (§8.3b): a deliberately DIFFERENT second implementation, not a feature flag over fln-kernel — separate normalization algorithm (eager where K1/K2 are lazy), simpler persistent maps and substitutions, its OWN decoder over Grimoire canonical wire objects (never the primary heap nodes), no dependency on the unsafe membrane, an independently derived error-class mapping, a separate build artifact, and a WASM build for a foreign execution substrate (the WASM Judge second engine).

Non-sharing is proven STRUCTURALLY: CI verifies fln-checker shares data schemas but no semantic implementation modules with fln-kernel (dependency-graph rules). The checker joins the consensus policies of §8.3c: standard (deterministic content-seeded sample + all high-risk forms — never runtime randomness), release (full closure), paranoid (everywhere, optionally distinct hosts). The honest economics: roughly 2x kernel CPU where it runs, single-digit percent end-to-end (PG-1b measures it). Common-mode risk R6 (shared Judgment Specification misreading) is why FOREIGN witnesses (lean4checker, lean4lean via export) exist in the council; this bead includes wiring the external-witness sampling seam.

Acceptance: full agreement with K1/K2 on the corpus; a seeded spec-divergence mutant (deliberately misimplement one rule in a test build) is caught by the differential lane; WASM build passes the same vectors bit-for-bit. Tests: non-sharing CI check; consensus-policy integration tests where a planted disagreement HALTS publication with both traces attached (never outvoted).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-02 CONSENSUS BOUNDARY — fln-checker is an independent veto/evidence seat, never an alternative admission authority. Its verdict and WASM/foreign witness results are serializable evidence but cannot construct the fln-kernel opaque checked state. Tests attempt receipt replay, forged checker success, stale-root success, unknown policy/version, and all-negative/one-positive vote combinations; none may publish when fln-kernel rejects or is inconclusive.
acceptance_criteria: fln-checker is a separate artifact and genuinely independent implementation: its own canonical-wire decoder, eager normalization strategy, maps/substitutions, error mapping and code modules; it shares only reviewed data schemas with fln-kernel, has no unsafe-membrane dependency, and builds/runs WASM-clean. Structural CI derives workspace dependencies/source ownership and rejects semantic implementation sharing. It agrees declaration-by-declaration with K1/K2 across the complete required corpus and identical native/WASM vectors. Standard policy uses deterministic content-seeded samples plus all high-risk forms, release checks the full closure, paranoid checks everywhere and may use distinct hosts; runtime randomness is forbidden. Checker/foreign-witness verdicts are veto/evidence seats only and cannot construct the kernel opaque accepted capability; any disagreement halts with traces, never votes through publication. Unit/property/model/differential/mutation tests cover each rule, decoder discrepancy, policy/sample determinism, stale roots, unknown versions, malformed wire objects, resource/cancellation outcomes, native/WASM identity, receipt replay, forged success, and all vote combinations; a seeded spec-rule divergence is detected. Real no-mock consensus E2E runs K1/K2/checker plus foreign witnesses, plants one-engine disagreements, restores, and retains strict policy/sample/root/verdict/trace/publication bundles. PG-1b overhead is measured honestly with raw samples and never used as correctness evidence. Dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, nonsharing/unsafe/dependency guards, WASM/differential/mutation/platform lanes pass. No feature flag over kernel, shared semantic module, checker-as-authority, quorum override, random sampling, mock-only WASM/foreign witness, unresolved disagreement, or deferred listed behavior satisfies closure.

title: W5: Golem runtime integration — generated intrinsics, effects, plugins, G2 parity
description:
Integrate the production Golem execution stack after its independently testable components exist: validated FIR-to-FLBC output, the FLBC interpreter host-call ABI, the census-generated intrinsic table, Marrow allocation/RC, asupersync IO/Task effects, initializer ordering and capability receipts, and the inbound Reference-plugin loader. This bead owns the no-mock end-to-end proof of the §11 claim; component beads may prove their own stable interfaces without pretending that future components already exist.

Required behavior: compile and execute real user-class Lean code (mathlib-style tactics, project macros, lakefile-shaped configuration, #eval and IO/Task bodies) on ABI-valued registers; cross interpreted code <-> native intrinsics <-> Reference-built plugin calls with zero representation conversion; preserve initialization/extension ordering; count heartbeats through the same allocator hook; enforce typed budgets and cancellation; retain RC balance and ownership-shadow evidence; record capability and plugin provenance. No test stub, prototype intrinsic, or oracle artifact may satisfy production acceptance.

Acceptance: the G0-3 corpus is promoted to the complete production stack; #eval/IO/Task and plugin results match the Reference at each registered comparison class; PG-7 interpreter measurements are emitted against the W1 baseline; the C4 inbound matrix is green at honest per-platform L-levels; initialization-DAG and capability receipts are complete; two runs at each certified thread count produce identical logical results and evidence identities where required.

Verification: unit contract tests at every component seam plus shared no-mock E2E scripts for pure computation, IO/Task ordering, capability denial, cancellation, panic/partial guards, plugin load/run/crash, and RC/heartbeat accounting. Every scenario emits the mandatory human and NDJSON evidence with the first cross-boundary divergence and final ownership/obligation state.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: Complete only when every Required behavior and Acceptance item in the description is demonstrated, the mandatory verification note is satisfied, and the close evidence names the exact unit/property/mutation suites plus shared no-mock E2E scenario IDs and archived human/NDJSON artifact locations. In particular: execute the G0-3 corpus on the complete production Golem stack; match each registered Reference comparison class; produce PG-7, C4, initialization-DAG, capability, determinism, RC-balance, cancellation, plugin-failure, and final-state evidence; and leave no production claim resting on a stub, mock, prototype intrinsic, hidden oracle, or unclassified divergence. This bead is the primary W5 mitigation owner for risk R12: profile real user code and metaprograms before optimization, publish distributions and tolerance thresholds, preserve fidelity under every optimization, and if PG-7 or PG-M misses, expand measured native-facade fast paths or Iron-JIT scope and downgrade the performance claim rather than changing semantics. The R12 handoff to fln-o0j and G6 binds exact corpus, host, interpreter/compiled mode, facade coverage, hot paths, raw samples, and unresolved bottlenecks.

title: W5: Initialization + capability-scoped effects — ordering, --pure-elab, receipts
description:
Implement initialization + capability-scoped effects (§11.5): initialize / builtin_initialize ordering, import-time extension replay interleaving, and real-world IO effects exactly as upstream — under asupersync capabilities, so a --pure-elab audit mode can DENY ambient filesystem/network/clock to metaprograms and report exactly which declarations demanded them.

The posture: capability-scoped metaprogramming is a sound-mode observability win and a frontier-mode sandbox, NEVER a semantic change to compliant code (faithful mode default ambient authority approximates an ordinary Lean process — drop-in means unchanged packages just run, with effects OBSERVED into provenance). Initialization DAG receipts (from the plugin door, §6.6) unify with this: cycles, duplicate initializers, and late capability requests are explainable. This bead also covers the interaction with Golem-hosted lakefile execution (Ledger, §13.3) — lakefiles are user code with declared effect surfaces.

Acceptance: initializer-ordering fixtures match Reference-observable behavior incl. interleaving with extension replay; --pure-elab on a corpus with known effectful metaprograms reports exactly the offender set; capability events land in Palimpsest with correct spans. Tests: ordering property tests over generated init DAGs; denial fixtures (a denied capability yields typed CapabilityDenied naming the alternative, never silent substitution — D2 discipline); provenance-completeness checks against the perturbation validator.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Reproduce Reference-observable initialize and builtin_initialize order, including import-time extension replay interleaving, duplicate initializers, cycle reporting, and late capability requests; initialization DAG receipts explain every ordering edge.
2. Route filesystem, network, clock, process, and other supported metaprogram effects through explicit asupersync capabilities. Faithful mode preserves ordinary ambient behavior while observing it; sound --pure-elab denies undeclared authority with typed CapabilityDenied naming the declaration, requested capability, source span, and viable alternative, never silently substituting behavior.
3. Capability and initialization events enter Palimpsest with complete declaration/span/causal provenance. Golem-hosted lakefile execution uses the same policy and declared effect surface. A known-effect corpus under --pure-elab reports exactly the offender set with no false omissions or accidental grants.
4. Named unit/property/model/mutation suites cover generated initialization DAG orders, replay interleavings, cycles/duplicates, capability allow/deny, late requests, faithful/sound/frontier separation, cancellation/resource exhaustion, receipt determinism, provenance completeness, and failure-atomic publication. Mutants that reorder initialization, omit a capability event, or silently grant/deny must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs real user metaprograms and a lakefile through Golem in ambient and pure modes, checks Reference-observable initialization order, exact offender reporting, typed denial, receipt/provenance output, and a subsequent clean authorized run. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/declaration/initializer/capability/request/receipt ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed facts, canonical source/environment roots, ordering edges, expected/actual decisions, resources/timings/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives DAG fixtures, offender manifests, receipts, provenance perturbation results, mutant outcomes, and E2E evidence. No pure-elab sandbox or compatibility claim closes from mocks or from unclassified missing events.

title: G0-4: Hygiene & syntax fidelity spike — macro-heavy corpus tree/position diff
description:
G0-4 Hygiene & syntax fidelity spike (§22.1-4, feeds §9): parse a curated set of macro-heavy Corpus files and compare Syntax trees, byte positions, and hygiene observables against the Reference (driven through its own frontend under instrumentation in the Tribunal sandbox).

Hygiene is the classic reimplementation graveyard: macro scopes appended to names, quotation/antiquotation semantics, macro_rules matching, and the parse/elaborate interleaving (a command can install a parser used three lines later) are all API surface that metaprograms and diagnostics observe. This spike visits the graveyard early. Curate files whose MEANING depends on hygiene corner cases: scope-shadowing across macro expansion, antiquotation splices, syntax categories with custom precedence, notation-priority interactions, auto-bound implicit capture.

Acceptance: (a) node kinds, atom/ident structure, SourceInfo (original vs synthetic, whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions) byte-agree on the curated set; (b) hygiene-decorated generated names agree under the stable-expansion-path derivation; (c) every divergence triaged: fidelity defect (fix) or undocumented-upstream-behavior (fixture + upstream report + Behavior Note candidate); (d) findings feed the fln-syntax / fln-parse interface freeze. Tests: the curated set becomes the standing hygiene fidelity rig in fln-conformance, run per-commit.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: A reviewed macro-heavy C0-C2 fixture manifest covers scope shadowing, quotation/antiquotation splices, macro_rules matching, custom categories and precedence, notation priority, auto-bound capture, parser installation visible to later commands, Unicode/trivia, and recovery boundaries. Quill and the instrumented Reference produce byte-equivalent node kinds, atom/ident structure, original versus synthetic SourceInfo, whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions, macro scopes, generated identifiers, expansion results, diagnostics, and acceptance under each declared comparison class. Stable expansion-path names and all outputs remain identical at 1, 8, and 32 threads. Every divergence is minimized and classified as fidelity defect or evidenced upstream behavior/Behavior-Note candidate before interface freeze. Unit/property/metamorphic/mutation tests cover capture avoidance, precedence/associativity, source spans, invalid input, interleaving, deterministic names, cancellation/resources, and recovery; named byte-versus-char, scope-drop, stale-parser, timing-name, and normalization mutants are killed. The standing real no-mock hygiene E2E compares complete trees/positions/names, plants divergence and malformed/resource cases, restores exact inputs, and retains strict trace/root/cleanup bundles. This bead is the primary G0-4 mitigation owner for risk R8: every undocumented hygiene/macro behavior found becomes a versioned fixture and trace row, and its classified result plus interface consequence is handed to Quill and the G3 gate; no unknown or sampled gap is allowed to disappear at handoff. Findings explicitly feed fln-parse/fln-syntax contracts; dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and fuzz/differential lanes pass. No simplified AST comparison, source normalization, hosted parser/macro fallback, hidden fixture exclusion, unresolved divergence, mock-only oracle, or deferred listed spike behavior satisfies closure.

title: W10: Backends & ABI doors — Iron native backend, JIT, C parity, outbound plugins
description:
Workstream W10 "Backends & ABI doors" (§17.3, §17.4, §11.3): the sovereign compilation path and the bidirectional plugin promise.

Scope: Iron static backend — FLBC to native objects/executables (x86-64, aarch64 first) with NO LLVM, no cranelift, no cc: the owned baseline emitter grown a static mode; lake build of executables with zero external toolchain (the G6 headline). Iron-JIT (frontier, standard-mode only, fln-unsafe-jit): baseline template JIT with inline caches burned in; results bit-identical to the interpreter by construction, spot-checked by a standing dual-run rig; certified builds never enable it. --backend c parity generator: emit C in the Reference idiom and drive the USER toolchain exactly as upstream leanc does (D2 optional tool #1) — parity apparatus and exotic-target escape hatch, never the anchor. Both backends dual-run governed: Iron pinned to interpreter results, interpreter pinned to Reference observable evaluation semantics on the differential corpus. ABI door outbound (§17.4, the harder direction, staged to G5): FrankenLean-built plugins loading into the Reference host, per-platform Tribunal matrix; inbound (Reference plugins into FrankenLean) lands earlier under W2/G2. Risk R5 honesty: outbound tiers per platform in the Parity Ledger rather than blocking G5.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
acceptance_criteria: W10 closes only when the reviewed target/profile and WASM packaging decisions are frozen before backend interfaces and every backend remains observationally pinned to Golem and the Reference corpus. Iron static mode lowers validated FLBC to real x86-64 and aarch64 objects/executables, including ABI calls, relocations, exceptions, stack maps, RC/FBIP behavior, debug/unwind policy, linking, and deterministic emission, with no LLVM, Cranelift, system assembler, linker, or C compiler. Real Lake projects build and run with cc absent. Iron-JIT is frontier/standard-only inside fln-unsafe-jit, dual-runs against the interpreter, revokes safely, and is structurally absent from certified/faithful artifacts. The C backend emits the Reference-compatible idiom and uses only the explicit D2 user compiler capsule; it is parity/exotic-target apparatus and excluded from reproducible sets. native_decide Tier 2 trace-guided replay admits only kernel-checked discharges. FrankenLean-built outbound plugins load into real Reference hosts across the declared C4 matrix with exact ABI/ownership/initialization behavior and honest per-platform evidence tiers. Unit/property/model/differential/mutation suites cover every instruction selection, relocation, ABI lowering, object format, optimizer/JIT guard, C emission, subprocess policy, plugin boundary, unsupported target, cancellation/resource limit, and recovery; named wrong-code, nondeterministic-layout, JIT-leak, hidden-cc, unchecked-replay, and ABI-ownership mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E builds/runs native executables without cc, dual-runs interpreter/JIT/static/C outputs, loads outbound plugins, injects malformed objects/plugin crashes/tool absence, restores exact state, and retains strict platform bundles. All W10 children, target/profile and packaging contracts, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, codegen differential/fuzz/mutation/fault/reproducibility/platform/performance lanes are green. No external codegen backend, shell-out assembler/linker, JIT in certified paths, C-backend anchor, mock-only host/plugin, unclassified platform gap, timing-only correctness claim, or deferred listed W10 feature satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: fln-kernel K1: literal-acceleration slice — KR-313 Nat ops + KR-314 String expansion (wires fln-bignum)
description: Follow-up to franken_lean-5p2. KR-313: on Nat literals the kernel computes succ/add/sub/mul/div/mod/gcd/pow(exp≤2^24)/beq/ble/land/lor/xor/shiftLeft/shiftRight (pin anchor type_checker.cpp:609 reduce_nat; divergence note: no Nat.blt at this pin — beq/ble only), gated on closed terms; requires wiring fln-bignum into fln-kernel (covenant already names fln-bignum as an allowed dependency). Also Nat.zero ⇄ lit 0 normalization so literal-vs-symbolic constructor forms are defeq (the k1_judgments comment in kr316_nat_literal_majors_convert_to_constructor_form marks exactly this boundary). KR-314: a String literal is defeq to its String.ofList constructor form both directions, and projection/recursor machinery expands literals the same way (anchor type_checker.cpp:1030) — closes the inductive_reduce_rec Str-literal early-return documented in tc.rs. Converts the remaining nat-literal replay family (5 in Init.Prelude: UInt*.ofNatLT/Char.ofNatAux) plus any decidability cases blocked on literal arithmetic. Verification: operation-table tests vs known values including the pow cap and the no-blt divergence note, mutation kills (wrong op mapping, dropped cap), replay census before/after, gates + covenant (LOC growth budgeted — bignum calls, not reimplementation).
acceptance_criteria: 1. KR-313 implements the complete pinned Nat literal operation table and exact dispatch/arity/closed-term guards, Nat.zero and constructor/literal normalization, the documented pow cap and no-Nat.blt pin behavior, and typed FL-INV-07 resource outcomes before oversized allocation. KR-314 expands String literals at every required recursor, projection, and defeq rung; KR-304/KR-308/KR-310 interactions discovered by replay remain covered. 2. Named public-authority unit/property tests cover all operations, boundary and large values, wrong operation/arity/head, zero and successor forms, offset and lazy-delta cases, string fold direction, recursor/projection behavior, and typed exhaustion. Mutation tests kill wrong mapping, missing cap, zero-normalization loss, offset errors, successor off-by-one, string reversal, over-acceptance, projection-index weakening, and missing recursor normalization. 3. The real no-mock replay accepts the complete pinned target census with zero rejected or inconclusive rows, decodes the full pinned stdlib with zero identity cross-check failures, exercises typed corruption rejection, and proves pristine recovery. 4. Replay evidence is retained as schema-versioned line-oriented NDJSON with rule/bead, fixture/hash, census totals, contract epoch, budget, expected/actual verdict and diagnostics, resource facts, mutation/corruption classification, artifact paths, cleanup, and final state. Repeated identical semantic records are deterministic apart from explicitly excluded timing/path fields. 5. fln-kernel and workspace tests, format, warnings-denied Clippy, UBS, and dependency/unsafe/LOC covenant gates are green; the fln-bignum dependency remains within the closed universe and no arithmetic authority is duplicated inside the kernel.
close_reason: Landed as ee425e8. Init.Prelude replay census: 1744/1755 -> 1755/1755 accepted, 0 rejected, 0 Inconclusive — the full pinned Prelude now replays through the kernel. All 11 decoded residuals closed, but the decode sharpened once more mid-flight: the 6 match auxiliaries + 5 ofNatLT/ofNatAux cases fell to KR-313 (reduce_nat in the whnf loop, pin :609) + the KR-308 offset machinery INSIDE lazy delta (pin :973) + the KR-304 decide shortcut (pin :1062); the LAST residual (List.get.match_1) was a different missing rule entirely — KR-310's projection half (pin :1101, same-index Proj pairs close on defeq scrutinees; the pair was PProd.0 of a List.rec stuck on an mdata-wrapped fvar, invisible to the renderer until the new first_divergence locator exposed it). KR-314 landed in all three pin sites (recursor major, proj scrutinee, defeq rung; String.ofList is a DEF at this pin — expansion is whnf'd to the real ctor). Divergence notes pinned by test: no Nat.blt; pow cap 2^24. FL-INV-07: oversized shift/pow results are typed Inconclusive BEFORE allocation (charge_bulk) — deliberate Behavior Note vs the pin (which grinds/OOMs). Verification: 14 new tests in k1_judgments (63 total, named per KR rule); 9-mutant campaign (mutation_campaign: div/mod swap, pow-cap drop, Nat.zero-operand drop, offset-zero-lit drop, succ-peel off-by-one, unreversed string fold, literal-pair over-acceptance, proj-idx-guard drop, string-rec whnf drop) — all KILLED by named tests, source byte-restored. Shared no-mock E2E scripts/e2e/kernel_replay.sh extended with a census-floor lane (accepted=1755, rejected={}, inconclusive=0 — regression trips it); full scenario green incl. 158,608-constant stdlib decode differential, corruption sweep (4/8 typed kills, 0 panics), recovery. Gates: fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test workspace (83 suites), ubs (0 critical), structure-guard (covenant 2,758/12,000 LOC; kernel->bignum edge acknowledged on main by BrightPelican f67b795). Typed limitations: reduce_native (Lean.reduceBool/Nat) remains a named follow-up slice (under-reduction only); bignum ABI-limb layout + PG-K stay on franken_lean-npl pending 53v.

title: W2: olean-next + fln olean tool — content-addressed modules, sealed capsules, diffs
description:
Implement olean-next + the fln olean tool (§7.4, frontier): the second module lane — a content-addressed, position-independent format: per-declaration records keyed by Ledger hashes, structure-shared term DAGs with dictionary compression, mergeable and diffable, mmap-friendly WITHOUT relocation, carrying certificate attachments (§8.6) inline. olean-next never replaces olean in faithful/sound artifacts (D-08); it is the Ledger native interchange and the format proof-cache federation speaks.

Capsule shapes: THIN (references into the shared CAS) and SEALED (a complete transitive object pack, verifiable on an air-gapped machine — the archival/audit form, and the .flnpack the WASM Judge consumes). Attested evidence objects may carry RaptorQ repair sidecars via the asupersync coder; repair reconstructs to staging, re-verifies the canonical hash and a full schema decode, publishes without changing identity, and journals the corruption/repair event — repair is a durability feature, NEVER a trust shortcut. The fln olean CLI tool converts both directions (olean <-> olean-next) and prints human-readable diffs of module contents — the un-opaquing of weakness census #8 (pointer-dump oleans are unmergeable, un-diffable, un-addressable).

Acceptance: round-trip olean -> olean-next -> olean preserves semantic hash and (faithful path) bytes; sealed capsules verify air-gapped (no CAS access); repair-sidecar loss and corruption drills pass with journaled events; diff output validated on known module pairs. Tests: format fuzzing under budgets; thin/sealed equivalence property tests; a tamper test (any flipped byte fails verification with a typed error naming the object); dictionary-compression determinism (same input, same bytes).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement a versioned, position-independent olean-next format with content-addressed per-declaration Ledger records, shared/dictionary-compressed term DAGs, certificate attachments, deterministic canonical encoding, mmap-friendly reads without relocation, and merge/diff operations. It remains a frontier/native interchange and never replaces faithful/sound olean artifacts.
2. THIN capsules reference validated shared-CAS objects; SEALED capsules contain the complete transitive object pack and verify on an air-gapped machine with no CAS/network access. Both forms resolve to identical semantic/logical roots and enforce version, bounds, dependency, certificate, and canonical-hash checks before publication.
3. Optional RaptorQ sidecars reconstruct only into staging; repaired bytes undergo full canonical hash and schema decode before atomic promotion with unchanged identity and a journaled corruption/repair event. Missing/insufficient/corrupt sidecars never confer trust or overwrite a valid object.
4. fln olean converts olean to olean-next and back, verifies capsules, and emits deterministic human and versioned-JSON semantic diffs. A faithful round trip preserves semantic hash and required original bytes. Named unit/property/fuzz/mutation suites cover codec bounds, DAG sharing/cycles, dictionary determinism, thin/sealed equivalence, merge/diff, every-byte tampering, repair thresholds, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and failure atomicity. Hash-bypass, trust-repair, missing-object, and partial-promotion mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane converts real pinned Reference/corpus oleans, verifies faithful byte/semantic round trips, packs thin and sealed forms, moves sealed output to a network/CAS-isolated process, checks it, compares known module diffs, corrupts/losses shards through the repair threshold, rejects over-threshold tampering, and verifies recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/module/object/capsule/shard/transaction ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/format/mode/profile/platform/cache/network facts, canonical source/object/certificate/capsule/logical roots, expected/actual verify/repair/diff, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives corpus fixtures, round-trip matrices, air-gap manifests, fuzz seeds, repair/fault matrices, mutant outcomes, and evidence bundles. No sealed, faithful-byte, repair, or trust claim closes from mocks or non-isolated verification.

4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. title: W12: Epoch ratchet — mechanical pin advances, census diffs, migration beads
description:
Implement the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (§22.5, §13.6): the mechanical 8 steps — (1) fetch + hash the official release into the epoch lab; (2) regenerate every census on every platform (ABI, olean, extern, builtin, options, CLI, LSP schemas); (3) run the existing corpus differentially and mine new behavior into fixtures + fresh instrumented-oracle traces; (4) machine-diff the public/Meta API, artifact, ABI, CLI, Lake, LSP surfaces; (5) regenerate facade stubs and OPEN MIGRATION BEADS for every moved symbol; (6) close or classify every new divergence (unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim); (7) run the unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs; (8) publish the capsule, evidence bundle, and migration report — then move the pin.

Cadence target: a new stable upstream release reaches R3 within four weeks and R4 by the following cycle, lag publicly visible on the dashboard (risk R14: the newest closed epoch stays R4 while later epochs ship R2/R3 previews — latest-parity never implied). Supported-epoch count: initial posture current + previous stable (OQ-10). Epoch bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) are the pressure-release valve keeping the treadmill from owning the roadmap.

Acceptance: the ratchet runs end-to-end against a SIMULATED epoch advance (a synthetic delta over the current pin) producing the capsule, the census diffs, the auto-opened migration beads, and the block-on-unclassified behavior; the lag dashboard renders from ledger data. Tests: census-diff correctness fixtures (planted deltas per surface must be caught and classified); bead-opening automation tests; block-on-unclassified enforcement test.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — resolve the D2 interaction explicitly. Release tooling must not spawn br. The ratchet produces a deterministic schema-versioned migration-issue manifest for every moved symbol and divergence, with exact operator/repository-harness br import instructions; applying it is an external maintenance action, not a FrankenLean runtime subprocess. This bead owns epoch acquisition, regeneration, differentials, surface diffs, facade regeneration, classification gates, downstream dual-epoch runs, capsule/report production, and the manifest. franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w consumes those outputs for project repair.

1. 2. Every generated artifact binds old/new release hashes, platform, extractor/schema versions, source inputs, and toolchain closure. Unclassified divergence, missing census/platform, stale fixture, failed downstream row, absent migration bead, or incomplete evidence blocks pin movement with no partial authoritative state. The Reference remains confined to the epoch lab/Tribunal capacities.
3. Migration bead creation is deterministic/idempotent with ownership, surface, old/new identities, dependency/gate links, acceptance tests, and evidence requirements. Supported-epoch policy starts at current plus previous stable per OQ-10; R3/R4 cadence and lag are dashboard facts, not implied latest parity. Epoch bridges remain separate, off-by-default, allowlisted evidence.
4. Named unit/property/golden/differential/mutation suites plant changes in every census/surface, verify diff classification and fixture mining, bead idempotency/dependencies, downstream dual-epoch execution, publication transaction/rollback, dashboard derivation, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and D8 confinement. Missed-delta, unclassified-pass, early-pin-move, duplicate/missing-bead, and false-lag mutants must be killed.
5. A shared E2E ratchet uses a sealed synthetic delta over the current pin to exercise every changed surface and blocking path, plus a no-mutation dry run against a real signed official release/candidate in the isolated lab; it produces capsule, census/API diffs, traces, migration beads, downstream results, dashboard data, and rollback proof without moving the live pin until all gates pass. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/epoch/surface/census/divergence/fixture/bead/gate/publication ids, redacted argv/cwd, old/new hashes/platform/extractor/schema/profile facts, canonical artifact/capsule/evidence roots, classification and expected/actual gate state, timings/resources/status, cleanup, and final state.

CI archives all censuses/diffs, corpus/trace deltas, generated facade/bead manifests, dual-epoch results, capsule/migration report, dashboard snapshot, rollback proof, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifest. No epoch claim or pin advance can proceed with an unclassified row, mocked census, or missing platform.

title: W1: Substrate & Contracts — fln-core, extraction law, Tribunal bootstrap, baselines
description:
Workstream W1 "Substrate & Contracts" (§22.2): the foundation everything else compiles against, plus the conformance apparatus bootstrapped FIRST so every later workstream is born differential.

Scope: fln-core (names, universe levels, Expr constructor inventory with cached hashes / loose-bvar ranges / has-fvar+has-mvar flags exactly as the Reference exposes them — §1.1; literals, options, byte positions); fln-hash (owned BLAKE3-class hashing, domain-separation registry, canonical serialization schemas); the mechanical contract-extraction toolchain (ABI_CONTRACT.md, OLEAN_CONTRACT.md, extern + builtin censuses per Appendices B/C — extracted from the pin by checked-in scripts, never hand-transcribed, CI re-extracts and fails on drift, Rule D5/D9); the Tribunal rig + differential harness against the Reference binaries (epoch laboratory: hashed official archives, CLI/LSP transcripts, quirk ledgers, per-platform reproduction commands — §18); the W1 baseline performance report of the Reference at the pin on both pinned bare-metal profiles (§19.1 — gate numbers are ratified against it); the dependency-closure allowlist + SUITE.lock ceremony (D1); windows functional CI; G0 coordination.

Depends on nothing; everything depends on it. Exit: contracts generated and CI-locked, Tribunal executes a Reference-vs-Reference smoke differential, baseline report checked in, closure audit green.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
acceptance_criteria: W1 closes only when its complete foundation is integrated, not merely when component crates compile. fln-core exposes the pinned Name/Level/Expr/literal/option/position semantics with stack-safe, resource-bounded canonical behavior; fln-hash owns domain-separated hashing and canonical schemas; mechanical ABI/OLEAN/ilean/extern/builtin extraction regenerates from the cryptographically bound pin into one canonical inventory with no handwritten layout values; Tribunal epoch labs, typed comparison classes, Parity Ledger, Reference smoke differential, campaign machinery, and ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out are permanent CI; SUITE.lock and the full transitive dependency allowlist are authoritative; faithful/sound/frontier modes, typed errors/diagnostics, performance harness and baseline report, Windows functional lane, convergence WIP governance, and upstream upgrade ledger all operate on the same contracts. Cold-checkout generation is byte-identical under locale/path/time/thread variation, generated Rust compiles, every policy/census row is total, and seeded pin/layout/symbol/schema/normalizer/dependency/mode/diagnostic/claim mutations fail with exact first-divergence evidence then recover cleanly. The baseline report measures the pinned Reference on both declared bare-metal profiles with raw samples, tails, host/isolation/cache facts, ratified or explicitly amended PG thresholds, and no benchmark promoted above OBSERVED. Real no-mock E2E proves contract extraction through consumption, Reference-vs-Reference Tribunal plumbing, hostile environment sealing, dependency rejection, Windows status honesty, and reproducible retained bundles. Unit/property/model/mutation/fuzz/fault tests cover every public parser, generator, validator, registry, and robot schema; scripts/check.sh, full cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS gates, contract drift jobs, and evidence validation are green. No ad-hoc runner, source-regex substitute where the Reference environment is required, handwritten constant, untyped census row, ambient compiler bypass, unclassified dependency, mock-only oracle, unsupported platform reported green, or deferred listed foundation satisfies W1.

title: W7: simp faithful core + deterministic compiled rewrite-index format
description:
Implement the simp faithful core + compiled rewrite indexes (§12.1, §12.2): the Reference simp semantics — simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree candidate retrieval, congruence closure interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors, attribute-driven extension — implemented natively end to end (driver, congruence interplay, extension dispatch are Anvil code behind obs-class Mirror facade rows), with the Corpus simp-heavy files plus instrumented-oracle rewrite traces as the fidelity corpus. Mathlib simp EXTENSIONS and simprocs are user code on Golem calling the native driver through the facade.

Compiled rewrite indexes (§12.2): per-library discrimination automata compiled at Ledger commit time and shipped as content-addressed CAS artifacts (an olean-next attachment) — mathlib tens of thousands of simp lemmas become a preloaded matcher, not a per-session index build. Staleness is impossible BY CONSTRUCTION (content-addressed against the simp-set own hash).

Acceptance: simp outcomes match the oracle on the simp-heavy fidelity corpus (rewrite firings, order where observable, result terms); index-backed retrieval produces identical candidate sets to freshly-built trees (representation-equivalence tests); index build determinism (same simp-set hash, same artifact bytes). Tests: golden rewrite-trace replay; dsimp/simp-only behavioral fixtures; congruence-interplay differentials; a stale-index mutation test (perturbed simp-set with unperturbed index must be structurally unloadable).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — this bead owns the native faithful simp semantics plus a deterministic, content-keyed rewrite-index builder/reader and proves loaded-index candidate equivalence to a fresh tree. It does not claim Ledger publication before W8 exists. Transactional CAS publication, olean-next attachment, remote hydration, retention, invalidation, and quarantine are owned by fln-publish-rewrite-indexes-gca.

1. Implement Reference-compatible simp-set discipline, discrimination retrieval, congruence interaction, dsimp, simp-only, attributes, extension dispatch, and observable rewrite ordering natively. Real mathlib simp extensions and simprocs execute as user code on Golem through the Native Mirror facade; no upstream implementation executes as a component.
2. Compile each library simp set into a deterministic versioned discrimination automaton stored as a content-addressed Ledger CAS artifact and olean-next attachment bound to the exact simp-set hash. A missing, corrupt, foreign-version, or stale index is structurally unloadable and falls back to a fresh safe index without changing candidate sets or results.
3. Golden instrumented-oracle traces and the simp-heavy corpus agree on goal outcome, result term, observable firing sequence, and facade calls. Compiled-index retrieval is representation-equivalent to a freshly built tree and same hash/input produces bit-identical bytes at threads 1, 8, and 32.

title: W9: Replay bundles + protocol rigs — deterministic server bugs, editor smoke matrix
description:
Implement session replay bundles + protocol rigs (§14.3, §18.2): every session can emit a replay bundle (init transcript, edit and request sequence, content ids or sealed sources, scheduling profile, normalized outbound transcript); fln server replay bundle reproduces the session and reports the FIRST divergence — server bugs become deterministic objects, not race anecdotes.

Protocol rigs: recorded editor sessions replayed against BOTH servers (FrankenLean and the Reference) with wire-level diffing under registered nondeterminism classes (request interleaving) normalized by versioned normalizer code; the vscode-lean4 smoke matrix in CI (open, edit, hover, goals, completion, go-to-def, widgets, waitForDiagnostics across representative projects incl. a mathlib slice); the C5 corpus grown continuously from real usage.

Acceptance: replay determinism — a bundle replays bit-identically under the lab runtime; the smoke matrix green across the editor scenarios; first-divergence reporting demonstrated on a seeded server mutant. Tests: bundle round-trip fixtures; normalizer-honesty tests (a normalizer may never discard an error body to pass — planted-divergence detection); torture under the asupersync lab: cancellation storms, fault injection, crash-recovery of session state with seed replay (§18.2 torture lane).

title: W8: Cache federation + epoch bridges — validated atp CAS, conservative cross-epoch reuse
description:
Implement cache federation + epoch-stable caching (§13.5, §13.6): the CAS syncs over atp (multi-donor, fountain-coded, resumable) or HTTPS fallback, entries signed and verified; a fln cache shim preserves the lake exe cache muscle memory and hydrates from the community existing archives during transition. Because records are keyed semantically, a cache populated by ANY machine on the epoch serves every project shape — first-mathlib-build becomes: sync frontier, elaborate your delta (PG-10: hydration >= 2x lake exe cache get on the lossy-path fixture, >= 1x clean).

Security law (R18): a remote entry is UNTRUSTED DATA until validated locally — node key and schema, content hashes and closure, epoch/platform compatibility, capability-input binding, kernel/checker receipts where relevant, signature policy, decompression bounds; a malformed entry becomes a miss plus a security event, never trusted-state poison; concurrent fill is lease-suppressed; corruption quarantines are first-class records.

Epoch-stable caching: toolchain bumps re-key the epoch, but the Ledger records WHY — a pin advance ships a machine-diffed census, and records provably untouched by the delta may carry epoch bridges (frontier, off by default, conservative allowlist, gated behind its own soundness rig — OQ-6 owns allowlist v1). The migration autopilot (audit project against the new epoch census, propose rewrites from the repair classes, lockstep builds, apply-transaction with rollback; never edits without explicit apply) is specified here and consumes the Bloodhound repair engine (W11).

Acceptance: PG-10 on the WAN fixtures; validation rejects every corpus of malformed entries; bridge soundness rig green on allowlist v1. Tests: multi-donor pull under injected loss; poisoned-entry fixtures; lease-suppression races under the lab; quarantine-and-revalidate drills.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-05 FEDERATION AND BRIDGE BOUNDARY — remote signatures and atp reconstruction prove transport/integrity only, never semantic reuse. Every hydrated object re-enters the local untrusted-candidate validation path. An epoch bridge is a versioned proof object over an allowlisted census-delta class and exact input roots; missing, unknown, ambiguous, revoked, or over-budget bridge evidence is a miss. Poison, replay, cross-tenant/capability, decompression, equivocation, lease, and bridge-unsoundness mutations must fail without contaminating trusted state.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Federate validated CAS entries through real multi-donor, fountain-coded, resumable atp transport with the specified internal HTTPS fallback and a compatible fln cache shim for existing community archives. Entries are semantically reusable across project shapes only within their validated epoch/platform/capability contract.
2. Treat every remote byte as untrusted until local validation confirms node key/schema/version, canonical content and closure hashes, epoch/platform/profile compatibility, capability-input binding, signatures, decompression/size/time bounds, and required kernel/checker receipts. Invalid data becomes a miss plus a bounded security event and quarantine record; it cannot poison trusted state. Concurrent fills are lease-suppressed and publication is atomic.
3. Epoch bridges are frontier, off by default, and allowed only for OQ-6 allowlist-v1 record classes proven untouched by the machine census delta. Each reuse carries old/new epoch identities, delta witness, bridge rule/version, local revalidation, and rollback path. Migration audit/proposal/lockstep/apply remains explicit and never edits without user apply authority.
4. Named unit/property/model/DPOR/fuzz/mutation suites cover manifest/signature/closure validation, decompression bombs, multi-donor reconstruction/resume, lease races, quarantine/revalidation, capability mismatches, epoch-delta proofs, bridge allow/deny, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and crash atomicity. Trust-before-validate, stale-bridge, lease-bypass, quarantine-reuse, and decompression-bound mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane syncs a real cache over atp across lossy/multi-donor paths and the supported fallback, hydrates a real project, races concurrent fills, supplies poisoned/corrupt entries, quarantines and revalidates, exercises an allowed and denied epoch bridge, and verifies exact clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/cache/node/donor/lease/quarantine/bridge/transaction ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted endpoints/argv/cwd, epoch/platform/profile/network/loss/seed/budget facts, canonical manifest/content/closure/receipt roots, validation/bridge decisions, expected/actual, timings/throughput/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. Reproducible PG-10 evidence on declared clean and lossy WAN fixtures shows hydration at least 2 times lake exe cache get on lossy and at least equal on clean, with distributions and corpus/machine/network facts. CI archives poison corpus, transport traces, bridge witnesses, fault matrices, mutant outcomes, benchmarks, and E2E manifests; mocks cannot close security or performance claims.

title: fln-hash canon: recursive encoder (write_body) and Expr/Level Drop overflow on deep trees
description:
Duplicate planning report for the deep canonical encoder and Drop stack-overflow lifecycle discovered after franken_lean-fnj. The full encoder plus destruction scope, evidence, and tests have been consolidated into franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy so there is one implementation owner.

## Steps to Reproduce

See the retained discovery comment on this bead and the canonical reproducer matrix in franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy.

## Acceptance Criteria

This duplicate may close only after its encoder and Drop requirements are explicitly preserved in franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy, the parent fln-hash bead depends on that canonical owner, and the close reason states that no implementation or feature was declared complete here.
close_reason: Duplicate of franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy (deep-tree destruction stack-safety), which the owner filed independently; the extra encoder write_body recursion I noted has been folded into 6gy via comment.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Companion to franken_lean-fnj (which fixed the DECODE path). Discovered during the fnj review (Claude Fable session, 2026-07-22). crates/fln-hash/src/canon.rs Canonical::write_body for Expr and Level is recursive over tree depth, and Expr/Level themselves (nested Arc<ExprNode>/Arc<Node>) recurse on Drop. A sufficiently deep tree therefore overflows the stack when re-encoded (e.g. computing a decl-content digest) or when dropped. LOWER REACHABILITY than fnj: this requires a deep tree already in memory, which now (post-fnj) can arise from decoding a valid deep olean — real mathlib terms are nowhere near deep enough to trigger it, but a crafted valid deep artifact could. Fix: make write_body iterative (explicit work-stack, mirroring the fnj decoder) and add manual iterative Drop impls for Expr and Level that disassemble the Arc chain without recursion. Regression: encode + drop a ~1e5-deep valid term on a 1 MiB-stack thread without abort. Not urgent; filed so the fnj decode fix isn't mistaken for closing the whole depth-safety story.

title: W7: Verdict — owned CDCL SAT with owned proof logging and checker
description:
Implement Verdict (fln-verdict, §12.5): an owned CDCL SAT solver — watched literals, VSIDS/CHB-class heuristics, clause management, inprocessing kept modest and honest — with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker: the bv_decide pipeline bit-blasts through Verdict, Verdict emits a checkable unsatisfiability certificate, and a small independent checker (kernel-adjacent in audit rigor, separate in code) validates it before the reflected proof is admitted. The external-binary TCB (CaDiCaL + LRAT toolchain, weakness census #7) dies here; Behavior Note BN-06 records the change (same theorems, owned certificate chain, no external solver).

Parity posture (risk R17 honesty): correctness is absolute from day one; competitiveness is a ratchet — PG-9 tracks Verdict against the Reference solver on the bv_decide corpus with a published gap budget (100% agreement; wall <= 2x Reference-with-CaDiCaL at G5, ratcheting thereafter). If the gap persists, it PUBLISHES honestly; semantics never depended on solver speed.

Acceptance: 100% verdict agreement on the bv_decide corpus; every unsat certificate validated by the independent checker before admission; PG-9 wall within budget at G5. Tests: SAT/UNSAT differential vs known-good results on standard benchmarks + the bv_decide corpus; certificate-checker independence verified structurally (no shared solver modules); fuzzed CNF + fuzzed bit-blasting inputs under budgets; a planted bogus-certificate must be rejected.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement the owned Verdict CDCL solver with watched literals, deterministic VSIDS/CHB-class decisions, clause learning/management, bounded honest inprocessing, versioned proof logging, and a separately implemented certificate checker. No external SAT solver or proof tool executes in any release path.
2. Every UNSAT result used by bv_decide carries a complete certificate accepted by the independent checker before a kernel-replayed reflected proof may enter the environment. SAT assignments are validated against the original CNF. Unknown proof versions, malformed or over-budget proofs, checker disagreement, cancellation, and exhaustion are typed failures/Inconclusive and never admission.
3. Structural evidence proves the checker shares no solver modules and is simpler by KLOC/dependency closure. BN-06 records the owned-chain compatibility change while the Parity Ledger shows 100 percent SAT/UNSAT and goal-closure agreement on the bv_decide corpus.
4. Named unit/property/fuzz/differential/mutation suites cover propagation, conflicts, analysis, backjumping, restarts, deletion, assignment validation, proof encode/decode/check, deterministic seeded search, adversarial CNF/bit-blasting inputs, budgets, and failure atomicity. Bogus-certificate, skipped-clause, invalid-model, and admit-before-check mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs real bv_decide goals through bit-blasting, Verdict, the independent checker, and kernel replay; covers SAT, UNSAT, planted bogus proof rejection, budget exhaustion, and a subsequent clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/goal/CNF/clause/conflict/certificate ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/cwd, build/profile/thread/seed/budget/platform facts, canonical goal/CNF/proof/verdict hashes, expected/actual, checker/kernel decisions, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. Reproducible PG-9 evidence on the declared corpus/machine reports agreement and distributions and meets wall time no worse than 2 times the pinned Reference-with-CaDiCaL at G5, with any miss published rather than hidden. CI archives proofs, manifests, mutant outcomes, benchmarks, and all named tests/E2E artifacts.
